jobs.title: Blackstone Multi-Asset Investing (BXMA) – Portfolio Strategy Group, Vice President
jobs.description:
Blackstone is the world’s largest alternative asset manager. We seek to create positive economic impact and long-term value for our investors, the companies we invest in, and the communities in which we work. We do this by using extraordinary people and flexible capital to help companies solve problems. Our $1.1 trillion in assets under management include investment vehicles focused on private equity, real estate, public debt and equity, infrastructure, life sciences, growth equity, opportunistic, non-investment grade credit, real assets and secondary funds, all on a global basis. Further information is available at www.blackstone.com. Follow @blackstone on , X, and .

Business Unit Overview:

Blackstone Multi-Asset Investing (BXMA) manages $93 billion across a diversified set of businesses. We strive to generate attractive risk-adjusted returns for our clients across market cycles. Our strategies include Absolute Return, Multi-Strategy, Total Portfolio Management, and Public Real Assets.

Job Description:

The Portfolio Specialist will sit within the Portfolio Strategy Group (PSG) and serve as the primary point of contact for a group of clients with customized absolute return portfolios.

PSG is an integral part of the Absolute Return investment team and works closely with all BXMA’s strategy-focused hedge fund research teams and the Portfolio Management team.

The role primarily will focus on conducting recurring client meetings to discuss portfolio performance and attribution, opportunity set, and client objectives.  The Portfolio Specialist will liaise with BXMA’s Institutional Client Solutions team, and a variety of other teams within BXMA, to ensure that:

  • Portfolios are positioned to accomplish the long-term objectives of our clients.

  • Performance outcomes are well understood by our clients in the context of broader market and industry developments.

  • Clients view BXMA as a long-term partner that can help enhance their organizations beyond simply managing their capital.        

Qualifications:

Blackstone seeks to hire individuals who are highly motivated, intelligent, have sound judgment, and have demonstrated excellence in prior endeavors.  In addition to strong analytical and time management skills, the successful candidate must meet the following qualifications:

  • 7+ years of hedge fund industry experience, preferably as an allocator or direct investor

  • Excellent interpersonal and communication skills, both written and verbal

  • Advanced Excel skills and effectively works with large amounts of performance and risk data

  • A desire to work in a dynamic environment

  • Manages ad hoc projects independently and to completion

  • A passion for global financial markets and hedge funds, and an understanding of different strategies that hedge funds employ


The duties and responsibilities described here are not exhaustive and additional assignments, duties, or responsibilities may be required of this position.  Assignments, duties, and responsibilities may be changed at any time, with or without notice, by Blackstone in its sole discretion.

Expected annual base salary range:

$185,000 - $200,000

Actual base salary within that range will be determined by several components including but not limited to the individual's experience, skills, qualifications and job location. For roles located outside of the US, please disregard the posted salary bands as these roles will follow a separate compensation process based on local market comparables.

Additional compensation and benefits offered in connection with the role consist of comprehensive health benefits, including but not limited to medical, dental, vision, and FSA benefits; paid time off; life insurance; 401(k) plan; and discretionary bonuses. Certain employees may also be eligible for equity and other incentive compensation at Blackstone’s sole discretion.

Follow @blackstone on , X, and .
 

Blackstone is the world’s largest alternative asset manager. Blackstone seeks to deliver compelling returns for institutional and individual investors by strengthening the companies in which the firm invests. Blackstone’s $1.3 trillion in assets under management include global investment strategies focused on real estate, private equity, credit, infrastructure, life sciences, growth equity, secondaries and hedge funds. Further information is available at www.blackstone.com. Follow @blackstone on , , and . 

Blackstone Private Wealth  

Blackstone Private Wealth was established to answer the growing demand for Blackstone products from high-net worth investors. Partnering with many of the world’s largest private banks and wealth management firms as well as family offices, Blackstone’s Private Wealth team packages and delivers the full breadth of Blackstone’s alternative product capability to these firms and their clients and provides ongoing product and advisor support, as well as education and training around alternatives. 

Job Description:

  • Establish and grow relationships with distribution partner home offices, business leads, research teams, fund origination and operations professionals, and provide on-going sales and support to build Blackstone brand awareness, thought leadership and deliver sophisticated alternative product solutions to our key distribution partners in Japan

  • Collaborate closely with internal Blackstone colleagues across the firm, including within and outside of Private Wealth and the investment business units to build pipelines, generate home office interest, onboard funds, and partner on fundraising

Responsibilities:

  • Develop and strengthen relationships across Blackstone’s distribution partnerships

  • Support our distribution partners in improving their profitability by encouraging the integration of our products and services into their business strategies.

  • Provide on-going service, support and product education to relationships

  • Seek new relationships that broaden distribution, increase sales and enhance profitability

  • Work effectively in a highly collaborative team environment

Qualifications:

Blackstone seeks to hire individuals who work well in a team-driven working group, are highly motivated, intelligent, have sound judgment and have demonstrated excellence in prior endeavors. 

The successful candidate must have:

  • Bachelor’s degree

  • 7-10+ years’ experience in asset management industry

  • Direct experience as a key account manager with major brokers or mega banks in Japan

  • Demonstrate passion for alternative investments and wealth market

  • Exceptional verbal and written communication skills in both Japanese and English

  • Good presentation and public speaking skills

  • Must be a self-starter and independent


The duties and responsibilities described here are not exhaustive and additional assignments, duties, or responsibilities may be required of this position.  Assignments, duties, and responsibilities may be changed at any time, with or without notice, by Blackstone in its sole discretion.

Blackstone is seeking a highly motivated professional to support our Private Wealth Business Development team in expanding our financial planning, analytics, and reporting capabilities. This role will have a variety of responsibilities and will work in close coordination across PW & Blackstone teams to:

  • Generate actionable business insights through in-depth analysis of key business initiatives
  • Prepare and deliver presentations to senior management, summarizing findings, recommendations, and potential impacts
  • Produce routine financial reporting to track, plan and analyze Private Wealth group expenses
  • Assist in global strategic planning, annual budget and business management routines
  • Model financial impact of business initiatives and new product structures
  • Create measurement frameworks for global initiatives with relevant KPIs
  • Drive best practices around data management, including proactive efforts to ensure data integrity and quality control
  • Support management of datasets and tools to enable efficient, effective and accurate reporting
  • Consolidate, organize, and manage information from numerous internal and external sources

Qualifications:

Blackstone seeks to hire individuals who work well in a team-driven working group, are highly motivated, intelligent, have sound judgment, and have demonstrated excellence in prior endeavors.

The successful candidate must have:

  • 3+ years of experience in financial services, wealth management, asset management or other related business experience – prior Financial Planning & Analysis experience preferred
  • Excellent interpersonal and communication skills, both written and verbal
  • Expert in Excel and PowerPoint
  • Knowledge and proficiency across Tableau, Salesforce, Sigma
  • Strong academic record
  • Strong analytical, quantitative, and problem-solving skills
  • Highly motivated and driven; works effectively in a team environment
  • Strong work ethic, attention to detail and multi-tasking skills
  • Entrepreneurial, operates independently once adequately trained

jobs.title: Real Estate Finance – Assistant Vice President, Singapore
jobs.description:
Blackstone is the world’s largest alternative asset manager. We seek to create positive economic impact and long-term value for our investors, the companies we invest in, and the communities in which we work. We do this by using extraordinary people and flexible capital to help companies solve problems. Our $1.1 trillion in assets under management include investment vehicles focused on private equity, real estate, public debt and equity, infrastructure, life sciences, growth equity, opportunistic, non-investment grade credit, real assets and secondary funds, all on a global basis. Further information is available at www.blackstone.com. Follow @blackstone on , X, and .
 

Blackstone Real Estate

Blackstone is a global leader in real estate investing. Blackstone’s real estate business was founded in 1991 and has US $319 billion of investor capital under management. Blackstone is the largest owner of commercial real estate globally, owning and operating assets across every major geography and sector, including logistics, data centers, residential, office andhospitality. Our opportunistic funds seek to acquire undermanaged, well-located assets across the world. Blackstone’s Core+ business invests in substantially stabilized real estate assets globally, through both institutional strategies and strategies tailored for income-focused individual investors including Blackstone Real Estate Income Trust, Inc. (BREIT). Blackstone Real Estate also operates one of the leading global real estate debt businesses, providing comprehensive financing solutions across the capital structure and risk spectrum, including management of Blackstone Mortgage Trust (NYSE: BXMT). 

Job Title:

Real Estate Finance – Assistant Vice President, Singapore

Key Responsibilities:

- Responsibilities include but not limited to:

  • Manage fund level liquidity projection, fund capital reserves and related reporting to senior management

  • Manage and coordinate with NY funds team on all fund level capital activities and related reporting

  • Provide analytical insights and perform ongoing/ad-hoc analysis on various fund related metrics to senior management and/or investors

  • Coordination and managing the quarterly valuation process

  • Ongoing investment limitation monitoring for deal flows

  • Ongoing fund level regulatory compliance monitoring

  • Liaise with accounting service provider and BX stakeholders on fund related matters

  • Consolidate property-level information from various source systems and/or portfolio companies and provide critical analysis

  • Liaise with portfolio companies and BX stakeholders to prepare quarterly investor templates

  • Participate in technology and process improvement initiatives

  • Other ad hoc tasks/projects

  • Able to build and maintain relationships and navigate diverse stakeholders 

  • Works closely with other offices in New York, Hong Kong, and other Asia offices

Qualifications:

Blackstone seeks to hire individuals who work well in a team-driven working group, are highly motivated, intelligent, have sound judgment and have demonstrated excellence in prior endeavors. 

Required Skills:

  • Candidate must be a B.A., B.Com./ Graduate, Post Graduate or C.A., with knowledge of accounting and finance

  • Fluent in English

  • 10+ years of accounting/financial analysis experience

  • Experience in a Big 4 firm will be an advantage

  • Knowledge of real estate, including market fundamentals will be an advantage

  • Strong Microsoft Office skills (MS Excel, MS PowerPoint and MS Word)

  • Strong attention to detail and ability to work both independently and in a team environment.

  • Ability to multi-task and manage shifting priorities in a fast-paced environment

  • Self-motivated with a passion for making business processes more efficient and successful

  • Excellent communication, problem solving and interpersonal skills necessary

  • Drive and desire to learn new technologies and grow technical and functional skill sets

Business Unit:

Private Wealth, Sydney

Business Description:

Private Wealth was established to answer the growing demand for Blackstone products from high-net worth investors. Partnering with many of the world’s largest private banks and wealth management firms, Blackstone’s Private Wealth team packages and delivers the full breadth of Blackstone’s alternative product capability to these firms and their clients and provides ongoing product and advisor support, as well as education and training around alternatives.  

Job Title:

Private Wealth – Sales, Vice President

Job Description:

In this role, the Vice President will be a senior representatives of the Private Wealth sales coverage team in Australia. The role will be 100% intermediary focused, Sydney-based with a predominant client focus on southern state coverage i.e. Melbourne, Adelaide and Perth.  Key clients and counterparts include major private banks and wealth management groups, as well as select investment consultants.

Being part of Private Wealth APAC, the role will be supported by, and will have significant interface with, members of the firm’s global Private Wealth group across all Blackstone investment funds, legal/compliance department, finance and other groups within the firm. In addition to the primary objective of driving sales volume for product launches, as well as maintaining close relationships with distributors. The role will also support and spearhead multiple projects at one time such as conducting market research, project management for product launches, client/investor due diligence process, marketing material requests, roadshow coordination and the fund closing process.

This is an excellent opportunity for someone who is keen to develop a career at one of the world-class Alternative Investment firms. In addition to gaining substantial product knowledge and driving sales for the firm, the role will also develop familiarity across other important aspects including legal documentations, product operations, marketing and more.

Qualifications:

Blackstone seeks to hire individuals who work well in a team-driven working group, are highly motivated, intelligent, have sound judgment and have demonstrated excellence in prior endeavors. 

The successful candidate must have:

  • Undergraduate degree in Finance, Commerce or similar faculty.

  • 10+ years’ experience relevant work experience – including strong knowledge and connections with the domestic wealth management distribution market.

  • RG146 designation.

  • Appropriate license requirement for Australia.

  • Experience selling or marketing diverse set of investment products.

  • Can-do attitude, a desire to work in a team environment with team members across different time zones.

  • Excellent presentation and public speaking skills required.

jobs.title: Portfolio Management – Life Sciences Valuations & Analytics, AVP
jobs.description:
Blackstone is the world’s largest alternative asset manager. We seek to create positive economic impact and long-term value for our investors, the companies we invest in, and the communities in which we work. We do this by using extraordinary people and flexible capital to help companies solve problems. Our $1.1 trillion in assets under management include investment vehicles focused on private equity, real estate, public debt and equity, infrastructure, life sciences, growth equity, opportunistic, non-investment grade credit, real assets and secondary funds, all on a global basis. Further information is available at www.blackstone.com. Follow @blackstone on , X, and .

Blackstone’s Portfolio Management - Portfolio Valuations and Analytics team is looking for an AVP to support the Life Sciences (“BXLS”) group. The core focus of the group is to prepare, evaluate, analyze, and summarize portfolio investment valuation results for BXLS. In addition to valuations, the team is called upon to handle a variety of ad hoc and recurring analyses related to portfolio monitoring and reporting.

  • Assist in day-to-day management of the team including, but not limited to, deliverable tracking, model development, valuation model reviews, presentation to senior leaders, and completion of ad-hoc deliverables
  • Lead the completion of valuation models in line with Blackstone’s valuation policy as well as requisite external guidance
  • Drive the onboarding process for new portfolio investments and ensure proper data collection procedures are in place
  • Manage the annual audit as well as interim reviews with third party valuation firms
  • Drive enhancements and improvements to the group’s valuations and reporting processes, procedures, offshore resources, and use of technology
  • Manage the creation and development of presentation materials to effectively communicate results within BXLS and wider Blackstone
  • Assist in the presentation of valuation results to various internal committees, external auditors, and limited partners
  • Assist in the on-going training and development of analysts and associates
  • Work closely with BXLS Management Reporting to provide inputs as well as other portfolio investment information to aid in the completion of planning deliverables
  • Maintain relationships with core constituencies including, deal professionals, Institutional Client Services, Portfolio Operations, Accounting, Operations and Finance teams
  • This is designed to be a role involving mentorship with trajectory toward greater managerial responsibilities based on performance

Qualifications:

  • Bachelor’s Degree in Finance, Economics, Accounting, or similar discipline (CFA and/or CPA a plus)
  • 6+ years of experience in valuations, transaction services, a private equity fund, consulting, or other relevant analytical roles
  • Proven experience working in a dynamic environment that requires execution of recurring deliverables as well as projects and initiatives in support of a rapidly growing business
  • Thorough understanding of ASC 820 and corresponding valuation guidance. Familiarity with a wide range of valuation approaches (e.g. Income, Market, Yield Analysis, OPM, Contingent Claims, PWERM etc.)
  • Strong interpersonal skills, high EQ, and proven capability to confidently and competently handle interactions with deal professionals, Asset Management, and portfolio company representatives
  • A self-motivated individual with strong work ethic and attention to detail
  • Excellent oral and written communication skills. Confidence presenting to large groups of senior leaders (e.g. Firmwide Valuation Committee, Board of Directors)
  • Proficiency in MS Excel and MS PowerPoint is required
  • Proficiency in iLevel a plus
  • Experience managing a team of at least 3 individuals a plus


The duties and responsibilities described here are not exhaustive and additional assignments, duties, or responsibilities may be required of this position.  Assignments, duties, and responsibilities may be changed at any time, with or without notice, by Blackstone in its sole discretion.

Expected annual base salary range:

$130,000 - $200,000

jobs.title: Blackstone Credit & Insurance (BXCI), Investment Analyst/Associate
jobs.description:
Blackstone is the world’s largest alternative asset manager. We seek to create positive economic impact and long-term value for our investors, the companies we invest in, and the communities in which we work. We do this by using extraordinary people and flexible capital to help companies solve problems. Our $1.1 trillion in assets under management include investment vehicles focused on private equity, real estate, public debt and equity, infrastructure, life sciences, growth equity, opportunistic, non-investment grade credit, real assets and secondary funds, all on a global basis. Further information is available at www.blackstone.com. Follow @blackstone on , X, and .
 

Business Unit:

Blackstone Credit and Insurance Group (BXCI), Japan

Business Description:

Blackstone Credit & Insurance (“BXCI”) is one of the world’s leading credit investors. Our investments span the credit markets, including private investment grade, asset based lending, public investment grade and high yield, sustainable resources, infrastructure debt, collateralized loan obligations, direct lending and opportunistic credit. We seek to generate attractive risk-adjusted returns for institutional and individual investors by offering companies capital needed to strengthen and grow their businesses. BXCI is also a leading provider of investment management services for insurers, helping those companies better deliver for policyholders through our world-class capabilities in investment grade private credit.

Job Title: Investment Analyst/ Associate

Job Description:

The position will offer exposure across our 1) private credit strategies, which include distressed, special situations and performing credit investing, 2) sustainable resource investing, which includes renewable energy, infrastructure and investment grade debt, and 3) asset backed financing, which includes private and public securitised structures. The role will include both private equity style and credit focused due diligence and analysis.

Analysts/Associates are generalists and participate in all aspects of projects and can expect to work on a number of assignments at one time. Due to the relatively small size of the professional staff, deal teams are typically small. Analysts/Associates are therefore expected to assume important and integral roles in the screening, analysing and structuring of investment opportunities, as well as regularly attend client and internal meetings, negotiations, and due diligence sessions.

Responsibilities:

Typically, work will include private-equity style due diligence and financial analysis, modelling, research, competitive analysis and benchmarking, development of presentations, assistance in the execution of transactions, and drafting of investment memoranda. As well as being involved in specific investment processes, candidates will help lead individual research projects, focused on either specific industries or companies.

Qualifications:

Blackstone seeks to hire individuals who work well in a team-driven working group, are highly motivated, intelligent, have sound judgment and have demonstrated excellence in prior endeavors. 

The successful candidate must have:

  • Strong academic track record
  • Strong analytical and quantitative skills
  • Strong English & Japanese communication skills
  • Prior buy side, investment banking, consulting, leveraged finance or securitisation experience is preferred
  • Strong financial modeling capabilities including the ability to build LBO model
  • Strong work ethic and intellectual curiosity, with the ability to operate in a lean team environment


The duties and responsibilities described here are not exhaustive and additional assignments, duties, or responsibilities may be required of this position. Assignments, duties, and responsibilities may be changed at any time, with or without notice, by Blackstone in its sole discretion.

The role focuses on financial analysis, performance-related data, and investor reporting and supports the marketing and content needs for prospective and current investors in the funds within the Private Equity business.

jobs.description:
Blackstone is the world’s largest alternative asset manager. We seek to create positive economic impact and long-term value for our investors, the companies we invest in, and the communities in which we work. Follow @blackstone on , X, and .

Job Description: 

The Portfolio Management - Valuations & Analytics team is looking for an Associate for the Hedge Fund Solutions (BXMA) group. The core focus of the group is to prepare, evaluate, analyze, and summarize portfolio investment valuation results for BXMA with a focus on private asset valuation including equity, credit, structured products, and structured risk transfers.

Responsibilities:

  • Prepare valuation models reviews, presentations for leaders, and ad-hoc deliverables
  • Apply valuation methodologies in line with Blackstone’s valuation policy as well as requisite external guidance
  • Attend valuation committee meetings and other meetings with senior-level stakeholders and present to leaders in Finance and BXMA
  • Maintain subject matter expertise in structured risk transfers, structured securities, credit, and complex valuations
  • Drive the onboarding process for technology and offshore team members and ensure proper data collection procedures are in place
  • Respond to auditor inquiries and coordinate valuation reviews with third party valuation firms
  • Assist in the presentation of valuation results to various internal committees, external auditors, and limited partners
  • Work closely with BXMA Management Reporting to provide inputs as well as other portfolio investment information to aid in the completion of business forecasts and other planning deliverables
  • Maintain productive relationships with core constituencies including, Asset Management, Investor Relations, Operations, Accounting, and Finance teams and deal professionals

Qualifications:

  • Bachelor’s Degree in Finance, Economics, Accounting, or similar discipline (CFA and/or CPA a plus)
  • 3+ years of private valuation experience in transaction services, a private equity fund, hedge fund, third-party valuation services provider, or similar organization
  • Understanding of ASC 820 and corresponding valuation guidance.
  • In depth understanding of the discount cash flow model, market multiples, and option pricing
  • Strong interpersonal skills, high EQ, and confidently and competently handles interactions with deal professionals, Asset Management, and portfolio company representatives
  • Excellent verbal and written communication skills.
  • Confidence presenting to large groups of individuals
  • Works well in a dynamic environment that requires execution of recurring deliverables as well as projects and initiatives in support of a growing business
  • Focused on process improvement
  • Proficiency in MS Excel and MS PowerPoint is required
  • Proficiency in Python a plus
  • Experience with Monte Carlo simulation a plus

jobs.title: Associate - Strategic Advisory (Generalist M&A)
jobs.description:
PJT Partners is a global advisory-focused investment bank. Our team of senior professionals delivers a wide array of strategic advisory, shareholder advisory, restructuring and special situations and private fund advisory and placement services to corporations, financial sponsors, institutional investors and governments around the world. We offer a unique portfolio of advisory services designed to help our clients achieve their strategic objectives. We also provide, through PJT Park Hill, private fund advisory and fundraising services for alternative investment managers, including private equity funds, real estate funds and hedge funds.

From the beginning, PJT Partners has firmly believed that having the best people is key to building an enduring franchise. Our perspective was, and remains, that a great team brings in both top tier clients and appeals to a wide-range of diverse, talented colleagues. Fostering an inclusive culture, which welcomes differing perspectives and beliefs, enables us to provide the best advice and insights to our clients.

To learn more about PJT Partners, please visit our website atwww.pjtpartners.com. 

Responsibilities

The Strategic Advisory group is one of the most experienced teams in the industry, and has been involved in some of the largest, most complex transactions in recent years.  Our global team focuses on a diverse set of industries, including financial sponsors, financial institutions, insurance, asset managers, technology, media & telecommunications, energy, power, utilities & renewables, consumer, services, industrials, healthcare, and real estate, gaming, lodging & leisure. 

Associates within the Strategic Advisory team would be responsible for the following:

  • Financial analysis and modelling
  • Drafting of client presentations
  • Preparation of memoranda for internal and external use
  • Conducting financial and other business-related research
  • Coordinating complex and time-sensitive processes
  • Active involvement in client facing situations
  • Assistance in the execution of transactions
  • Participation in talent development efforts, including recruiting

Qualifications

As a firm, we seek to hire individuals who are highly motivated, intelligent, and are proven self-starters. In addition to strong analytical and quantitative skills, the successful candidate should have excellent communication skills, a demonstrated ability to write effectively, interests outside of work and a desire to work in a team environment.  A strong background in finance, accounting, and alternative investment strategies is required. To be considered for the position, applicants must also meet the following criteria:

  • Bachelor’s Degree
  • 2-5 years of investment banking experience, or an MBA degree and 2+ years of investment banking or closely related experience
  • Resume must be in PDF format.

Expected annualized base salary of $200,000 to $250,000.

This range represents the low and high ends of the expected annualized base salary for this position. The specific base salary received by any employee will be informed by a number of factors, including but not limited to, role level and scope, location, candidate’s relevant qualifications, skills and experience.

Base salary is one component of PJT Partners’ compensation structure. In addition to base salary, PJT Partners’ compensation structure may include additional rewards, incentives, and benefits, including but not limited to, a discretionary bonus component.

PJT is an equal opportunity employer. We do not discriminate on the basis of, and will consider all qualified applicants for employment without regard to race, color, religious creed, religion, sex, pregnancy, national origin, ancestry, citizenship status, age, marital or partnership status, sexual orientation, gender identity expression, disability, medical condition, genetic information or predisposition, veteran or military status, status as a victim of domestic violence, a sex offense or stalking, or any other category protected by law. PJT Partners also complies with all applicable laws with regard to providing reasonable accommodation of disabilities to applicants. For more information or to request an accommodation, please contact Human Resources.

California Applicants: PJT Partners will consider for employment qualified applicants with arrest and/or conviction records in a manner consistent with applicable law including, but not limited to, the San Francisco Fair Chance Ordinance and/or Los Angeles Fair Chance Initiative for Hiring Ordinance.

View our Applicant Privacy Notice https://info.pjtpartners.com/PJT_Global_Applicant_Privacy_Notice here. If you are a California resident, please refer to our California Applicant Privacy Notice https://info.pjtpartners.com/California_Applicant_Privacy_Notice for further information.

*In order to be considered, please ensure your resume/CV is submitted in PDF format.

jobs.title: Analyst - Strategic Advisory (Insurance)
jobs.description:
PJT Partners is a global advisory-focused investment bank. Our team of senior professionals delivers a wide array of strategic advisory, shareholder advisory, restructuring and special situations and private fund advisory and placement services to corporations, financial sponsors, institutional investors and governments around the world. We offer a unique portfolio of advisory services designed to help our clients achieve their strategic objectives. We also provide, through PJT Park Hill, private fund advisory and fundraising services for alternative investment managers, including private equity funds, real estate funds and hedge funds.

From the beginning, PJT Partners has firmly believed that having the best people is key to building an enduring franchise. Our perspective was, and remains, that a great team brings in both top tier clients and appeals to a wide-range of diverse, talented colleagues. Fostering an inclusive culture, which welcomes differing perspectives and beliefs, enables us to provide the best advice and insights to our clients.

To learn more about PJT Partners, please visit our website atwww.pjtpartners.com. 

Responsibilities

The Strategic Advisory group is one of the most experienced teams in the industry, and has been involved in some of the largest, most complex transactions in recent years. Our global team focuses on a diverse set of industries, including technology, media & telecommunications, consumer & retail, financials & insurance, energy, oil & gas, power, utilities & renewables, industrials, aerospace & defense, healthcare and real estate, gaming, lodging, and leisure.

The Insurance Analyst will be a critical member of PJT’s Insurance platform with direct involvement in both deal execution and business development. The team is stable, tight-knit, collaborative and is seeking to add to its talent pool to respond to a sustained increase in deal activity. Insurance Analysts should expect to work on lean deal teams executing complex and intellectually satisfying projects for blue-chip clients.

We are seeking qualified candidates who possess knowledge and interest in Insurance Advisory. Analysts would be responsible for the following:

  • Financial analysis and modeling
  • Drafting of client presentations
  • Preparation of memoranda for internal and external use
  • Conducting financial and other business-related research
  • Coordinating complex and time-sensitive processes 
  • Active involvement in client facing situations
  • Assistance in the execution of transactions
  • Participation in talent development efforts, including recruiting

Qualifications

PJT Partners seeks to hire individuals who are highly motivated, intelligent and have excellent academic, leadership and extracurricular records. In addition, qualified candidates will possess the following:

  • 1 – 3 years of experience in an Insurance group at a bulge bracket or boutique advisory firm or in other relevant field
  • Analytical and detail oriented with strong problem solving skills
  • Strong verbal and written communication skills
  • Self-motivated and proactive
  • Aptitude to work collaboratively in a team environment
  • Robust organizational and time management skills
  • Strong work ethic and ability to work well under pressure
  • Resume must be in PDF format 

Expected annualized base salary of $120,000 to $150,000.

This range represents the low and high ends of the expected annualized base salary for this position. The specific base salary received by any employee will be informed by a number of factors, including but not limited to, role level and scope, location, candidate’s relevant qualifications, skills and experience.

Base salary is one component of PJT Partners’ compensation structure. In addition to base salary, PJT Partners’ compensation structure may include additional rewards, incentives, and benefits, including but not limited to, a discretionary bonus component.

PJT is an equal opportunity employer. We do not discriminate on the basis of, and will consider all qualified applicants for employment without regard to race, color, religious creed, religion, sex, pregnancy, national origin, ancestry, citizenship status, age, martial or partnership status, sexual orientation, gender identity expression, disability, medical condition, genetic information or predisposition, veteran or military status, status as a victim of domestic violence, a sex offense or stalking, or any other category protected by law. PJT Partners also complies with all applicable laws with regard to providing reasonable accommodation of disabilities to applicants. For more information or to request an accommodation, please contact Human Resources.

California Applicants: PJT Partners will consider for employment qualified applicants with arrest and/or conviction records in a manner consistent with applicable law including, but not limited to, the San Francisco Fair Chance Ordinance and/or Los Angeles Fair Chance Initiative for Hiring Ordinance

Applicant Privacy Notice:

View our Applicant Privacy Notice https://info.pjtpartners.com/PJT_Global_Applicant_Privacy_Notice  here. If you are a California resident, please refer to our California Applicant Privacy Notice https://info.pjtpartners.com/California_Applicant_Privacy_Notice  for further information.

jobs.title: Analyst - PJT Park Hill (Private Equity - Project Management)
jobs.description:
PJT Partners is a global advisory-focused investment bank. Our team of senior professionals delivers a wide array of strategic advisory, shareholder advisory, restructuring and special situations and private fund advisory and placement services to corporations, financial sponsors, institutional investors and governments around the world. We offer a unique portfolio of advisory services designed to help our clients achieve their strategic objectives. We also provide, through PJT Park Hill, private fund advisory and fundraising services for alternative investment managers, including private equity funds, real estate funds and hedge funds.

From the beginning, PJT Partners has firmly believed that having the best people is key to building an enduring franchise. Our perspective was, and remains, that a great team brings in both top tier clients and appeals to a wide-range of diverse, talented colleagues. Fostering an inclusive culture, which welcomes differing perspectives and beliefs, enables us to provide the best advice and insights to our clients.

To learn more about PJT Partners, please visit our website atwww.pjtpartners.com. 

Responsibilities:

PJT Park Hill’s Private Equity Group offers analysts the chance to engage directly in advising “best in class” private equity firms on all aspects of the capital raise – including strategic fundraising advisory, market positioning and ultimately raising capital from the top institutional investors across the globe.

The private placement process uniquely develops both the quantitative and qualitative skills required at more advanced levels across the financial spectrum.

Our Private Equity team is seeking a Project Management Analyst with strong analytical and writing skills, who takes initiative, has great attention to detail, works well under pressure, and has the ability to work well both independently and within teams in a boutique investment advisory firm. Each fundraising deal is typically run by a two-person deal team – a Senior Project Manager and an Analyst.

The Project Management Analyst will be based out of our London office and will provide support to PJT Park Hill’s business in Asia.

Candidates for the position must possess knowledge of and interest in the alternative asset industry—specifically private equity. The job requires management of multiple projects often under time constraints/deadlines, interaction and constant communication with clients, and great attention to detail.

Due to the nature of this role supporting the Hong Kong region, we ask for flexibility in the hours worked within this role, starting at 7am but finishing early. 

Additional responsibilities include:

  • Support the Hong Kong deal team in all aspects of the fundraising process;
  • Work directly with clients in drafting offering memorandums and marketing materials,
  • Coordinate roadshow scheduling and logistics;
  • Prepare for and coordinate all due diligence meetings and materials; complete LP due diligence questionnaires;
  • Assist the Partners in various research projects and the sourcing, diligence and origination of new mandates;
  • Manage and maintain external PJT Park Hill materials, including marketing/pitch materials, fund offering booklets and executive presentations for the senior Partners.

Qualifications

PJT Partners seeks to hire individuals who are highly motivated, intelligent and have demonstrated excellence in prior endeavors. In addition, qualified candidates will possess the following:

  • 1-2 years prior experience in private placements, investment banking, alternative asset investor relations or similar fields;
  • Bachelor Degree required with Accounting, Finance, or Economics major preferred
  • Strong, detail-oriented analytical skills;
  •  Knowledge of financial modelling and leveraged buyout analysis;
  • Ability to work in a fast paced, dynamic environment and to handle multiple projects at a time
  • A self-starter with the ability to work effectively as part of a team
  • Excellent interpersonal and communication skills, both written and verbal
  • Strong work ethic and results-oriented mentality;
  • Personal and professional integrity;
  • Proficiency in Excel and PowerPoint.
  • Fluency in Japanese, Mandarin or Korean would be helpful

This role could offer future relocation opportunities to Hong Kong over the medium term (3-4 years) so would be particularly interesting to somebody connected to the region.

PJT is an equal opportunity employer. We do not discriminate on the basis of, and will consider all qualified applicants for employment without regard to race, color, religious creed, religion, sex, pregnancy, national origin, ancestry, citizenship status, age, marital or partnership status, sexual orientation, gender identity expression, disability, medical condition, genetic information or predisposition, veteran or military status, status as a victim of domestic violence, a sex offense or stalking, or any other category protected by law. jobs.title: Principal - PJT Park Hill (Private Capital Solutions)
jobs.description:
PJT Partners is a global advisory-focused investment bank. Our team of senior professionals delivers a wide array of strategic advisory, shareholder advisory, restructuring and special situations and private fund advisory and placement services to corporations, financial sponsors, institutional investors and governments around the world. We offer a unique portfolio of advisory services designed to help our clients achieve their strategic objectives. We also provide, through PJT Park Hill, private fund advisory and fundraising services for alternative investment managers, including private equity funds, real estate funds and hedge funds.

From the beginning, PJT Partners has firmly believed that having the best people is key to building an enduring franchise. Our perspective was, and remains, that a great team brings in both top tier clients and appeals to a wide-range of diverse, talented colleagues. Fostering an inclusive culture, which welcomes differing perspectives and beliefs, enables us to provide the best advice and insights to our clients.

To learn more about PJT Partners, please visit our website atwww.pjtpartners.com. 

Responsibilities

PJT Park Hill (“Park Hill”) is our industry-leading advisor and intermediary business focused on alternative investment vehicles in the private equity, real estate, hedge fund and secondary advisory asset classes.

The Private Capital Solutions group, our secondary advisory practice, is one of the most experienced investment banking teams in the industry and has been involved in some of the largest, most complex transactions since the team’s founding in 2008. The practice leverages the firm’s global relationships to deliver superior results across the private capital universe. Our highly specialized team focuses on delivering independent advisory and execution services to financial sponsors and institutional investors across a broad spectrum of strategic transactions including:

  • Institutional Investor Solutions: Divesture of limited partnership portfolios of private fund interests, valuation of private fund interests, transaction structuring and execution support.
  • Financial Sponsor Solutions: Fund recapitalizations, fund restructurings, tender offers, management team spinouts and portfolio asset sales;
  • Structured Liquidity Solutions: Preferred equity, NAV financing, Securitizations and other debt-oriented solutions for illiquid financial holdings

Principals within Private Capital Solutions would be responsible for the following:

  • Leading the advisory and execution of secondary transactions, including LP portfolio sales, GP-led solutions (continuation funds, restructurings, and tender offers) and other private market liquidity solutions;
  • Overseeing the structuring of complex transactions, including managing negotiations, pricing and term sheet development;
  • Overseeing financial modelling, valuation analysis and due diligence processes to ensure robust transaction execution;
  • Participating in both internal and external client meetings, negotiation of contracts and other agreements and due diligence sessions;
  • Managing and mentoring junior team members, providing guidance on deal execution and fostering their professional development.

Qualifications

PJT Partners seeks to hire individuals who are highly motivated, intelligent and have demonstrated excellence in prior endeavors. In addition, qualified candidates will possess the following:

  • 10+ years of financial services experience in private equity, secondary advisory or investment banking;
  • Bachelor’s degree in Accounting or Finance is preferred but not required
  • Outstanding quantitative and analytical skills;
  • Strong verbal and written communication skills;
  • Proven ability to work collaboratively and effectively within a team environment;
  • Exceptional organization and time management skills;
  • Strong work ethic and ability to work under pressure.

PJT is an equal opportunity employer. We do not discriminate on the basis of, and will consider all qualified applicants for employment without regard to race, color, religious creed, religion, sex, pregnancy, national origin, ancestry, citizenship status, age, marital or partnership status, sexual orientation, gender identity expression, disability, medical condition, genetic information or predisposition, veteran or military status, status as a victim of domestic violence, a sex offense or stalking, or any other category protected by law. PJT Partners also complies with all applicable laws with regard to providing reasonable accommodation of disabilities to applicants. For more information or to request an accommodation, please contact Human Resources.

PJT Partners’ Strategic Advisory French Coverage team in Paris is seeking for an Associate with strong analytical and writing skills, who takes initiative, has great attention to detail, works well under pressure, and has the ability to perform both independently and within teams in a boutique investment advisory firm. Associates within the Advisory group work directly with companies in evaluating, structuring and recommending financial and strategic alternatives.   • Conducting research of targeted industries for investment purposes.

  • Performing valuation analysis of prospective investment opportunities under consideration, including the assessment of financial risks.

  • Comparing a company’s financial data with other companies of the same industry, size, and location to the forecast of industry trends.

  • Designing and develop quantitative models to assess the economic performance of targeted companies.

  • Preparing written analysis and evaluations of investment opportunities for use by PJT Partners management and for review by clients.

  • Participating in both internal and external client meetings, negotiation of contracts and other agreements, and due diligence sessions.

PJT Partners seeks to hire individuals who are highly motivated, intelligent and have demonstrated excellence in prior endeavors. In addition, qualified candidates will possess the following:

jobs.title: Vice President - Restructuring and Special Situations (French Speaker)
jobs.description:
PJT Partners is a global advisory-focused investment bank. Our team of senior professionals delivers a wide array of strategic advisory, shareholder advisory, restructuring and special situations and private fund advisory and placement services to corporations, financial sponsors, institutional investors and governments around the world. We offer a unique portfolio of advisory services designed to help our clients achieve their strategic objectives. We also provide, through PJT Park Hill, private fund advisory and fundraising services for alternative investment managers, including private equity funds, real estate funds and hedge funds.

Fostering an inclusive culture, which welcomes differing perspectives and beliefs, enables us to provide the best advice and insights to our clients.

To learn more about PJT Partners, please visit our website atwww.pjtpartners.com. 

Responsibilities

The Restructuring and Special Situations Group advises large companies, governments and creditor groups in financially distressed situations. The group has been involved in most of the largest and most high-profile reorganizations that have occurred in the last decade. In a restructuring transaction, a wide range of strategic advisory services are provided, including advice on recapitalizations, reorganizations, corporate valuations, exchange offers, distressed mergers and acquisitions and raising debt and equity capital.

VPs in the Restructuring and Special Situations Group have the opportunity to develop a diverse and broad corporate finance skill set. Financial restructuring transactions are complex and involve multi-party negotiations with a number of key stakeholders including, among others, the board of directors, management, secured and unsecured lenders and equity holders.

Additional responsibilities include:

  • Helping to lead the origination and execution of restructuring transactions across our platform;
  • Frequent senior-level interaction with clients and investors;
  • Developing detailed business plans;
  • Developing strategies to implement various restructuring transactions;
  • Evaluating financial returns to current and potential investors;
  • Attending key client negotiating and strategy sessions;
  • Managing complex and often time-sensitive restructuring transactions from start to finish;
  • Participating in both internal and external client meetings, negotiation of contracts and other agreements, and due diligence sessions.

Qualifications

PJT Partners seeks to hire individuals who are highly motivated, intelligent and have demonstrated excellence in prior endeavors. In addition, qualified candidates will possess the following:

  • At least 6 years of work experience in corporate concentrated in restructuring, leveraged & acquisition finance or credit
  • Fluency in English and French is required
  • Bachelor’s or Masters degree
  • Strong qualitative and analytical skills
  • Strong communication skills
  • Ability to run client meetings and coordinate deal execution
  • Strong written and presentation skills
  • A desire to work in a team environment, often under tight deadlines
  • Strong knowledge of accounting and finance
  • Ability to mentor and train more junior bankers
  • Personal and professional integrity

PJT is an equal opportunity employer. We do not discriminate on the basis of, and will consider all qualified applicants for employment without regard to race, color, religious creed, religion, sex, pregnancy, national origin, ancestry, citizenship status, age, marital or partnership status, sexual orientation, gender identity expression, disability, medical condition, genetic information or predisposition, veteran or military status, status as a victim of domestic violence, a sex offense or stalking, or any other category protected by law. jobs.title: Vice President - Strategic Advisory (India Coverage)
jobs.description:
PJT Partners is a global advisory-focused investment bank. Our team of senior professionals delivers a wide array of strategic advisory, shareholder advisory, restructuring and special situations and private fund advisory and placement services to corporations, financial sponsors, institutional investors and governments around the world. We offer a unique portfolio of advisory services designed to help our clients achieve their strategic objectives. We also provide, through PJT Park Hill, private fund advisory and fundraising services for alternative investment managers, including private equity funds, real estate funds and hedge funds.

From the beginning, PJT Partners has firmly believed that having the best people is key to building an enduring franchise. Our perspective was, and remains, that a great team brings in both top tier clients and appeals to a wide-range of diverse, talented colleagues. Fostering an inclusive culture, which welcomes differing perspectives and beliefs, enables us to provide the best advice and insights to our clients.

To learn more about PJT Partners, please visit our website atwww.pjtpartners.com. 

Responsibilities

PJT Partners’ Strategic Advisory team in Mumbai is seeking for a Vice President with strong analytical and writing skills, who takes initiative, has great attention to detail, works well under pressure, and has the ability to perform both independently and within teams in a boutique investment advisory firm.

The Advisory group is one of the most experienced investment banking teams in the industry, and has been involved in some of the largest, most complex transactions of the past twenty years. Our global team focuses on a diverse set of industries, including technology, media & telecommunications, energy, power & utilities, oil & gas, industrials, healthcare, and real estate, gaming & lodging.

Vice Presidents within the Advisory group work directly with companies in evaluating, structuring and recommending financial and strategic alternatives. They typically work on a wide variety of transactions including mergers, acquisitions, joint ventures, asset sales, restructurings and divestitures. They are staffed directly on client teams and are expected to be involved in all aspects of a transaction, including due diligence, valuation analysis, and the negotiating of contracts and other agreements. The comparatively small size of this group offers professionals the unique opportunity to gain client exposure and work closely with senior members of the team, many of whom were previously group heads at leading investment banking firms.

Responsibilities for this individual will include:

  • Analysing financial data and developments in financial markets important to the business of PJT Partners.

  • Conducting research of targeted industries for investment purposes.

  • Performing valuation analysis of prospective investment opportunities under consideration, including the assessment of financial risks.

  • Comparing a company’s financial data with other companies of the same industry, size, and location to the forecast of industry trends.

  • Designing and developing quantitative models to assess the economic performance of targeted companies.

  • Preparing written analysis and evaluations of investment opportunities for use by PJT Partners management and review by clients.

  • Participating in both internal and external client meetings, negotiation of contracts and other agreements, and due diligence sessions.

  • Work with senior management and coordinate associate / analyst materials

  • Mentoring and training of juniors

In addition to core advisory responsibilities, the individual may play a role in supporting the establishment and ongoing management of PJT Partners’ merchant banking activities in India. This may include, subject to internal governance and regulatory approvals, acting as or supporting the Principal Officer for the purposes of applicable SEBI (Merchant Bankers) regulations, with responsibility for oversight of merchant banking operations, governance, compliance, and regulatory engagement in India. The Vice President will be a critical member of PJT’s expanding India Coverage team with direct involvement in both deal execution and business development, working on lean deal teams with direct exposure to the most senior practitioners. The role will span across a number of sectors. Given the lean nature of our teams all our bankers are flexible and should be prepared to work in sectors/countries outside their main area of focus.

Qualifications

PJT Partners seeks to hire individuals who are highly motivated, intelligent and have demonstrated excellence in prior endeavors. In addition, qualified candidates will possess the following:

  • Bachelor’s Degree in finance, law, accountancy or business management from a government recognised university or institution, or from an overseas university or institution

  • Minimum of 5 years’ directly comparable experience within investment banking at an India based merchant bank and with a strong focus on M&A

  • Certificate for NISM Series-IX: Merchant Banking Certification Examination

  • A strong background in finance/accounting is required

  • Strong analytical and quantitative skills

  • Excellent communication skills

  • Resume must be in PDF format

jobs.title: Vice President - Restructuring and Special Situations
jobs.description:
PJT Partners is a global advisory-focused investment bank. Our team of senior professionals delivers a wide array of strategic advisory, shareholder advisory, restructuring and special situations and private fund advisory and placement services to corporations, financial sponsors, institutional investors and governments around the world. We offer a unique portfolio of advisory services designed to help our clients achieve their strategic objectives. We also provide, through PJT Park Hill, private fund advisory and fundraising services for alternative investment managers, including private equity funds, real estate funds and hedge funds.

From the beginning, PJT Partners has firmly believed that having the best people is key to building an enduring franchise. Our perspective was, and remains, that a great team brings in both top tier clients and appeals to a wide-range of diverse, talented colleagues. Fostering an inclusive culture, which welcomes differing perspectives and beliefs, enables us to provide the best advice and insights to our clients.

To learn more about PJT Partners, please visit our website atwww.pjtpartners.com. 

Responsibilities

The Restructuring and Special Situations Group advises large companies, governments and creditor groups in financially distressed situations. The group has been involved in most of the largest and most high-profile reorganizations that have occurred in the last decade. In a restructuring transaction, a wide range of strategic advisory services are provided, including advice on recapitalizations, reorganizations, corporate valuations, exchange offers, distressed mergers and acquisitions and raising debt and equity capital.

VPs in the Restructuring and Special Situations Group have the opportunity to develop a diverse and broad corporate finance skill set. Financial restructuring transactions are complex and involve multi-party negotiations with a number of key stakeholders including, among others, the board of directors, management, secured and unsecured lenders and equity holders.

Additional responsibilities include:

  • Helping to lead the origination and execution of restructuring transactions across our platform;
  • Frequent senior-level interaction with clients and investors;
  • Developing detailed business plans;
  • Developing strategies to implement various restructuring transactions;
  • Evaluating financial returns to current and potential investors;
  • Attending key client negotiating and strategy sessions;
  • Managing complex and often time-sensitive restructuring transactions from start to finish;
  • Participating in both internal and external client meetings, negotiation of contracts and other agreements, and due diligence sessions.

Qualifications

PJT Partners seeks to hire individuals who are highly motivated, intelligent and have demonstrated excellence in prior endeavors. In addition, qualified candidates will possess the following:

  • At least 6 years of work experience in corporate concentrated in restructuring, leveraged & acquisition finance or credit
  • Fluency in English is required
  • Bachelor’s or Masters degree
  • Strong qualitative and analytical skills
  • Strong communication skills
  • Ability to run client meetings and coordinate deal execution
  • Strong written and presentation skills
  • A desire to work in a team environment, often under tight deadlines
  • Strong knowledge of accounting and finance
  • Ability to mentor and train more junior bankers
  • Personal and professional integrity

PJT is an equal opportunity employer. We do not discriminate on the basis of, and will consider all qualified applicants for employment without regard to race, color, religious creed, religion, sex, pregnancy, national origin, ancestry, citizenship status, age, marital or partnership status, sexual orientation, gender identity expression, disability, medical condition, genetic information or predisposition, veteran or military status, status as a victim of domestic violence, a sex offense or stalking, or any other category protected by law. jobs.description:
PJT Partners is a global advisory-focused investment bank. Our team of senior professionals delivers a wide array of strategic advisory, shareholder advisory, restructuring and special situations and private fund advisory and placement services to corporations, financial sponsors, institutional investors and governments around the world. Responsibilities

PJT Park Hill’s Private Equity Group offers analysts the chance to engage directly in advising “best in class” private equity firms on all aspects of the capital raise – including advisory work, market positioning and ultimately raising capital from the top institutional investors across the globe.

The private placement process uniquely develops both the quantitative and qualitative skills required at more advanced levels across the financial spectrum.

Our Private Equity team is seeking a Project Management Analyst with strong analytical and writing skills, who takes initiative, has great attention to detail, works well under pressure, and has the ability to work well both independently and within teams in a boutique investment advisory firm. Each fundraising deal is typically run by a two-person deal team – a Senior Project Manager and an Analyst.

Candidates for the position must possess knowledge of and interest in the alternative asset industry—specifically private equity. The job requires management of multiple projects often under time constraints/deadlines, interaction and constant communication with clients, and great attention to detail.

Additional responsibilities include:

  • Support the deal team in all aspects of the fundraising process;
  • Work directly with clients in drafting offering memorandums and marketing materials,
  • Coordinate roadshow scheduling and logistics;
  • Prepare for and coordinate all due diligence meetings and materials; complete LP due diligence questionnaires;
  • Assist the Partners in various research projects and the sourcing, diligence and origination of new mandates;
  • Manage and maintain external PJT Park Hill materials, including marketing/pitch materials, fund offering booklets and executive presentations for the senior Partners.

Qualifications

PJT Partners seeks to hire individuals who are highly motivated, intelligent and have demonstrated excellence in prior endeavors. In addition, qualified candidates will possess the following:

  • 1-2 years prior experience in private placements, investment banking, alternative asset investor relations or similar fields;
  • Bachelor Degree required with Accounting, Finance, or Economics major preferred
  • Strong, detail-oriented analytical skills;
  •  Knowledge of financial modeling and leveraged buyout analysis;
  • Ability to work in a fast paced, dynamic environment and to handle multiple projects at a time
  • A self-starter with the ability to work effectively as part of a team
  • Excellent interpersonal and communication skills, both written and verbal
  • Strong work ethic and results-oriented mentality;
  • Personal and professional integrity;
  • Proficiency in Excel and PowerPoint.

Expected annualized base salary of $120,000 to $130,000.

This range represents the low and high ends of the expected annualized base salary for this position. The specific base salary received by any employee will be informed by a number of factors, including but not limited to, role level and scope, location, candidate’s relevant qualifications, skills and experience.

Responsibilities

PJT Park Hill (“Park Hill”) is our industry-leading advisor and intermediary business focused on alternative investment vehicles in the private equity, real estate, hedge fund and secondary advisory asset classes.

The Private Capital Solutions group, our secondary advisory practice, is one of the most experienced investment banking teams in the industry and has been involved in some of the largest, most complex transactions since the team’s founding in 2008. The practice leverages the firm’s global relationships to deliver superior results across the secondaries universe. Our highly specialized team focuses on delivering independent advisory and execution services to financial sponsors and institutional investors across a broad spectrum of strategic transactions including:

  • Financial Sponsor Solutions: Fund recapitalizations, continuation vehicles, fund restructurings, tender offers, preferred equity / other debt oriented solutions, management team spinouts and portfolio asset sales
  • Structured Liquidity Solutions: Securitizations, refinancings of illiquid financial holdings and joint ventures 
  • Institutional Investor Solutions: Divesture of private fund interests, valuation of private fund interests, transaction structuring and execution support.

Analysts within the Private Capital Solutions - Secondaries group work directly with clients in evaluating, structuring and recommending financial and strategic alternatives. The remit of the position spans the private capital spectrum with an emphasis on private equity secondary transactions. The role is industry and asset class agnostic, and the Analyst will be a generalist across transactions.

Analysts are key deal team members who are staffed directly on client teams and are expected to be involved in all aspects of a transaction, including due diligence, valuation analysis, client and process management, and investor engagement.

The comparatively small size of this group and the frequent cross-collaboration across PJT offers professionals the unique opportunity to gain client exposure and work closely with senior members across the PJT platform.

Qualifications

PJT Partners seeks to hire individuals who are highly motivated, intelligent and have demonstrated excellence in prior endeavors. In addition, qualified candidates will possess the following:

  • At least 2 years of investment banking M&A, private equity or secondary advisory experience strongly preferred;
  • Outstanding quantitative and analytical skills;
  • Strong verbal and written communication skills;
  • Proven ability to work collaboratively and effectively within a team environment;
  • Exceptional organization and time management skills;

jobs.description:
PJT Partners is a global advisory-focused investment bank. Our team of senior professionals delivers a wide array of strategic advisory, shareholder advisory, restructuring and special situations and private fund advisory and placement services to corporations, financial sponsors, institutional investors and governments around the world. We offer a unique portfolio of advisory services designed to help our clients achieve their strategic objectives. We also provide, through PJT Park Hill, private fund advisory and fundraising services for alternative investment managers, including private equity funds, real estate funds and hedge funds.

From the beginning, PJT Partners has firmly believed that having the best people is key to building an enduring franchise. Our perspective was, and remains, that a great team brings in both top tier clients and appeals to a wide-range of diverse, talented colleagues. Fostering an inclusive culture, which welcomes differing perspectives and beliefs, enables us to provide the best advice and insights to our clients.

To learn more about PJT Partners, please visit our website atwww.pjtpartners.com. 

Responsibilities

The Associate focused in the Power, Utilities & Renewables space will be a critical part of expanding the platform with heavy involvement in both deal execution and business development, working with lean deal teams and directly with senior power & utilities practitioners at PJT.

Responsibilities for this individual will include:

  • Evaluating, structuring, and recommending financial and strategic alternatives, including the analysis of mergers, acquisitions, and other strategic combinations, asset sales, and divestures.

  • Leading the day-to-day execution of transactions, including due diligence, valuation analysis, and the negotiating of contracts and other agreements; work with senior management and coordinate associate / analyst materials.

  • Analysing financial data and developments in financial markets important to the business of PJT Partners.

  • Conducting research of targeted industries for investment purposes.

  • Performing valuation analysis of prospective investment opportunities under consideration, including the assessment of financial risks.

  • Comparing a company’s financial data with other companies of the same industry, size, and location to the forecast of industry trends.

  • Designing and develop quantitative models to assess the economic performance of targeted companies.

  • Preparing written analysis and evaluations of investment opportunities for use by PJT Partners management and review by clients.

  • Participating in both internal and external client meetings, negotiation of contracts and other agreements, and due diligence sessions.

We are seeking candidates who possess knowledge of and interest in the Power & Utilities industry, as this role will be specifically within PJT’s Power & Utilities sector in Strategic Advisory.

Qualifications

We seek to hire individuals who are highly motivated, intelligent, and are proven self-starters. In addition to strong analytical and quantitative skills, the successful candidate should have excellent communication skills, a demonstrated ability to write effectively, interests outside of work and a desire to work in a team environment. A strong background in finance/accounting is required. To be considered for the position, applicants must also meet the following criteria:

  • Bachelor’s Degree

  • Minimum 3 years of experience in infrastructure / power & utilities investment banking (utilities, power, infrastructure, renewables or clean energy)

  • Resume must be in PDF format

PJT is an equal opportunity employer. We do not discriminate on the basis of, and will consider all qualified applicants for employment without regard to race, color, religious creed, religion, sex, pregnancy, national origin, ancestry, citizenship status, age, martial or partnership status, sexual orientation, gender identity expression, disability, medical condition, genetic information or predisposition, veteran or military status, status as a victim of domestic violence, a sex offense or stalking, or any other category protected by law. PJT Partners also complies with all applicable laws with regard to providing reasonable accommodation of disabilities to applicants. For more information or to request an accommodation, please contact Human Resources.

California Applicants: PJT Partners will consider for employment qualified applicants with arrest and/or conviction records in a manner consistent with applicable law including, but not limited to, the San Francisco Fair Chance Ordinance and/or Los Angeles Fair Chance Initiative for Hiring Ordinance

Applicant Privacy Notice:

View our Applicant Privacy Notice http://info.pjtpartners.com/PJT_Global_Applicant_Privacy_Notice here. If you are a California resident, please refer to our California Applicant Privacy Notice https://info.pjtpartners.com/California_Applicant_Privacy_Notice  for further information.

*In order to be considered, please ensure your resume/CV is submitted in PDF format.
