jobs.title: Associate, Capital Advisory
jobs.description:
We are trusted investment banking advisors to business owners and senior executives of leading private equity firms and their portfolio companies and to public and privately held companies around the world. Our services include mergers and acquisitions advisory, private funds and capital markets advisory and valuations and fairness opinions. As one tightly integrated team of more than 1,400 professionals in 25+ offices in 16 countries, we offer an unobstructed perspective on the global private capital markets, backed by superb execution and a deep commitment to client success. With extensive industry knowledge and relationships, timely market intelligence and strategic insights, we forge deep, productive client relationships that endure for decades. Connect with us to learn more at www.lincolninternational.com.

At Lincoln International, we know that the success of our global organization is a direct outcome of the strength of our people. We encourage diversity of thought and create talent density by attracting, retaining and engaging high performers from all demographics, backgrounds and perspectives. We value diversity, equity and inclusion as a catalyst to foster innovation and creative problem solving, access the best candidates and adapt to a highly dynamic, competitive environment. We know that employing a diverse team and valuing the unique perspectives of our people allows us to better understand and serve the needs of our clients.

Lincoln International is committed to creating a diverse environment and is proud to be an equal opportunity employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, gender, gender identity or expression, sexual orientation, national origin, genetics, disability, age, or veteran status. We encourage you to read more about our commitment to diversity and Environmental, Social & Governance (ESG) efforts.

Lincoln International is seeking an Associate to join our growing Capital Advisory Team, based in Frankfurt. Successful candidates have excellent cognitive and interpersonal abilities, want to develop their professional and personal skills, and are motivated by the prospect of building a leading M&A advisory firm. Successful Associates are motivated self-starters with the desire and potential to succeed in a fast-paced, people oriented and entrepreneurial environment.

In Germany, our team comprises over 90 bankers covering a very broad range of sectors, including Industrials & Renewables, Consumer, Technology, Business Services, Healthcare, as well as Capital Advisory, located in Frankfurt, Munich and Berlin. We are proud to consistently rank among the top in M&A league tables, especially for sell sides for private equity clients. In 2023, Lincoln International successfully advised on over 300 transactions worldwide.

The European Lincoln International Capital Advisory Group successfully advised on 350+ transactions worldwide since its inception in the range of Euro 50 to 350 million. In 2024 we had another record performance of our global Capital Advisory Group by providing creative and alternative financing solutions to clients out of 5 European offices with more than 25 dedicated professionals in order to secure capital for acquisition financings, investments, refinancings & recapitalisations, staple financings staving off defaults, restructuring businesses and navigating distressed situations and bankruptcies, and developing structured capital solutions.

Duties and Responsibilities:

Responsibilities at Lincoln International are often more entrepreneurial than at other firms providing self-motivated individuals with many opportunities to contribute to a dynamic and growing platform.

  • Conduct market research and financial analysis to support client engagements
  • Support senior team members within the Capital Advisory Group (CAG) in Frankfurt in executing debt financing transactions (acquisition financing, refinancing and recapitalisation deals) as part of the wider European CAG team
  • Prepare marketing documents and pitches
  • Carry out financial analysis & modelling, including analysing business forecasts, scenario analysis and covenant sensitivities
  • Identify and contacting finance providers on transactions
  • Collaborate with cross-functional teams both across Europe within the CAG team as well as local M&A teams to ensure seamless project execution
  • Attend client meetings and participate in discussions
  • Interact with senior bankers and clients’ senior executives
  • Leading or implementing initiatives focused on growing the firm’s franchise value

The firm offers a unique, flexible culture that rewards initiative and allows an Associate to develop as quickly as he/she is capable.

The following factors differentiate the offering at Lincoln International:

  • Unrivalled deal flow and the corresponding learning opportunities that arise from this
  • A true ‘mid-cap approach’, working in small deal teams with extensive client exposure as well as cross border contexts
  • Introduction to a vast array of industries
  • Entrepreneurial spirit and flat hierarchies, which will mean significant team exposure, including to very seasoned and experienced senior bankers and provides an opportunity to make a valued contribution and have an impact
  • A steep learning curve in corporate finance, valuation and strategic thinking
  • An extensive Training Curriculum at all levels
  • Opportunity to participate in our US and European Secondment Programmes
  • Mentoring Scheme, onboarding sessions and structured developmental feedback
  • The chance to build your career within a growth environment.  

Qualifications:

The Associate position at Lincoln International offers an excellent learning experience and provides a solid foundation upon which an individual can build a future business career. Interested candidates should bring the following requirements:

  • 3-4 years prior professional experience in Capital Advisory, Leveraged Finance, Transaction Services or Business Modelling experience, preferably in the mid market
  • Fluency and thorough understanding in Accounting, Corporate Finance and/or Leveraged Finance coupled with excellent Business Acumen
  • An exceptional, well rounded academic background, ideally educated to Masters level in a business related subject
  • Great analytical skills and attention to detail, along with a willingness to develop and learn
  • Solid and growing understanding of business models and sectors coupled with a good grasp of corporate strategy and themes related to company growth and development
  • A proactive and self-motivated approach, with a commitment to personal growth, underpinned through professional and academic excellence as well as other pursuits and personal projects
  • Adaptability, dependability and the ability to manage a dynamic workload
  • Clear and engaging communication skills- verbally as well as written. Is able to relate and connect to other stakeholders quickly- clients, management teams and LI colleagues
  • Collaborative style and a personally engaging manner
  • International experiences and perspectives
  • Excellent written and spoken German and English skills, other European Languages are a plus
  • Proficiency in Microsoft Excel and PowerPoint

Additional Information:

Lincoln International is not currently hiring individuals for this position who now or in the future require sponsorship for employment visa status.

Click hereto view Lincoln International's Candidate Privacy Notices.

Are you looking to leverage your sales skills in a dynamic, fast-paced, investment banking environment? Lincoln International offers sophisticated investment banking services to companies of various sizes, including large corporations pursuing middle-market deals and mid-sized companies engaged in “life event” transactions. We are seeking inside sales specialists to successfully support our bankers with mergers and acquisitions opportunities by prospecting and cold-calling into the C-suite.

This is an exciting opportunity to become a member of our newly formed Private Company Coverage (PCC) team and play a critical role in the development and success of a new division within Lincoln International.

This is a unique opportunity and might be the right fit for you if:

  • You can talk to anyone about anything at any time, especially over the telephone.
  • Your communication skills are unparalleled, and you speak clearly and with persuasion.
  • You’re an excellent writer.
  • You’re self-motivated to “knock it out of the park every single time.”
  • You have a positive can-do attitude and look for the best in people and situations.
  • Your competitive spirit and passion for winning is off the charts.

Duties & Responsibilities:

As an expert in sales and marketing for the leading investment bank in the middle-market, you will work with our team of “A” players on a variety of matters as it relates to business development:

  • Leverage phone calls, email, LinkedIn, and other appropriate channels to contact and persuade C-level executives to take introductory meetings with our bankers.
  • Utilize Salesforce to record prospect communication activities, drive follow-up with senior business development professionals and bankers where appropriate.
  • Build and modify phone scripts and email templates for prospecting.
  • Understand Lincoln International’s service offerings, industry profiles, and basic investment banking concepts.
  • Compile and submit weekly reports.
  • Prepare for and successfully pass the Securities Industry Essentials (SIE), Series 7, and Series 63 examinations to obtain General Securities Representative and state registration credentials.

Qualifications:

Required:

  • Bachelor’s degree
  • 1+ years of relevant sales experience
  • Microsoft Excel and Word experience

Preferred:

  • Cold-Calling experience
  • Appointment Setting experience
  • Salesforce experience

Additional Information:

Lincoln International is not currently hiring individuals for this position who now or in the future require sponsorship for employment visa status.

#LI-REMOTE

This is a full-time position, with the ability to work remotely.

The salary range for this role is $70,000-$75,000 on an annual basis. This is the lowest to highest salary we in good faith believe we would pay for this role at the time of this posting. We may ultimately pay more or less than the posted range, and the range may be modified in the future. An employee’s pay position within the salary range will be based on several factors including, but not limited to, relevant education, qualifications, certifications, experience, skills, seniority, geographic location, performance, shift, travel requirements, revenue-based metrics, and business or organizational needs. 

This job is also eligible for bonus pay.

We offer a comprehensive package of benefits to eligible employees.

You can apply for this role through LinkedIn or Lincoln’s Company Page, or through Lincoln’s Internal Jobs Board if you are a current employee.

Note: No amount of pay is considered to be wages or compensation until such amount is earned, vested, and determinable. The amount and availability of any bonus, commission, or any other form of compensation that are allocable to a particular employee remains in the Company's sole discretion unless and until paid and may be modified at the Company’s sole discretion, consistent with the law.

If you are passionate about inside sales, we want to hear from you! Apply today to join a leading investment banking firm and make a significant impact on our client relationships and business growth.

Duties & Responsibilities:

Lincoln International is seeking Analysts to join our rapidly growing Valuations & Opinions Group (VOG). Successful candidates have strong cognitive and interpersonal abilities, want to develop their professional and personal skills and are motivated by the prospect of building a leading valuations practice.

The Analyst position within the Valuations & Opinions Group provides an excellent opportunity to become an expert in valuations. The Analyst serves as the analytical and organizational anchor for the Valuations & Opinions Group. Responsibilities at Lincoln International are often more entrepreneurial than at other firms providing self-motivated individuals with many opportunities to contribute to a dynamic and growing platform. Successful Analysts are assertive, motivated self-starters with the desire and potential to succeed in a fast-paced, entrepreneurial environment.

We envision the Valuations and Opinions Analyst will be initially tasked with the following:

  • Preparing, completing, and ensuring the accuracy of financial models and sophisticated valuation analyses
  • Writing valuation reports and presentations
  • Valuation and analysis of amongst other things, mortgage whole loans, student loans, consumer loans as well as RMBS, CMBS, CLO, ABS, and other structured finance securities
  • Development and maintenance of valuation models for complex financial instruments including private structured finance securities
  • Coordinating recurring valuation projects with team members across multiple geographies
  • Interacting with clients and preparing deliverables
  • Maintaining proprietary valuation databases
  • Tracking the capital markets and analyzing any changes within the markets
  • Preparing marketing materials for meetings with potential clients
  • Supporting the senior members of the Valuations & Opinions Group

Qualifications:

The Analyst position at Lincoln International is extremely challenging, offers an excellent learning experience and provides a solid foundation upon which an individual can build a future business career. Interested candidates should meet the following requirements:

Professional and Cultural Characteristics:

  • Authentic individual with high integrity, whose personal and professional values are consistent with Lincoln’s Culture Statement (Excellence, Entrepreneurship, Integration, Collaboration, Integrity)
  • Assertive, articulate, and self-motivated, can manage multiple tasks and deadlines in a fast-paced environment
  • Strong collaborator who enjoys working in an entrepreneurial environment
  • Driven and ambitious professional who gains satisfaction from achieving personal and team goals
  • Ability to demonstrate good judgment and handle highly confidential information in a professional manner

Additional Qualifications:

  • Six months to two years of structured products experience required 
  • Bachelor's degree in Finance, Accounting, Mathematics, Engineering, or Economics from top tier institution.
    • Strong academic record (both undergraduate and graduate, if applicable)
  • Proficiency at financial modeling with advanced Microsoft Excel skills
  • Strong writing skills and experience with PowerPoint and Microsoft Word
  • Effective organizational skills and the aptitude to manage people and work products
  • Strong communication skills and experience interacting with teammates and clients
  • One year of professional experience in valuations, corporate finance, fixed income or accounting is strongly preferred

jobs.title: Experienced Analyst, Portfolio Valuations
jobs.location: New York, NY; Dallas, TX; Atlanta, GA; San Francisco, CA
jobs.description:
We are trusted investment banking advisors to business owners and senior executives of leading private equity firms and their portfolio companies and to public and privately held companies around the world. Our services include mergers and acquisitions advisory, private funds and capital markets advisory and valuations and fairness opinions. As one tightly integrated team of more than 1,400 professionals in 25+ offices in 16 countries, we offer an unobstructed perspective on the global private capital markets, backed by superb execution and a deep commitment to client success. With extensive industry knowledge and relationships, timely market intelligence and strategic insights, we forge deep, productive client relationships that endure for decades. Connect with us to learn more at www.lincolninternational.com.

At Lincoln International, we know that the success of our global organization is a direct outcome of the strength of our people. We encourage diversity of thought and create talent density by attracting, retaining and engaging high performers from all demographics, backgrounds and perspectives. We value diversity, equity and inclusion as a catalyst to foster innovation and creative problem solving, access the best candidates and adapt to a highly dynamic, competitive environment. We know that employing a diverse team and valuing the unique perspectives of our people allows us to better understand and serve the needs of our clients.

Lincoln International is committed to creating a diverse environment and is proud to be an equal opportunity employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, gender, gender identity or expression, sexual orientation, national origin, genetics, disability, age, or veteran status. We encourage you to read more about our commitment to diversity and Environmental, Social & Governance (ESG) efforts.

Lincoln International follows a hybrid work structure with an emphasis on in office collaboration.

Our employees work in our offices Monday through Thursday each week, with the option to work remotely or from the office on Fridays.

Duties & Responsibilities:

Lincoln International is seeking Analysts to join our rapidly growing Valuations & Opinions Group (VOG). Successful candidates have strong cognitive and interpersonal abilities, want to develop their professional and personal skills, are excited to gain exposure to a broad range of asset classes and industries, and are motivated by the prospect of building a leading valuations practice.

The Analyst position within the Valuations & Opinions Group provides an excellent opportunity to become an expert in portfolio valuations, though Analysts may occasionally be required or volunteer to work on valuation engagements for other valuation services. The Analyst serves as the analytical and organizational anchor for the Valuations & Opinions Group and would have the opportunity to work with clients including many of the most sophisticated, blue chip institutional investment firms in the industry. Responsibilities at Lincoln International are often more entrepreneurial than at other firms providing self-motivated individuals with many opportunities to contribute to a dynamic and growing platform. Successful Analysts are assertive, motivated self-starters with the desire and potential to succeed in a fast-paced, entrepreneurial environment.

We envision the Valuations and Opinions Analyst will be initially tasked with the following:

  • Supporting our portfolio valuation team, which provides valuations of illiquid debt and equity securities held by hedge funds, private equity funds, and business development companies in connection with fair value reporting of the funds’ assets to investors
  • Performing financial statement analysis and other quantitative and qualitative assessments
  • Reviewing client investment memoranda, board of directors’ presentations, specific debt and equity agreements, and financial models to develop an understanding of the company or securities being analyzed
  • Compiling statistical summaries of companies’ financial information, developing and computing financial ratios, and presenting the analyses in an organized manner and/or using our standard formats and presentations
  • Reviewing and compiling published financial information, such as public company filings, press releases, reports from published research analysts, and Bloomberg data across a wide range of industries and sectors
  • Identifying relevant comparable public companies and M&A transactions, based upon information services such as S&P Capital IQ and Bloomberg, along with knowledge of the appropriate Standard Industrial Classification and other methods, as appropriate
  • Constructing and reviewing valuation and other financial analyses, including the guideline public companies, guideline precedent transactions, and discounted cash flow analyses, using Lincoln International proprietary models
  • Researching and providing information on:
    • General economic conditions
    • Industry-specific conditions and trends
    • Acquisitions and divestitures within specific industries
    • Various investment attributes of publicly traded and privately held securities
  • Participating in due diligence meetings, communicating with clients and responding to auditor questions regarding valuation analyses prepared by Lincoln International
  • Designing and drafting client deliverables
  • Assisting in the preparation of fee proposals to clients, including scoping discussions, pitch materials, and internal engagement pricing committee memoranda
  • Performing other ad-hoc research, analytics, and support for the VOG team as required
  • Coordinating with internal administrative support teams to prepare engagement invoices and other compliance/end-of-engagement close out procedures

Qualifications:

The Analyst position at Lincoln International is extremely challenging, offers an excellent learning experience and provides a solid foundation upon which an individual can build a future business career. Interested candidates should meet the following requirements:

Professional and Cultural Characteristics:

  • Authentic individual with high integrity, whose personal and professional values are consistent with Lincoln’s Culture Statement (Excellence, Entrepreneurship, Integration, Collaboration, Integrity)
  • Assertive, articulate, and self-motivated, can manage multiple tasks and deadlines in a fast-paced environment
  • Strong collaborator who enjoys working in an entrepreneurial environment
  • Driven and ambitious professional who gains satisfaction from achieving personal and team goals
  • Ability to demonstrate good judgment and handle highly confidential information in a professional manner

Additional Qualifications:

  • Excellent analytic foundation with advanced understanding of financial statements and various valuation methodologies (DCF, LBO, comparable company analysis, etc.)
  • Excellent understanding of financial statements analysis with the ability to work through key assumptions, implicit caveats, and limitations
  • Bachelor's degree in Finance, Accounting or Economics from top tier institution; Master’s degree is preferred but not required
    • Strong academic record of 3.5 GPA or above (both undergraduate and graduate)
  • Proficiency at financial modeling with advanced Microsoft Excel skills
    • Understanding of integrated cash flow modeling is preferred
  • One year of professional experience in valuations, corporate finance, accounting, or related
  • Strong writing skills and experience with PowerPoint and Microsoft Word
  • Effective organizational skills and the aptitude to manage people and work products
  • Strong communication skills and experience interacting with teammates and clients
  • Active CPA and/or minimum of CFA Level I is preferred, but not required

Additional Information:

Lincoln International is not currently hiring individuals for this position who now or in the future require sponsorship for employment visa status.

The salary range for this role is $95,000 to $105,000 on an annual basis. This is the lowest to highest salary we in good faith believe we would pay for this role at the time of this posting. We may ultimately pay more or less than the posted range, and the range may be modified in the future. This range is only applicable for jobs to be performed in Illinois, New York or California. An employee’s pay position within the salary range will be based on several factors including, but not limited to, relevant education, qualifications, certifications, experience, skills, seniority, geographic location, performance, shift, travel requirements, revenue-based metrics, and business or organizational needs.  
 
This job may also be eligible for discretionary bonus pay.**
 
We offer a comprehensive package of benefits including medical, dental, and vision insurance, and retirement benefits to eligible employees.
 
You can apply for this role through LinkedIn or Lincoln’s Company Page, or through Lincoln’s Internal Jobs Board if you are a current employee.

Lincoln International is seeking a Graduate Intern for a period of twelve months to join our rapidly growing Valuations & Opinions Group (VOG). We envision the Valuations and Opinions Intern will be initially tasked with the following in support of our Asset-backed Finance Team:

  • Preparing, completing, and ensuring the accuracy of financial models and sophisticated valuation analyses
  • Writing valuation reports and presentations
  • Valuation and analysis of amongst other things, mortgage whole loans, student loans, consumer loans as well as Rated Feeder, SRT, CFO, RMBS, CMBS, CLO, ABS, and other structured finance securities
  • Development and maintenance of valuation models for complex financial instruments including private structured finance securities
  • Coordinating recurring valuation projects with team members across multiple geographies
  • Interacting with clients and preparing deliverables
  • Maintaining proprietary valuation databases
  • Tracking the capital markets and analyzing any changes within the markets
  • Preparing marketing materials for meetings with potential clients
  • Supporting the senior members of the Valuations & Opinions Group

The Intern position at Lincoln International is extremely challenging, offers an excellent learning experience and provides a solid foundation upon which an individual can build a future business career. Interested candidates should meet the following requirements:

  • Excellent analytic foundation with advanced understanding of financial statements and various valuation methodologies (DCF, LBO, comparable company analysis, etc.)
  • Excellent understanding of financial statements analysis with the ability to work through key assumptions, implicit caveats, and limitations
  • Recently graduated with a Bachelor's or Master's degree in Finance, Accounting or Economics from a top tier institution
    • Strong academic record, both undergraduate and graduate, of at least 2:1 (UK), 3.5 GPA (US), 1.5 GPA (Germany) or local equivalent 
  • Proficiency at financial modeling with advanced Microsoft Excel skills
    • Understanding of integrated cash flow modeling is preferred
  • Experience or interest in Structured Products
  • Strong writing skills and experience with PowerPoint and Microsoft Word
  • Effective organizational skills and the aptitude to manage people and work products
  • Strong communication skills and experience interacting with teammates and clients

jobs.title: Intern, M&A
jobs.description:
We are trusted investment banking advisors to business owners and senior executives of leading private equity firms and their portfolio companies and to public and privately held companies around the world. Our services include mergers and acquisitions advisory, private funds and capital markets advisory and valuations and fairness opinions. As one tightly integrated team of more than 1,400 professionals in 25+ offices in 16 countries, we offer an unobstructed perspective on the global private capital markets, backed by superb execution and a deep commitment to client success. With extensive industry knowledge and relationships, timely market intelligence and strategic insights, we forge deep, productive client relationships that endure for decades. Connect with us to learn more at www.lincolninternational.com.

At Lincoln International, we know that the success of our global organization is a direct outcome of the strength of our people. We encourage diversity of thought and create talent density by attracting, retaining and engaging high performers from all demographics, backgrounds and perspectives. We value diversity, equity and inclusion as a catalyst to foster innovation and creative problem solving, access the best candidates and adapt to a highly dynamic, competitive environment. We know that employing a diverse team and valuing the unique perspectives of our people allows us to better understand and serve the needs of our clients.

Lincoln International is committed to creating a diverse environment and is proud to be an equal opportunity employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, gender, gender identity or expression, sexual orientation, national origin, genetics, disability, age, or veteran status. We encourage you to read more about our commitment to diversity and Environmental, Social & Governance (ESG) efforts.

In Belgium, we are a generalist M&A team that covers a broad range of sectors, including Industrials, Consumer, Tech, Business Services and Healthcare. Europe-wide, we are proud to consistently rank among the top in M&A league tables, especially for sell-sides for private equity clients. In 2025, Lincoln International successfully advised on over 420 transactions worldwide.

We offer:

To support our team in Brussels, we are looking for curious and driven individual who would like to join us as interns for 3-5 months. Internship start will be May 2026. Our internship in Brussels offers the chance to gain hands-on experience and valuable exposure to high-level financial advisory work, providing you as intern with the opportunity to develop key skills in a dynamic, fast-paced environment.

What else you can look forward to:

  • Unrivalled deal flow and the corresponding extensive learning opportunities ranging from marketing efforts and origination to live projects and execution
  • A true ‘mid-cap approach’, which means working in small deal teams within one or two sector groups, being able to grab different business models and experience a variety of different deal situations
  • Entrepreneurial spirit and flat hierarchies, which will mean significant team exposure to seasoned bankers and the opportunity to make valuable contributions
  • A steep learning curve in corporate finance, valuation and strategic thinking
  • Wallstreet Prep/Training the Street, access to our Lincoln Learning Platform and our own Lincoln AI tool as development support


Your Role

You will receive the opportunity to work alongside experienced bankers and be involved in all aspects of an M&A transaction - thereby develop a solid understanding of M&A processes.

Key responsibilities include the following:

  • Conducting market, industry and company research
  • Supporting deal execution and contributing to the drafting of marketing materials such as teasers, information memorandums, and other relevant documentation
  • Carrying out financial analyses and assisting with valuations, identifying a buyer universe, participating in the due diligence and closing processes
  • Involvement in pitch presentation preparation
  • Supporting senior bankers with business development and meeting preparation

You offer:

  • An exceptional, well rounded academic background
  • A genuine curiosity for business models and sectors, coupled with a good understanding of corporate strategy and themes, related to company growth and development
  • Previous internships in Corporate Finance related fields that have nurtured your interest in accounting, Finance, M&A, and valuation which you know would like to develop further
  • Great analytical skills and attention to detail, along with a willingness to develop and learn.
  • A proactive and self-motivated approach, with a commitment to personal growth, whether through academic excellence, creative pursuits, or personal projects.
  • Adaptability, dependability, and the ability to manage a dynamic workload
  • Clear communication skills, with the ability to collaborate well with others, and a personally engaging manner
  • An array of international experiences and perspectives
  • Excellent written and spoken Dutch and English skills, French is considered a plus
  • Proficiency in Microsoft Excel and PowerPoint

During an internship at Lincoln, you will be an integral part of the team and actively contribute to live pitches and deals.   • Market and sector analyses
  • Pitches, Information Memoranda, and other deal-related documents
  • Financial models
  • Business valuations
  • Buyer analyses
  • Due diligence

Qualifications:

  • Ambitious third-year bachelor or master’s students
  • Above-average academic results, combined with extracurricular activities
  • Enthusiastic and motivated team player, with a clear interest in M&A
  • Strong analytical thinking, business acumen, and attention to detail
  • Proficient in both Dutch and English

Are you curious about pursuing a career in M&A?
We are now accepting applicants for our full-time internship program in Stockholm during the fall of 2026!

The Role
The Intern position offers an opportunity to work in a challenging, supportive, and team-oriented environment. We invest in the development of our junior bankers through proactive on-the-job training combined with global training programs. By combining a high degree of client and senior banker exposure with a robust deal flow you will quickly develop into a critical anchor in our deal team. Our globally integrated organization provides the opportunity to work on cross-border engagements with international clients and deal teams, giving you an unmatched global perspective.

What we are looking for
To succeed as an Intern, we believe that you have:
▪ A commercial mindset and a genuine interest in financial markets
▪ Excellent analytical skills; creativity combined with a critical mind and attention to detail
▪ Excellent writing and verbal communication skills
▪ Strong computer skills (Excel, PowerPoint, Word)
▪ Strong drive and an ability to work independently with an ample capacity for work
▪ A team player attitude; relationship building personality
▪ Fluency in English and one Nordic language

The duration of the internship will be August to December 2026. Applications are reviewed on an ongoing basis but please submit no later than 31 March. Important: Please attach a transcript of your grades to your cover letter or CV in order to be considered.

Duties & Responsibilities:

Based in Chicago, Illinois, the Valuation Systems Analyst will serve as a key member of Lincoln’s Global Business Enablement team, with the primary role of supporting the technology underlying Lincoln’s proprietary valuation model. The Valuation Systems Analyst will work with internal and external stakeholders to understand business challenges, using technology and process as tools to address these challenges. The Valuation Systems Analyst will gain a deep understanding of Lincoln’s Valuations and Opinions business. 

The Valuation Systems Analyst position at Lincoln provides an excellent opportunity to work in a demanding, yet supportive, team-oriented environment. The Valuation Systems Analyst should have relevant professional experience, be entrepreneurial, poised, and able to deal with many different people both inside as well as outside the firm. Strong communication skills are critical to success in this role.  Lincoln fosters a cohesive and supportive work environment and encourages teamwork.

Primary Responsibilities:

  • Play a leading role in the development and maintenance of a high performance and scalable Anaplan model and related infrastructure by collaborating with team members to perform new build and existing re-architecture, updates & optimization
  • Collaborate closely Lincoln’s Valuations & Opinions group to gather feedback and requirements, drive the product roadmap, and design solutions
  • Assist in the development and maintenance of integrations and automated reporting templates in Workiva, integrations with 3rd party data sources, and data pipelines to downstream databases
  • Provide technical support for global Anaplan users and manage product support
  • Assist with change management including helping lead training sessions, developing training and support materials, drafting communications, and collaborating with leadership to drive platform adoption
  • Work closely with team members to design effective solutions, share best practices, support learning and development, and actively contribute to team success.
  • Work with internal teams across departments, external teams, and clients to achieve program goals

Qualifications:

Interested candidates should meet the following requirements:

Professional and Cultural Characteristics:

  • Authentic individual with high integrity, whose personal and professional values are consistent with Lincoln’s Culture Statement (Excellence, Entrepreneurship, Integration, Collaboration, Integrity)
  • Assertive, articulate, and self-motivated, can manage multiple tasks and deadlines in a fast-paced environment
  • Resourceful, motivated problem solver with ability to identify and execute next steps
  • Strong collaborator who enjoys working in an entrepreneurial environment
  • Driven and ambitious professional who gains satisfaction from achieving personal and team goals
  • Desire to learn new concepts and technology to enhance solutions and collaborate with teams
  • Ability to effectively interface effectively with all levels of management and banking staff
  • Strong written and verbal communication skills
  • Strong interpersonal skills and work well in a team environment

Other Qualifications:

    • Passion for technical problem solving, business analytics and financial modeling
    • Bachelor’s degree in Accounting Information Systems, Engineering, Computer Science, Information Technology, Management Information Systems, Accounting, Finance, or equivalent
    • 1-2 years Anaplan model building experience required
      • Anaplan L2 certification or higher
    • 1-2 years of overall relevant technical experience (or related consulting or financial experience)
      • Other experience in software implementation or corporate finance and valuations a plus
    • Experience in developing, deploying, and troubleshooting complex multidimensional models or other software solutions
    • Experience working with internal and external stakeholders to understand functional reporting requirements, translating these into technical requirements, and completing iterative reporting build based on these requirements
    • Experience in change management, drafting internal and external communications, training materials, and presentations
    • Experience in valuations and finance a plus

jobs.title: Vice President, Asset-Backed Finance
jobs.location: New York, NY; Dallas, TX; Chicago, IL
jobs.description:
We are trusted investment banking advisors to business owners and senior executives of leading private equity firms and their portfolio companies and to public and privately held companies around the world. Our services include mergers and acquisitions advisory, private funds and capital markets advisory and valuations and fairness opinions. As one tightly integrated team of more than 1,400 professionals in 25+ offices in 16 countries, we offer an unobstructed perspective on the global private capital markets, backed by superb execution and a deep commitment to client success. With extensive industry knowledge and relationships, timely market intelligence and strategic insights, we forge deep, productive client relationships that endure for decades. Connect with us to learn more at www.lincolninternational.com.

At Lincoln International, we know that the success of our global organization is a direct outcome of the strength of our people. We encourage diversity of thought and create talent density by attracting, retaining and engaging high performers from all demographics, backgrounds and perspectives. We value diversity, equity and inclusion as a catalyst to foster innovation and creative problem solving, access the best candidates and adapt to a highly dynamic, competitive environment. We know that employing a diverse team and valuing the unique perspectives of our people allows us to better understand and serve the needs of our clients.

Lincoln International is committed to creating a diverse environment and is proud to be an equal opportunity employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, gender, gender identity or expression, sexual orientation, national origin, genetics, disability, age, or veteran status. We encourage you to read more about our commitment to diversity and Environmental, Social & Governance (ESG) efforts.

Lincoln International follows a hybrid work structure with an emphasis on in office collaboration.

Our employees work in office Monday through Thursday each week, with the option to work remotely or from the office on Fridays. Our New York office is located at 299 Park Avenue, just blocks from Grand Central Station.

Duties & Responsibilities:

Lincoln International’s Valuations & Opinions Group, is an industry leader in the valuation of alternative and illiquid investments for investment funds, including business development companies (BDCs), hedge funds, and private equity groups.  The group also advises on valuation matters for corporate transactions, including acquisitions and divestitures, leveraged buyouts, restructurings, recapitalizations, related party transactions, and for general tax, accounting, litigation, and regulatory purposes.

Lincoln International is seeking a Vice President to join the Valuations & Opinions Group with fixed income structured product experience.  Officers work closely with the firm’s Managing Directors and clients in providing valuation and financial advisory services across multiple engagements and offices. Lincoln International’s practice is growing rapidly, and we are looking to add a talented individual to support our client service and quality control activities, expand our capabilities and bolster our marketing efforts. Responsibilities at Lincoln International are often more entrepreneurial than at other firms providing self-motivated individuals with many opportunities to contribute to a dynamic and growing platform. A successful candidate will be an assertive, motivated self-starter with the desire and potential to succeed in a fast-paced, entrepreneurial environment. Prior investment banking and/or transaction experience is preferred but not required.

Key Responsibilities:

  • Leading project teams providing valuation and financial advisory services to clients, including determining appropriate valuation methodologies and reviewing financial models for amongst other things, mortgage whole loans, student loans, consumer loans as well as RMBS, CMBS, CLO, ABS, and other structured finance securities
  • Providing quality control oversight to engagement teams
  • Managing, training and developing associates and analysts at the firm
  • Proactive calling on potential new clients including alternative investment managers (hedge funds, private equity, private credit, BDCs, etc.), financial institutions, privately held business owners and publicly traded companies
  • Identifying and communicating potential engagements to management and writing business proposals and engagement letters

Qualifications:

  • Minimum of five years’ experience in performing business valuations, valuations of illiquid and complex securities, and/or structuring, trading, or whole loan modelling
  • Understanding of collateral risk characteristics, deal structures, terms, and conditions
  • Experience with third party systems such as Bloomberg and Intex
  • Proficiency in designing financial models for discounted cash flow, market multiple, market transaction, and option pricing analyses
  • Ability to develop valuation approaches for various forms of securities and unique instruments including equity and debt securities, loans, derivative instruments, structured products and intangible assets
  • Strong analytical and problem-solving skills and ability to critically analyze companies and understand key business issues, including value drivers and investment risks
  • Strong verbal and written communication skills
  • Working knowledge of credit markets
  • Experience with successfully managing teams of associates and analysts
  • Understanding of authoritative guidance related to valuations a plus
  • ASC-820 and IFRS 13, valuation industry best practices, and emerging issues
  • Ability to act as a primary point of contact with clients
  • Ability to effectively multi-task, managing multiple client engagements simultaneously
  • Confidently and credibly connect with potential new clients and lead client meetings
  • Excellence in MS Word, Excel and PowerPoint
  • Bachelor’s or Master's degree in Finance, Accounting, Mathematics, Statistics, or Economics or MBA; strong academic record (both undergraduate and graduate)
  • Active CPA, ASA and/or CFA preferred but not required

Additional Information:

Lincoln International is not currently hiring individuals for this position who now or in the future require sponsorship for employment visa status.

#LI-HYBRID

#LI-NYC

The salary range for this role is $250,000 on an annual basis. This is the lowest to highest salary we in good faith believe we would pay for this role at the time of this posting.  We may ultimately pay more or less than the posted range, and the range may be modified in the future.  This range is only applicable for jobs to be performed in New York City.  An employee’s pay position within the salary range will be based on several factors including, but not limited to, relevant education, qualifications, certifications, experience, skills, seniority, geographic location, performance, shift, travel requirements, revenue-based metrics, and business or organizational needs.  

This job is also eligible for bonus pay.

We offer a comprehensive package of benefits to eligible employees.

jobs.description:
We are trusted investment banking advisors to business owners and senior executives of leading private equity firms and their portfolio companies and to public and privately held companies around the world. Our services include mergers and acquisitions advisory, private funds and capital markets advisory and valuations and fairness opinions. As one tightly integrated team of more than 1,400 professionals in 25+ offices in 16 countries, we offer an unobstructed perspective on the global private capital markets, backed by superb execution and a deep commitment to client success. We encourage diversity of thought and create talent density by attracting, retaining and engaging high performers from all demographics, backgrounds and perspectives. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, gender, gender identity or expression, sexual orientation, national origin, genetics, disability, age, or veteran status. We encourage you to read more about our commitment to diversity and Environmental, Social & Governance (ESG) efforts.

Lincoln International is seeking a Vice President to join our growing M&A team in Frankfurt, covering primarily the DACH region. This role entails working closely with the firm’s Managing Directors and Directors in building relationships with clients, potential clients, and finance providers. This individual will need to have deep experience of executing financing transactions in the mid-market and will be expected to contribute to Business Development as well as Associates’ and Analysts’ growth at the firm.

In Germany, our team comprises over 90 bankers covering a very broad range of sectors, including Industrials & Renewables, Consumer, Technology, Business Services, Healthcare, as well as Capital Advisory, located in Frankfurt, Munich and Berlin. We are proud to consistently rank among the top in M&A league tables, especially for sell sides for private equity clients. In 2023, Lincoln International successfully advised on over 300 transactions worldwide.

The European Lincoln International Capital Advisory Group successfully advised on 350+ transactions worldwide since its inception in the range of Euro 50 to 350 million. In 2024 we had another record performance of our global Capital Advisory Group by providing creative and alternative financing solutions to clients out of 5 European offices with more than 25 dedicated professionals in order to secure capital for acquisition financings, investments, refinancings & recapitalisations, staple financings staving off defaults, restructuring businesses and navigating distressed situations and bankruptcies, and developing structured capital solutions.

Duties and Responsibilities:

Responsibilities at Lincoln International are often more entrepreneurial than at other firms providing self-motivated individuals with many opportunities to contribute to a dynamic and growing platform.

  • Conceptualising, organising, processing and delivering new business presentations
  • Contributing to transaction process planning and deal structuring conversations
  • Producing and reviewing transaction marketing materials and complex integrated financial models;
  • Managing Analysts and Associates to ensure timelines are met and quality is maintained
  • Acting as key client point of contact for day-to-day queries, with an ability to discuss deal issues internally and with third parties
  • Identifying and contacting finance providers on transactions
  • Preparing term sheets and reviewing transaction due diligence reports and loan agreements
  • Training and developing Associates and Analysts at the firm
  • Contributing to the team and the firm’s marketing efforts as well as initiatives focused on growing the firm’s franchise value.

The firm offers a unique, flexible culture that rewards initiative and allows an Vice President to make a significant impact. For this role, we are looking for individuals with the potential to develop into senior professionals within the organisation over time.

The following factors differentiate the offering at Lincoln International:
  • Unrivalled deal flow and the corresponding learning opportunities that arise from this
  • A true ‘mid-cap approach’, working in small deal teams, across multiple countries and embedded in a European team with extensive client exposure as well as cross border contexts
  • Entrepreneurial spirit and flat hierarchies, which will mean significant senior banker exposure and the opportunity to make a significant impact
  • A steep learning curve in client interaction, project management and strategic thinking
  • Exposure to a vast array of industries
  • An extensive Training Curriculum at all levels
  • Mentoring Scheme, coaching and structured developmental feedback
  • The chance to build your career towards more senior levels within a growth environment

Qualifications:

The Vice President position at Lincoln International offers an excellent learning experience and provides a solid foundation upon which an individual can build a future business career. Interested candidates should bring the following requirements:

  • Extensive track record of executing financing transactions, preferably in the mid-market, with at least 5.5, ideally 6-7 years’ experience in Capital Advisory/Leverage Finance. 
  • Experience and demonstrated ability in seeking and identifying new business opportunities in concert with Managing Directors.
  • Ability to confidently and credibly connect with potential new clients and to lead client meetings.
  • Excellent finance, accounting and financial modeling skills, coupled with pronounced business acumen and the ability to form clear commercial views.
  • Experience in successfully managing teams of Associates and Analysts; and leading project work.
  • An exceptional, well rounded academic background, ideally educated to Masters level in a business related subject. 
  • Great analytical skills and attention to detail, along with a willingness to develop and learn.
  • A proactive and self-motivated approach, with a commitment to personal and team growth, underpinned through professional and academic excellence as well as other pursuits and personal projects.
  • Adaptability, dependability and the ability to manage a dynamic workload.
  • Clear and engaging communication skills- verbally as well as written. Ability to relate and connect to other stakeholders quickly- clients, management teams and LI colleagues.
  • Collaborative style and a personally engaging manner.
  • International experiences and perspectives. 
  • Excellent written and spoken German and English skills, other European Languages are a plus.
  • Proficiency in Microsoft Excel and PowerPoint.

Additional Information:

Lincoln International is not currently hiring individuals for this position who now or in the future require sponsorship for employment visa status.

  • REQUIRED: Strong track record of executing restructuring and financing transactions with at least 6-8 years’ experience in leverage finance of which at least 2 in debt advisory role(s)
  • Assertive, self-motivated and desire to succeed in a fast-paced, entrepreneurial environment;
  • Ability to seek and identify new business opportunities in concert with Managing Directors;
  • Ability to confidently and credibly connect with potential new clients and to lead client meetings;
  • Excellent finance, accounting and financial modeling and good business acumen;
  • Strong verbal and written communication skills;
  • Experience to successfully manage teams of Associates and Analysts; and
  • Degree in finance and/or accounting desirable, or have successfully completed significant coursework in these or related areas.

Additional Information:

Lincoln International is not currently hiring individuals for this position who now or in the future require sponsorship for employment visa status.

#LI-NYC

The salary for this role is $250,000.  This is the lowest to highest salary we in good faith believe we would pay for this role at the time of this posting.  We may ultimately pay more or less than the posted range, and the range may be modified in the future.  This range is only applicable for jobs to be performed in New York City.  An employee’s pay position within the salary range will be based on several factors including, but not limited to, relevant education, qualifications, certifications, experience, skills, seniority, geographic location, performance, shift, travel requirements, revenue-based metrics, and business or organizational needs.

jobs.title: LionTree - 2027 US Advisory Summer Analyst - NY
jobs.description:
About LionTree:

LionTree is a differentiated global investment and merchant banking firm focused on companies in the technology, media, telecom and consumer-related industries. LionTree was founded in 2012 and has offices in New York, San Francisco, and London to serve a global client base. LionTree has built a successful track record by leveraging its unique insights and relationships to assist clients across its integrated product set: Investment Banking Advisory, Public Markets / IPO Advisory and Merchant Banking Investments.

Since our formation, LionTree has advised on over $850bn of deal volume across over 300+ transactions. LionTree represents clients ranging from large-cap leaders to emerging growth companies. Select notable transactions include: advising Naver on its $1.2bn acquisition of Poshmark, Take-Two Interactive on its $12.7bn acquisition of Zynga, The Athletic on its sale to the New York Times, MGM Studios on its $8.5bn sale to Amazon, WarnerMedia (AT&T) and Discovery’s ~$140bn merger, Viacom on its $47bn merger with CBS, Pandora on its $3.5bn sale to SiriusXM, and Charter on its $78.7bn acquisition of Time Warner Cable.

Program Description:

LionTree offers a 11-week summer analyst program for rising seniors. As a summer analyst, you will be fully immersed in the day-to-day activities of our New York office. The program is designed to provide a realistic view of our full-time Investment Banking Analyst Program. Upon completion of the program, Summer Analysts have the opportunity to receive full-time Analyst offers for a post-graduation start date.

As a summer analyst, you will:

  • Obtain a strong foundation in financial analysis through training from LionTree professionals as well as third-party providers
  • Have the opportunity to work on live client assignments alongside LionTree deal teams
  • Present financial and strategic analysis to the firm’s leadership during your final project
  • Attend orientation and firm events where you will learn about LionTree and the industry while meeting members of the firm globally
  • Gain access to an invaluable peer network of interns, as well as senior firm leaders and other peers through an assigned mentorship team

jobs.title: Graduate Analyst
jobs.description:
Futures Sport & Entertainment - Australia

Futures is a leading global sports and entertainment strategy consultancy. We are part of the Interpublic Group, one of the world’s premier advertising and marketing services companies. We deliver independent and objective analysis of the historic and future value of sport and sponsorship properties, and their contribution to the media and marketing mix. We work for leading teams, federations, events and global brands, as well as investment banks, management consultancies and even governments. Our work covers five broad areas: media, consumer, market, sponsorship and sport research. All are delivered via our market-leading digital dashboards.

The Role

Futures’ core projects span media, sponsorship and fan analytics. Graduate Analysts work alongside Futures’ more seasoned Analysts on projects for sport governing bodies and clubs. Their focus is to help optimise sponsorships, better understand how fans follow sport, and how to drive growth opportunities, domestically or overseas.

Graduate Analysts are required to demonstrate strong analytical skills, creativity in problem-solving and the ability to work effectively as part of a team. They should also have a demonstrable interest and knowledge in the world of sports and entertainment.

Graduate Analysts are tasked with delivering timely, insightful solutions, and help in the development of our software and proprietary tools. They will be able to take direction, complete tasks by given deadlines, manage their workload, communicate clearly and confidently with their peers, managers and clients, and present themselves in a professional manner.

Developing skills for a Graduate Analyst include client service and project management skills that are core to the performance of a professional consultancy business.

Key Responsibilities

  • Manage, analyse and interpret complex data
  • Interpret data to deliver bespoke commercial insight to clients
  • Structure and deliver analysis
  • Regular reporting on status and issues to line management
  • Attendance at key client meetings where appropriate
  • Clear and timely communications with clients
  • Ability to interpret and understand client requests in order to deliver timely and appropriate responses
  • Management and prioritisation of personal deliverables across multiple projects or workstreams
  • Team and project administrative support, including but not limited to, the keeping of meeting notes and the initial development of project timelines and trackers
  • Contribute to writing and preparing client reports and presentations, including and ability to translate complex information into clear, simple, well-presented reports
  • Reading, understanding and insightful points of view on core industry trends across the sports and entertainment landscape
  • Internal presentation of research projects to small groups

Core Skills and Attributes

  • Meticulous attention to detail
  • Excellent quantitative skills, including advanced Excel and PowerPoint skills
  • Continual development of technical skills, especially SQL, with a focus on developing sound analytical processes
  • Self-starter attitude, comfortable taking initiative and leading work streams independently, with some degree of supervision

Important Note: This job description serves as a guide to the scope and range of activities that may be required of the incumbent and may change at any time according to the needs and priorities of the Company.

We kindly encourage all candidates to apply through the official application link, as only submissions received via this channel can be reviewed. To maintain a fair and consistent process for everyone, we ask that all queries and updates be directed through our HR team.

Candidates who attempt to contact team members outside the formal process will not be considered.

We make our careers website accessible to any and all users. If you need an accommodation to participate in the application process, please contact us at JobAppAccommodation@ipgdxtra.com. This email address is not for general employment inquiries or vendors; rather it is strictly for applicants who require special assistance accessing our employment website. Due to volume, messages sent to this email address that are not related to an accommodation cannot be answered.

jobs.title: Director of Convertible Sales
jobs.description:
Solutions for Today’s Challenges. Vision for Tomorrow’s Opportunities.

Join William Blair, the Premier Global Partnership.

Identify and execute complex and large scope fixed income sales and generate revenue aligned with William Blair’s sales strategy. Target key investors and actively surface cross sale leads, striving to increase account penetration. Maintain longstanding internal partnership with other sales professionals and actively develop leads sourced by others. Manage strong relationships with key institutional investors; synthesize client issues by leveraging seasoned expertise into meaningful advice. Leverage pertinent sales opportunities to associate staff. Serve as a sounding board to less experienced staff.

Responsibilities may include but are not limited to:

  • Identify and execute complex and/or large scope equity linked sales aligned with firm and function sales strategy. Target key investors and promote William Blair’s value proposition and competitive advantage. Actively surface cross-sale leads for fellow sales professionals, seeking to increase account penetration. Leverage pertinent sales opportunities to associate staff.
  • Establish and manage strong relationships with key equity linked investors through regular meetings and touchpoints. Synthesize client issues by leveraging seasoned technical expertise into innovative and actionable advice. Promote William Blair’s capabilities and introduce relevant subject matter/sector experts as appropriate.
  • Continually expand depth and breadth of expertise of William Blair’s fixed income products and pricing strategies, as well as current research findings and investment opportunities.
  • Proactively convey research expertise, products and investment opportunities to equity linked clients. Suggest approach/options to accomplish client objectives. Solicit orders that maximize value to both parties.
  • Maintain longstanding internal partnerships with sales professionals and actively develop leads sourced by others.
  • Guide sales meeting preparation by suggesting innovative approaches to materials/presentation content. Review presentations drafted by others. Lead high impact sales meetings.
  • Guide and mentor less experienced staff. Answer questions; help resolve issues; serve as a sounding board for solving client issues and approaching the close of sales.
  • Ensure all work transactions and products are aligned with firm and function policies, procedures and standards.
  • Additional responsibilities as requested.

QUALIFICATIONS:

  • Bachelor’s Degree in Finance, Accounting or Business required
  • MBA from top business school preferred
  • Minimum 5 years of institutional bond sales experience from a top brokerage firm required
  • SIE Exam, Series 7 and Series 63 must be acquired within 90 days of employment
  • Excellent marketing, business development and selling skills
  • Excellent organizational and prioritization skills
  • Excellent verbal and written communication skills
  • Excellent problem solving and analytical skills
  • In depth knowledge of the bond markets
  • Ability to work effectively in a team environment
  • Proficient computer skills (Excel, Word, PowerPoint)

This role is entirely commission-based. Commission does not include any other forms of compensation.

Our featured benefit offerings include medical, dental and vision coverage, employer paid long-term disability and life insurance, 401(k), profit sharing, Maven family & fertility benefit, parental leave (including adoption, surrogacy, and foster placement), as well as other voluntary benefits.

jobs.title: Equity Research Associate - BioTech
jobs.description:
Solutions for Today’s Challenges. Vision for Tomorrow’s Opportunities.

Join William Blair, the Premier Global Partnership.

William Blair’s sell-side equity research team is responsible for delivering superior investment ideas and value-added perspective for over 600 quality growth companies. Through exhaustive fundamental research and unique insight derived from independent thinking, the team’s award-winning analysts work tirelessly to identify companies that can deliver superior investment returns for clients. Acting as the company's laboratory for new investment ideas, it specializes in providing investment analysis on companies in the following industry sectors: consumer; financial services and technology; global industrial infrastructure; global services; healthcare; and technology, media, and communications.

Assemble and validate data on quality growth companies and conduct fundamental research and analyses on industry sectors, companies and securities at the direction of senior Research Analysts. Develop and/or update financial models and other statistical data for security analyses. Document findings and develop reports, presentation materials and educational tools for investment teams, clients and prospects.

Responsibilities include but may not be limited to:

  • Conduct research, build and/or update financial models and other statistical data for use in the formulation of security analysis.
  • Prepare internal and external written reports, notes, and other documents to communicate investment ideas, research, news events and opinions regarding security analysis.
  • Visit companies, attend conferences and other relevant events. Build and maintain relationships with company executives, prepare records and files to develop and maintain sources of information to assist in analysis activities.
  • Answer inquiries from customers, personnel of the firm and the general public regarding individual securities or industry groups.
  • Identify and formulate new investment ideas, and issue opinions and recommendations to clients on securities and companies in one or more industry groups targeted to follow, in order to generate new business activity.
  • Participate in other projects as requested by management.

Qualifications:

  • M.D. or Ph.D. in biology or chemistry (or related life sciences field) strongly preferred.
  • Minimum of 1-2 years related work or internship experience.
  • Strong writing skills required.
  • Strong conceptual and critical thinking skills.
  • Strong accounting and financial statement analysis skills.
  • Capable of building computer generated financial models.
  • Delivers clear, effective verbal and written communication and takes responsibility for understanding others.
  • Proficient in Internet searches and Microsoft Office products.
  • Practical and capable of simplifying and summarizing complex information.
  • Commits to satisfying internal and external customers.
  • SIE Exam, Series 7, 63, 86, and 87 required (must be obtained within first 180 days).

jobs.description:
Solutions for Today’s Challenges. Vision for Tomorrow’s Opportunities.

Join William Blair, the Premier Global Partnership.

Execute in-depth analyses to support new business development, transaction execution and the delivery of investment banking products and services to clients and prospects. Perform research and financial modeling, apply core valuation methods, and complete due diligence activities at the direction of the Investment Banking team lead. Work closely with client staff to advance projects, gathering necessary information, describing financial modeling methodology, and presenting results directly related to scope of analysis. Provide training and mentorship to summer interns and less experienced Analysts.

Responsibilities include but may not be limited to:

  • Perform extensive financial/valuation analyses to value debt and equity for mergers, acquisitions and capital raising transactions. Form varied approaches to analyses once fundamental base of approach is set.
  • Build financial models to value potential and actual clients and/or industries. Execute core valuation methods, such as comparable companies, precedents and discounted cash flow. Effectively synthesize information to convey complex financial modeling outcomes.

jobs.title: Investment Banking Analyst, Healthcare
jobs.description:
Solutions for Today’s Challenges. Vision for Tomorrow’s Opportunities.

Join William Blair, the Premier Global Partnership.

The Global Healthcare Group is actively looking for an Investment Banking Analyst that will support the team in our London office. As part of this role, the Analyst will work across multiple sub-sectors, notably Healthcare IT, Healthcare Services, Pharma Services, Life Sciences & Diagnostics and MedTech, amongst others.

The Analyst will execute in-depth analyses to support new business development, transaction execution and the delivery of investment banking products and services to our clients. In addition, the Analyst will work closely with clients to advance projects, gather necessary diligence information, build financial models, and craft materials critical to the success of completing transactions.

Responsibilities include but may not be limited to:

  • Lead and support in the preparation of financial analyses and presentations necessary for evaluating potential opportunities and transactions
  • Prepare pitch books and live deal materials, including, confidential investor presentations, management presentations, amongst others
  • Execute valuation methodologies such as discounted cash flow, leveraged buyout, precedent transactions, and comparable company analyses
  • Drive process workstreams forward by coordinating and communicating with team members and serving as a point of contact for clients
  • Work alongside Senior Bankers to assist with new business opportunities and broader business development efforts
  • Mentor summer interns and invest in teaching subsequent classes of analysts, serving as a William Blair ambassador across all aspects of the role
  • Additional responsibilities as requested

Qualifications:

  • Bachelor’s Degree required
  • Previous experience at the Analyst level is essential
  • Client first attitude with a drive to deliver exceptional results
  • Thorough quantitative and analytical skills to develop financial models and valuations analyses, with a strong understanding of accounting and finance  
  • Excellent mathematical, writing, verbal and communication skills
  • Ability to work on multiple tasks simultaneously (projects, pitches and business development support)
  • Ability to collaborate with other team members, whilst also being able to work autonomously as required
  • Proficient in Microsoft Office (PowerPoint, Excel and Word)

jobs.title: Investment Banking Analyst, Secondary Advisory
jobs.description:
Solutions for Today’s Challenges. Vision for Tomorrow’s Opportunities.

Join William Blair, the Premier Global Partnership.

William Blair’s Secondary Advisory team of experts provide financial sponsors and their investors with holistic, bespoke solutions across all stages of the private equity lifecycle. The private equity community relies on us for premier guidance and expertise when navigating GP-led transactions (single-asset and multi-asset continuation vehicles, fund restructurings & recapitalizations, spin-outs & spin-ins, portfolio company shareholder liquidity solutions, strip sales, and tender offers) and LP-led transactions. An Analyst in a GP-Led Secondaries role will perform extensive research and execute in-depth analyses to support new business development, transaction execution and the delivery of private market products and services to sponsor clients and prospects, as well as limited partners.

Responsibilities may include but are not limited to:

  • Build bottom-up three statement and LBO financial models to evaluate potential and actual client outcomes, utilizing core valuation methods. Integrate outputs with fund-level analysis (e.g. GP / LP distributions, fund waterfalls).  Effectively synthesize information to convey complex financial modeling outcomes.
  • Demonstrate curiosity of the various private market strategies. Assimilate information to perform insightful analyses.
  • Assist in the development, production and delivery of proposals and presentations to clients and prospects. 
  • Perform due diligence, research, financial analysis, and documentation of potential and live transactions.
  • Take ownership of all deal admin related tasks, such as maintaining deal trackers, scheduling buyer calls, and updating Salesforce.
  • Actively participate in client meetings and internal discussions.
  • Anticipate potential deal issues and pass concerns through to team members in real time.
  • Manage transaction process, include investor tracking and client communications.
  • Partner with more senior-level team members in the development of marketing materials that highlight the competitive advantage of William Blair Private Capital Advisory. 
  • Take ownership of buyer dialogue communications, which includes taking thorough notes on buyer calls, managing incoming emails, and sending follow-up notes when appropriate.
  • Mentor summer interns and invest in teaching subsequent classes of analysts, imparting best practices.

Qualifications and attributes:

  • Bachelor’s Degree required
  • Prior experience (including internships) in investment banking, private equity or Big 4 transaction services required; previous experience as an Analyst, or equivalent preferred
  • Profound understanding of corporate finance and accounting concepts paired with strong understanding of core valuation methodologies, including public and precedent transaction comparables
  • Excellent work ethic and genuine curiosity for private markets transactions
  • Sound judgement, coachability, and ability to balance assertiveness with open-mindedness as required
  • Detail oriented with strong analytical abilities and critical thinking and problem-solving skills
  • Ability to manage expectations of senior bankers while working on multiple projects simultaneously
  • Commitment to satisfying internal and external customers
