jobs.title: Client Portfolio Manager
jobs.description:
D.A. Davidson Companies is an independent, employee-owned company with a rich history spanning 90 years. We are dedicated to conducting our business in accordance with the highest standards of integrity and ethics, and delivering outstanding service to our clients and each other. We support a friendly, open and supportive culture, and encourage candid communication and productive engagement that make our companies and each of us better. Just as we work to improve our clients’ financial well-being, we also work to strengthen local communities—and giving back is one of our core values. You can learn more about our company culture and impact in our latest annual report.
Davidson Investment Advisors is seeking a Client Portfolio Manager to join our growing team. This role is an exciting opportunity to help serve and expand a key segment of our business while contributing to the strong culture of our firm. 
Summary: 
The Client Portfolio Manager plays a critical role in serving nonprofit, endowment, corporate, insurance, and charitable trust clients by helping them structure, manage, and steward their investment portfolios. This position offers the opportunity to work closely with our business development, investment, and operations teams to deliver exceptional client experiences rooted in integrity, professionalism, and a deep understanding of financial markets.
Ideal candidates bring strong communication skills, a passion for mission aligned investing, and the ability to build trust with complex institutional clients. While this role is primarily client service focused, it also plays an important part in driving the ongoing growth and refinement of this business segment.
 Qualifications/Requirements:
• Bachelor’s degree in finance, business or a related field
• 5+ years of experience in financial services, insurance, banking, or nonprofit leadership
• Ability to work independently, analyze complex problems, and provide thoughtful, solutions-oriented recommendations
• Strong understanding of investment strategies and best practices for institutional and nonprofit clients
• Excellent organization and proficiency with Client Relationship Management systems
• High proficiency in Microsoft Office 
• Self-starter with excellent organizational and communication skills, with the ability to multitask in a fast-paced environment effectively
• Detail-oriented with a strong sense of ownership and accountability 
• Collaborative mindset with a commitment to contributing to team success.
Duties: 
• Provide exceptional client service to institutional clients of the firm
o Prepare and present to boards of directors and other decision makers
o Maintain consistent communication with staff and board members regarding market conditions, portfolio strategies, performance, and potential enhancements
o Collaborate with investment and operations teams to align investment strategies and operational deliverables with client needs
o Monitor client portfolios to ensure ongoing compliance with Investment Policies 
• Implement investment strategies for clients that align with their objectives and risk tolerances
• Prospect, pitch, and convert new institutional clients to our firm’s services
• Work closely with D.A. Davidson Financial Advisors to deliver integrated solutions for shared clients
• Drive initiatives to enhance efficiency and optimize workflows; proactively seek ways to improve the team’s processes
• Perform any other duties and special projects as necessary Adhere to all the firm’s Code of Ethics and Policies and Procedures, as well as its Team Agreement, which is a commitment to the following behaviors:
o Curiosity (avoid becoming defensive and remaining open to learning)
o Accountability (taking 100% responsibility (no more; no less) for results; no blaming)
o Candor (engaging in courageous truth-telling to increase power and speed in decisions)
o Awareness (tapping every emotional and intuitional resource for decisions)
o Passion (discovering and allowing people to do what they do best)
o Appreciation (expressing gratitude and fostering a culture of sincere cooperation)
What we offer:
Competitive salary plus excellent benefits and perks including, but not limited to:  
• Medical, Dental and Vision
• Company 401(k)and ESOP contribution 
• Generous sick, vacation, and maternity/parental leave
• Paid holidays
• Professional Development Opportunities
• Tuition Reimbursement ($15,000 lifetime cap)
• Discounted personal insurance including home, auto and recreational vehicles
• Charitable gift-matching program
• Davidson Day of Giving – Our tradition of positively impacting communities in which we live and work.  
The compensation offered will be determined on a case-by-case basis considering a variety of factors including, but not limited to, the skills, relevant work experience, and geographic location of each specific candidate.

jobs.title: Equity Research Associate, Industrial Distribution Team
jobs.description:
D.A. Davidson & Co. - the Strength of Advice
Our firm delivers a breadth of quality financial solutions to individual and institutional investors, companies, and government entities. D.A. Davidson Companies provides straightforward, personalized advice, nationally recognized research, and capital market investment banking expertise to clients who need help achieving their strategic financial goals.
Equity Research Associate: 

D. A. Davidson is seeking an Associate to join a new Industrial Distribution Team and assist in all facets of the franchise, including modeling, note writing, and helping create differentiated research analyses. As the associate develops, responsibilities will expand to include communicating with clients and sales/traders, as well as participating in the creative process. The role requires accuracy, attention to detail and a great deal of common sense. Strong writing, modeling, Excel, and PowerPoint skills are required. Experience in equity research, investment banking, public accounting, consulting or in the Industrial Distribution industry is preferred.

Responsibilities & Professional Experience:

  • Demonstrated ability to generate ideas: work closely and communicate effectively in a time sensitive environment, ability to balance multiple priorities and meet deadlines, strong organizational skills, client service minded and strong writing skills
  • Creative thinker with strong problem-solving skills: working with large data sets in Excel/Access, and ability to source and process information independently
  • Strong communication skills: particularly an ability to write efficiently and concisely
  • Technical skills: Bloomberg and/or FactSet skills helpful, advanced Excel (macros, pivot tables, conditional formatting, short cuts), and statistics (regression, variance, covariance, and correlations)
  • Use mastery of Excel functions: shortcuts, developing complex logic formulas, mastery of reference formulas, ability to integrate graphics/data tables, formatting, and basic programming

Requirements:

  • 1-2 years of experience in sell-side research (preferably on Industrials teams), on the buy-side, investment banking, public accounting, consulting, or within the Industrial Distribution industry is preferred
  • Comfortable working 10+ hour workdays and some weekends
  • Advanced understanding of financial modeling, experience building and maintaining 3-statement models
  • SIE, Series 63, 86 & 87 is preferred, or expected to be completed within the first 90 days of joining
  • Excellent written and verbal communication skills
  • Eager to work in a fast-paced environment both independently and collaboratively
  • Bachelor's Degree or higher form an accredited college or university, concentration in Finance, Accounting, Math, or Statistics preferred
  • MBA, CPA, or CFA a plus
  • Demonstrated academic ability with a minimum 3.5 GPA

jobs.title: Experienced Investment Banking Analyst, Technology
jobs.description:
D.A. Davidson & Co., a leading full-service investment banking firm with operations nationwide, is accepting applicants for full-time Analyst positions working with the firm’s Investment Banking Technology Group. The Experienced Analyst will play an important role working with senior bankers on strategic assignments, assisting clients with mergers and acquisitions, public offerings, private placements and other advisory services.
D.A. Davidson & Co. – The Strength of Advice
Our firm delivers a breadth of quality financial solutions to individual and institutional investors, companies, and government entities. D.A. Davidson Companies provides straightforward, personalized advice, nationally recognized research, and capital market investment banking expertise to clients who need help achieving their strategic financial goals.
At D.A. Davidson, we are committed to fostering a diverse environment that supports the development and inclusivity of all employees.  We are an equal opportunity employer and value diversity at our company. We do not discriminate on the basis of race, religion, color, national origin, sex, gender, gender expression, sexual orientation, age, marital status, veteran status, or disability status. We will ensure that individuals with disabilities are provided reasonable accommodation to participate in the job application or interview process, to perform essential job functions, and to receive other benefits and privileges of employment. Please contact us to request accommodation.

Qualifications:

  • Bright, professional, and motivated with the desire to excel in an intellectually challenging environment
  • 1-2 years experience working in investment banking or transaction experience finance required.
  • Have executed at least one deal start to finish
  • Interest and curiosity in the Technology sector
  • Bachelor’s degree with a minimum 3.5 GPA.
  • Do you enjoy preparing research/analytical content for pitch books, memorandums, presentations and other projects?
  • Working knowledge of business software (especially Word, Excel and PowerPoint).
  • High level of financial knowledge (financial modeling, macroeconomics, current events, etc.).
  • Excellent verbal and written communications skills, strong work ethic and teamwork skills. Self-motivated, with a strong desire to excel in a challenging field.
  • Good judgment in confidential situations and ability to exercise discretion when handling confidential information.
  • Flexibility to work long hours, nights, and weekends, as needed for workflow.
  • Possession of Series 79 (Series 7 may be applicable) and 63 licenses require

In this role you will:

  • Develop financial modules to analyze a wide variety of client-specific projects.
  • Collaborate with an industry team from peer analysts to managing directors.
  • Support business development activities of senior investment bankers.
  • Attend client meetings and interact on transactional and technical issues.
  • Are you looking to actively participate in live deals, such as M&A, IPOs, and advisory?

jobs.title: Experienced Investment Banking Associate, Technology
jobs.description:
D.A. Davidson & Co. - the Strength of Advice
Our firm delivers a breadth of quality financial solutions to individual and institutional investors, companies, and government entities. D.A. Davidson Companies provides straightforward, personalized advice, nationally recognized research, and capital market investment banking expertise to clients who need help achieving their strategic financial goals.
D.A. Davidson’s investment banking division is a leading full-service investment bank that offers comprehensive financial advisory and capital markets expertise for public and private companies, including M&A, equity and debt offerings. The group has extensive transaction experience serving middle market clients worldwide across four industry verticals: Consumer, Diversified Industrials, Financial Institutions, and Technology. Together with its European strategic partner, MCF Corporate Finance, D.A. Davidson originates and executes transatlantic M&A transactions under the common brand of D.A. Davidson MCF International.
D.A. Davidson Companies is an employee-owned financial services firm offering a range of financial services and advice to individuals, corporations, institutions and municipalities nationwide. Founded in 1935 and headquartered in Montana, with investment banking offices in Atlanta, Boston, Chicago, Denver, Great Falls, New York, Orange County, Portland and Salt Lake City, the company has approximately 1,400 employees and offices in 27 states. 
Investment Banking – Experienced Associate 1 or 2 
D.A. Davidson & Co. is accepting applicants for Associate Year 1 and 2 positions in the Technology Investment Banking Group. The Associate will play a vital role working with senior bankers on strategic assignments, assisting clients with mergers and acquisitions, public offerings, private placements and other advisory services.
Associates start on a four-year program with a deep dive into their industry of focus, receive mentorship and sales and public speaking training. Associates are encouraged to assist in recruiting, college outreach, cultural initiatives and ongoing analyst training.

Qualifications:

  • Bright, professional, and motivated with the desire to excel in an intellectually challenging environment
  • Three years minimum of professional experience working in investment banking, private equity, corporate development, etc. is required
  • Bachelor’s degree
  • Highly motivated, confident, professional, and coachable
  • Proficiency with Capital IQ and PitchBook is a plus
  • Advanced knowledge of business software (especially Word, Excel and PowerPoint)
  • Strong knowledge of financial modeling, corporate finance and accounting
  • Excellent verbal and written communications skills including business writing
  • Able to handle multiple projects with competing priorities
  • Strong work ethic and team oriented approach
  • Good judgment in confidential situations and ability to exercise discretion
  • High degree of comfort and confidence interacting with senior executives
  • Ability to train and provide mentorship to analysts and interns
  • Flexibility to work long hours, nights, and weekends, as needed
  • Securities Industry Essentials Exam, Series 79 (Series 7 may be applicable) and 63 licenses strongly preferred but not required. New hires without these licenses will require successful completion upon hiring 

In this role you will:

  • Work closely with senior bankers to support business development, sourcing and execution of transactions
  • Actively participate in live deals, such as M&A, capital offerings and strategic advisory
  • Supervise investment banking analysts, including mentoring and oversight of the work product
  • Take the lead for developing important technical analysis and presentations
  • Create marketing materials
  • Communicate and interact with clients in executing transactions
  • Travel is required (i.e. client meetings, conferences, etc.)
  • Participate in recruiting efforts

D.A. Davidson & Co., a leading investment banking firm with operations nationwide, is accepting applicants for a full-time Vice President position in our Washington, D.C. office to work on a team passionate about the Diversified Industrials industry. The Vice President is expected to project manage strategic assignments assisting clients with mergers and acquisitions, public offerings, private placements and other advisory services. The Vice President is expected to translate Director and Managing Director wishes into achievable tasks, assign projects to Associates and Analysts and see these projects through to completion, be a coach to junior bankers, and build relationships with potential clients.   • Strong transaction history – M&A or Capital Markets experience required, transaction experience in the Aerospace and Defense or Government Services industry strongly preferred
  • Ability to assist senior bankers in business development
  • Ability to lead transaction execution teams

  • Strong financial modeling ability and computer proficiency

  • Superior work ethic and teamwork skills
  • Good judgment and ability to exercise discretion when handling confidential information
  • Ability to perform in both team and self-directed settings
  • Highly motivated, with a desire to excel in a challenging field
  • MBA preferred, with 3 years and investment banking experience, or undergraduate degree with a minimum of 6 years investment banking experience
  • Possession of Series 79 (Series 7 may be applicable) and 63 licenses preferred but not required. New hires without these licenses will be required upon hiring

Duties:

  • Lead the day-to-day execution of transactions (Sell-Side M&A / Buy-Side M&A / Private Capital Raise / Public Offerings), including preparing and reviewing company marketing documents and financial models, managing marketing and due diligence processes, analyzing financing alternatives and negotiating documentation
  • Establish client trust by identifying needs, project leadership, transaction structuring, company positioning, due diligence, negotiating, etc.
  • Assist senior bankers in the business development efforts
  • Contribute to revenue targets for deal teams through relationship development and identifying and recommending deal opportunities for the firm
  • Manage, train and develop Associates and Analysts at the firm
  • Ensure high quality collaboration and production of work flow and product from Associates and Analysts

companyDetails.description: Our firm delivers a breadth of quality financial solutions to individual and institutional investors, companies, and government entities. D.A. Davidson Companies provides straightforward, personalized advice, nationally recognized research, and capital market investment banking expertise to clients who need help achieving their strategic financial goals.

jobs.title: Managed Assets Research Associate
jobs.description:
D.A. Davidson Companies is an independent, employee-owned company with a rich history spanning 90 years. We are dedicated to conducting our business in accordance with the highest standards of integrity and ethics, and delivering outstanding service to our clients and each other. We support a friendly, open and supportive culture, and encourage candid communication and productive engagement that make our companies and each of us better. Just as we work to improve our clients’ financial well-being, we also work to strengthen local communities—and giving back is one of our core values. You can learn more about our company culture and impact in our latest annual report.
Summary/Function: 
D.A. Davidson & Co.’s Managed Assets Research Department provides a multitude of services for our firm’s advisors and their clients. The research team is responsible for developing and maintaining recommendations across the mutual fund, ETF and Separately Managed Account (SMA) landscape. Further, this research department is also responsible for managing a wide array of asset allocation portfolios on a discretionary basis for our firm’s advisors and their clients. Communications with our financial advisors are primarily provided through quarterly presentations, model portfolio reports, client portfolio reviews, conference calls, and availability by direct phone or email for engagement with our advisors. 
The primary function of the Research Associate position is to support the overall activities of the Managed Assets Research department. We are looking for an associate with an organic interest in the financial markets and investments, who is motivated by the prospect of playing a vital role in delivering high level of service and quality investment solutions to our advisors and their clients. 
Qualifications:
• Bachelor’s degree in economics, business, marketing or related field required. 
• Series 7 & 65/66, or willingness to obtain.
• Progress toward, or desire to pursue, the CFA or CAIA charter designation also viewed positively.
• 2+ years of experience in the financial services industry, preferably in an environment of working with financial advisors.
• Strong writing and proofreading skills.
• Proficiency in Microsoft Office applications, especially Excel, Word and PowerPoint. Experience with Adobe Acrobat, and/or Morningstar Direct a significant plus.
• Strong communication skills, written and verbal, with excellent organizational skills.
• Articulate, confident, driven professional with the ability to work well independently, and as part of a cohesive team in a fast-paced environment. 
Duties:
• Provide research and marketing support, as needed, to the department’s director and broader team.
• Assist in the continued development of the research team’s communications process and procedures.  This consists of, but is not limited to:
o Proofreading/editing text, creating supporting charts & tables, and ultimately distributing presentations and reports through different mediums.
o Maintaining and developing investment reports and note templates.
o Maintain and continue to develop the Managed Assets Research intranet site, internal department databases, and other communications. 
o Keeping organized files for our internal and external publications and other department activities, as appropriate.
• Respond to frequent ad hoc research requests from financial advisors, typically by phone and/or email.
• Assist the research team in client portfolio reviews and proposals. Recommendations in these reviews and proposals often utilize the team’s asset allocation portfolios and selections from our Supervised and Approved Research lists for Mutual Funds, ETF’s and SMA’s. 
What we offer:
Competitive salary plus excellent benefits and perks including, but not limited to:  
•Medical, Dental and Vision
•Company 401(k)and ESOP contribution 
•Generous sick, vacation, and maternity/parental leave
•Paid holidays
•Professional Development Opportunities
•Tuition Reimbursement ($15,000 lifetime cap)
•Discounted personal insurance including home, auto and recreational vehicles
•Charitable gift-matching program
•Davidson Day of Giving – Our tradition of positively impacting communities in which we live and work.  
The potential base pay hiring range for this role is $90,000 – 110,000 annually. The compensation offered will be determined on a case-by-case basis considering a variety of factors including, but not limited to, the skills, relevant work experience, and geographic location of each specific candidate.
D.A. Davidson has been in business for over 90 years! As an employee-owned company, our success is driven by our high standards of business ethics, integrity and the belief that our Associates are our most important assets.  We hope you will consider joining us!

jobs.description:
D.A. Davidson Companies is an independent, employee-owned company with a rich history spanning 90 years. We are dedicated to conducting our business in accordance with the highest standards of integrity and ethics, and delivering outstanding service to our clients and each other. We support a friendly, open and supportive culture, and encourage candid communication and productive engagement that make our companies and each of us better. We are seeking a highly motivated and experienced Vice President, Fixed Income Trader with deep expertise in taxable bonds to join our institutional trading desk. This role has a primary emphasis on corporate bond trading, while also requiring a foundational understanding of mortgage-backed securities (MBS) and agency securities. The ideal candidate will possess a strong grasp of credit markets, trading strategies, and risk management, and will contribute meaningfully to trade execution, idea generation, and overall desk profitability.

jobs.title: Analyst/Junior Associate, Credit Sales & Trading
jobs.description:
Seaport Global is seeking to hire an Analyst / Junior Associate to support its Asia credit sales and trading desk in Singapore. This role is designed for a recent graduate or early-career professional who is motivated to build a career in fixed income markets and develop into a front-office revenue-generating role over time. The successful candidate will work closely with senior sales and trading professionals across investment grade, high yield, and related credit products.

Responsibilities:

  • Assist the credit sales and trading desk with day-to-day market coverage, pricing, and monitoring of secondary market activity.
  • Support the preparation of daily market commentary, issuer updates, and trade ideas for internal and client use.

  • Respond to client inquiries and assist with execution support, trade follow-ups, and post-trade processes.
  • Conduct fundamental credit analysis, including review of financial statements, capital structures, and event risk.
  • Work closely with sales, trading, research, and syndicate teams to support client engagement and execution.

Qualifications:

  • Recent graduate or up to 2 years of relevant experience in finance, markets, trading, sales, or a related field.
  • Bachelor’s degree in finance, economics, business, or related area of study. 
  • Proficiency in Excel and comfort working with market data; familiarity with Bloomberg is a plus.
  • Strong interest in fixed income and credit markets, with a desire to pursue a front-office career.
  • Self-motivated, disciplined, and able to perform in a fast-paced market environment.
  • Solid analytical and quantitative skills with strong attention to detail.
  • Strong written and verbal communication skills.

jobs.title: Investment Research Associate
jobs.description:
Seaport Research Partners Overview
Seaport Research Partners (“SRP”) is a rapidly expanding, innovative sell -side research platform. SRP empowers self-driven analysts/strategists by reshaping economics and delivering a fully integrated solution to support, enhance and grow their franchise, allowing them to focus exclusively on producing the customized and differentiated research that matters most to clients. Seaport’s platform is increasingly attracting top analysts and buy-side customers alike.
Twenty-four (24) senior analysts/strategists have joined the platform and bring an average of 20 years’ experience, most with prior experience at bulge bracket firms and many receiving industry recognition such as II rankings.
SRP operates as a division of Seaport Global Securities, which brings established global client relationships, access to over 4,000 opened institutional accounts, 210 senior salespeople & traders across the cap structure, asset management, global equity and debt capital markets, a full suite of corporate access services including multi-sector and niche conferences, fixed income & derivatives research and investment banking. Seaport Global’s offices throughout the US as well as London provide a superior network and deep infrastructure.
Opportunity
We are looking for investment associates to join our equity research team. This will be a remote, work from home opportunity. 

Qualifications:

  • Minimum of one to five years’ work experience in either investment research, banking, accounting or consulting. 
  • Majors in computer science, engineering, finance or accounting preferred.
  • Strong quantitative and excel skills.
  • Python or other programming skills and experience. 
  • Ability to work in a team environment and help problem solve.  
  • Entrepreneurial mindset with a commitment to excellence and contributing to the team’s success.
  • Ability to work independently in a fast-paced team environment.
  • Strong communication, writing, and presentation skills.

jobs.title: Research Analyst
jobs.description:
Penn Capital, a majority owned subsidiary of Seaport Global and a SEC registered investment adviser and manager, seeks a Research Analyst to play a key role within the portfolio team. The Research Analyst follows industry level developments and performs security level analysis to provide buy and sell recommendations regarding securities in all Penn Capital portfolios – credit and equity products. The Research Analyst collaborates with each product’s Portfolio Manager implementing the investment process.

Responsibilities

  • Provide buy recommendations for each product utilizing credit and equity market analysis.  The research process utilizes a combination of quantitative analysis (such as financial statement analysis and cash flow modeling) and qualitative analysis (such as interviewing industry management teams and understanding industry dynamics).  
  • Utilize credit market analysis such as cash flow modeling, liquidity analysis and covenant review to provide a Penn Risk Recommendation (PRR) independent of credit rating agency opinion.  
  • Perform equity market analysis that utilizes credit market analysis as well as the identification of fundamental catalysts, Capital Structure Catalysts and cash flow modeling.  
  • Demonstrate strong analytical skills, sound investment judgement, strong interpersonal skills, and a high level of energy and motivation.

Qualifications:

  • Bachelor’s Degree in Finance, Marketing, Business, or a related field.
  • Masters of Business Administration with a focus on Investment Management or Investment Banking preferred.
  • Chartered Financial Analyst (CFA) designation preferred.
  • Minimum 5 years of professional investment experience, preferably in public credit and/or equity markets.
  • Minimum 5 years of experience working within a “buyside” investment advisor preferred.
  • Demonstrated ability to effectively pitch credit and/or equity ideas to portfolio managers. 
  • Excellent communication skills including verbal, written and presentation; ability to interact and build relationships with consultants and clients
  • Ability to work as a collaborative team player, treating colleagues with the highest level of respect and professionalism.
  • Excellent Bloomberg and Microsoft Office (Excel, Microsoft Word, PowerPoint) skills.
  • Flexibility for business travel.

jobs.title: Analyst / Associate - Capital Solutions - New York
jobs.description:
Firm Profile

Ducera Partners is a leading, independent investment banking and strategic advisory firm. We take a collaborative, partnership-based approach to deliver superior results to our clients across a variety of advisory verticals, including mergers and acquisitions, strategic advisory, restructuring and liability management, capital solutions, and risk solutions. Ducera leverages its extensive experience with an execution-focused approach to advise clients through pivotal moments where judgment, creativity, and precision are critical. Founded in 2015, Ducera has advised on over $925 billion in transactions across a variety of industries and has offices in New York, Los Angeles, San Francisco, and Stamford.

Responsibilities

The Capital Solutions group advises companies as they navigate complex capital markets decisions. Clients rely on Ducera to structure and execute tailored financing solutions across credit, equity, structured equity, and hybrid capital. Drawing on deep transaction experience and an extensive global investor network, we help clients evaluate all available alternatives and execute optimal outcomes. Ducera has advised corporate and sponsor clients on a wide range of transactions, including acquisition financings, dividend recapitalizations, stressed and special situations financings, project finance, structured products, growth capital raises, and minority equity investments. Our integrated approach to structuring, capital provider access, and execution has made Ducera a trusted partner for clients’ capital needs.

A Senior Analyst / Junior Associate on our Capital Solutions team is expected to:

  • Play an integral role in the origination and execution of financing mandates, including primary capital raises, refinancings, recapitalizations, and merger and acquisition financings, while working closely with senior bankers, clients, and investors.
  • Draft and coordinate marketing materials to drive origination and execution efforts, including client pitches, capital markets updates, confidential information memoranda, investor presentations, and other ad-hoc client-facing materials.
  • Build, refine, and maintain detailed financial models and credit analyses used directly in client materials, lender diligence, and financing negotiations.
  • Prepare and interpret company and transaction comparables to assess market pricing, leverage tolerance, covenant structures, and investor appetite.
  • Perform fundamental credit analysis, including assessment of business risk, cash flow sustainability, leverage and coverage metrics, covenant headroom, and downside scenarios.
  • Manage day-to-day transaction execution workstreams, including data room setup and maintenance, diligence coordination, Q&A tracking, and interaction with lenders, investors, and other counterparties.
  • Support investor outreach and capital formation efforts by maintaining investor databases, tracking feedback and indications of interest, and assisting with capital allocation analyses.
  • Monitor financing market conditions, comparable transactions, and investor activity to inform financing strategies and client recommendations.
  • Maintain internal transaction and pipeline tracking tools, ensuring accuracy and organization in a fast-paced, multi-mandate environment.
  • Over time, take an active role in covering clients (financial sponsors, public companies, private corporates) and investors (alternative asset managers, direct lenders, credit hedge funds, etc.).
  • Work closely with senior bankers and clients in a lean team setting, demonstrating ownership, judgment, and professionalism while managing multiple priorities and deadlines.

Qualifications

Qualified applicants will prove to be highly motivated and team-oriented with excellent leadership records. The successful candidate will demonstrate a strong background in financial modeling, finance, and accounting as well as possess excellent verbal and written communication skills, outstanding project management skills, and exceptional attention to detail. Additional qualifications include but are not limited to:

  • Strong technical and corporate finance foundation, including detailed financial statement analysis, cash flow modeling, and the development of fully integrated financial projection models to support financing transactions.
  • Intermediate working knowledge of the leveraged finance ecosystem, including leveraged loans, high yield bonds, and private credit, with a solid understanding of various capital structures, financing alternatives, and interpretation of credit agreements / indentures.
  • Demonstrated proficiency in preparing and analyzing company and transaction comparables across leveraged loan, high yield, and private credit markets to assess pricing, structure, and market receptivity.
  • Fundamental credit underwriting capabilities, including evaluation of business and industry risk, cash flow sustainability, leverage and coverage metrics, covenant headroom, and downside sensitivities.
  • Experience supporting and executing capital markets and financing transaction processes, including preparation of lender and investor marketing materials, management presentations, data room coordination, and investor Q&A workflows.
  • Strong organizational and process-management skills, with the ability to utilize technology and data tools to build and maintain financing databases, investor and lender relationships, market comps, and transaction pipeline tracking.
  • Highly collaborative and team-oriented professional with a strong work ethic, attention to detail, and the maturity to interface directly with clients, investors, and senior deal bankers.
  • Proactive self-starter with an entrepreneurial spirit that is comfortable operating within a lean organization and a desire to play an integral role in business development efforts / strategies.
  • Completion of a profile on Suited to further assist in an evaluation of your candidacy: Creating a Suited Profile

Additional Information

To be considered, candidates must hold a bachelor’s degree (or foreign equivalent) and extensive knowledge of and background in capital markets.

Expected annualized base salary range of $125,000 – 200,000.

The annual base salary range reflects the low and high targets of the estimated base salary range for this position. The actual base salary an employee in this role receives may vary depending on a variety of factors, including but not limited to work location, job-related knowledge, and prior experience. The range listed reflects base salary only, and the total compensation package may include other components.

Ducera Partners is an equal opportunity employer.  We do not discriminate on the basis of, and will consider all qualified applicants for employment without regard to race, color, religious creed, religion, sex, pregnancy, national origin, ancestry, citizenship status, age, martial or partnership status, sexual orientation, gender identity expression, disability, medical condition, genetic information or predisposition, veteran or military status, status as a victim of domestic violence, a sex offense or stalking, or any other category protected by law. Ducera Partners also complies with all applicable laws with regard to providing reasonable accommodation of disabilities to applicants.

Investment Banking Candidates: Ducera Partners uses the Suited Assessment as part of our candidate evaluation and recruitment process to expand our consideration of candidates beyond traditional hiring metrics. Information on what is measured by the assessment, Suited’s data retention policies, and how to request a reasonable accommodation may be found here: Resource Guide for Investment Banking Candidates. Completing the Suited assessment is a mandatory part of our recruiting process; however, should you believe that even with appropriate reasonable accommodations enabled you require an alternative selection procedure, please email us at recruiting@ducerapartners.com. To see the results of our AEDT bias audit required under New York City Local Law 144, please click here:

jobs.title: Analyst - Investment Banking - Los Angeles
jobs.description:
Firm Profile

Ducera Partners is a leading, independent investment banking and strategic advisory firm. We take a collaborative, partnership-based approach to deliver superior results to our clients across a variety of advisory verticals, including mergers and acquisitions, strategic advisory, restructuring and liability management, capital solutions, and risk solutions. Ducera leverages its extensive experience with an execution-focused approach to advise clients through pivotal moments where judgment, creativity, and precision are critical. Founded in 2015, Ducera has advised on over $925 billion in transactions across a variety of industries and has offices in New York, Los Angeles, San Francisco, and Stamford.

Role Overview

Ducera Partners is seeking to expand its team in its Los Angeles office with investment banking team members to assist in deal execution and commercial activities across the broad range of products Ducera offers.  Assignments may include mergers & acquisitions, strategic advisory, restructuring, liability management, capital markets, and growth capital, and frequently involve exposure to new issue advisory services (including ABS related issuances), strategic advisory assignments associated with contingent financial risk and natural disaster, government and public-sector clients.

Team members can expect exposure to assignments focused on the western market, integration on assignments with teams in Los Angeles (primary), San Francisco and New York, and activity driven exposure to advisory opportunities in topical industries and products. Recent power, utility, government and public policy assignments have included advising elected officials and regulators on wildfires/natural disasters, liability frameworks and capital markets implications, and new issuance advisory assignments including achieving the lowest cost on new security issuances.  This role provides experienced investment banking professionals the opportunity to operate at the intersection of corporate finance, strategic advisory, public policy, and capital markets, applying rigorous financial analysis and transaction execution skills to assignments.

Responsibilities

Team members are expected to work on a wide range of assignments across active products and industries (including those identified above). Work will include, but is not limited to:

  • Working closely with senior bankers and clients in a lean team setting, demonstrating initiative, ownership, judgment, and professionalism while managing multiple priorities and deadlines.
  • Coordinating complex, time-sensitive workstreams across internal teams and external parties, including clients, legal advisors, and other transaction participants.
  • Actively participating in transaction execution, developing and reviewing financial analyses and models, developing and reviewing transaction structures, financing alternatives, implementation paths, and scenario analyses as well as supporting senior bankers in client-facing interactions, commercial activities and deal execution.
  • Preparing, reviewing, and presenting client materials, including pitch books, strategic advisory presentations, transaction presentations, recommendations, and review of capital markets, ratings and credit perspectives to support discussions with clients and other stakeholders. Work involves developing and presenting perspectives, informed by reviewing substantial amounts of content, third party perspectives and recommendations, legislative and regulatory documents, credit and equity research, market reference points and internal experience.
  • Monitoring, tracking, interpreting and informing team on market activity and industry activity, including tracking and maintaining database of comparable transactions, fundamental credit and cost of capital implications, alternative approaches to execution, investor lists, and developments which will inform strategies, future activity, and client recommendations.
  • Maintain internal transaction and pipeline tracking tools, ensuring accuracy and organization in a fast-paced, multi-mandate environment, tracking news, legislative and regulatory activity and monitoring for potential opportunities. Over time, take an active role in covering clients and developing business development efforts.
  • Manage day-to-day transaction execution workstreams, including senior banker and clients requests and analysis on time with strong attention to detail.

Qualifications

  • Ducera Partners seeks to hire individuals who are highly motivated, self-starting, team-oriented, intelligent and have excellent academic, leadership and extracurricular records. Successful candidates will demonstrate a strong background in finance, accounting, economics as well as possess excellent verbal and written communication skills, outstanding project management skills, and exceptional attention to detail. Additional qualifications include but are not limited to:

    • Experience in investment banking with strong technical and corporate finance foundation.
    • Strong organizational and process-management skills, with the ability to utilize technology and data tools to build and maintain databases, investor and lender relationships, market comps, and transaction pipeline tracking.
    • Demonstrated proficiency in preparing client presentations and analyzing company and transaction comparables.
    • Highly collaborative and team-oriented professional with a strong work ethic, attention to detail, and the maturity to interface directly with clients, investors, and senior deal bankers.
    • Proactive self-starter with an entrepreneurial spirit that is comfortable operating within a lean organization and a desire to play an integral role in business development efforts / strategies across topical industries and products.
    • Completion of a profile on Suited to further assist in an evaluation of your candidacy: Creating a Suited Profile

Expected annual base salary range: $110,000 - $140,000

The annual base salary range reflects the low and high targets of the estimated base salary range for this position. jobs.title: Associate - Investment Banking - Los Angeles
jobs.description:
Firm Profile

Ducera Partners is a leading, independent investment banking and strategic advisory firm. We take a collaborative, partnership-based approach to deliver superior results to our clients across a variety of advisory verticals, including mergers and acquisitions, strategic advisory, restructuring and liability management, capital solutions, and risk solutions. Ducera leverages its extensive experience with an execution-focused approach to advise clients through pivotal moments where judgment, creativity, and precision are critical. Founded in 2015, Ducera has advised on over $925 billion in transactions across a variety of industries and has offices in New York, Los Angeles, San Francisco, and Stamford.

Role Overview

Ducera Partners is seeking to expand its team in its Los Angeles office with investment banking team members to assist in deal execution and commercial activities across the broad range of products Ducera offers.  Assignments may include mergers & acquisitions, strategic advisory, restructuring, liability management, capital markets, and growth capital, and frequently involve exposure to new issue advisory services (including ABS related issuances), strategic advisory assignments associated with contingent financial risk and natural disaster, government and public-sector clients.

Team members can expect exposure to assignments focused on the western market, integration on assignments with teams in Los Angeles (primary), San Francisco and New York, and activity driven exposure to advisory opportunities in topical industries and products. Recent power, utility, government and public policy assignments have included advising elected officials and regulators on wildfires/natural disasters, liability frameworks and capital markets implications, and new issuance advisory assignments including achieving the lowest cost on new security issuances.  This role provides experienced investment banking professionals the opportunity to operate at the intersection of corporate finance, strategic advisory, public policy, and capital markets, applying rigorous financial analysis and transaction execution skills to assignments.

Responsibilities

Team members are expected to work on a wide range of assignments across active products and industries (including those identified above). Work will include, but is not limited to:

  • Working closely with senior bankers and clients in a lean team setting, demonstrating initiative, ownership, judgment, and professionalism while managing multiple priorities and deadlines.
  • Coordinating complex, time-sensitive workstreams across internal teams and external parties, including clients, legal advisors, and other transaction participants.
  • Actively participating in transaction execution, developing and reviewing financial analyses and models, developing and reviewing transaction structures, financing alternatives, implementation paths, and scenario analyses as well as supporting senior bankers in client-facing interactions, commercial activities and deal execution.
  • Preparing, reviewing, and presenting client materials, including pitch books, strategic advisory presentations, transaction presentations, recommendations, and review of capital markets, ratings and credit perspectives to support discussions with clients and other stakeholders. Work involves developing and presenting perspectives, informed by reviewing substantial amounts of content, third party perspectives and recommendations, legislative and regulatory documents, credit and equity research, market reference points and internal experience.
  • Monitoring, tracking, interpreting and informing team on market activity and industry activity, including tracking and maintaining database of comparable transactions, fundamental credit and cost of capital implications, alternative approaches to execution, investor lists, and developments which will inform strategies, future activity, and client recommendations.
  • Maintain internal transaction and pipeline tracking tools, ensuring accuracy and organization in a fast-paced, multi-mandate environment, tracking news, legislative and regulatory activity and monitoring for potential opportunities. Over time, take an active role in covering clients and developing business development efforts.
  • Manage day-to-day transaction execution workstreams, including senior banker and clients requests and analysis on time with strong attention to detail.
  • Providing mentorship, training, and guidance to junior team members.

Qualifications

Ducera Partners seeks to hire individuals who are highly motivated, self-starting, team-oriented, intelligent and have excellent academic, leadership and extracurricular records. Successful candidates will demonstrate a strong background in finance, accounting, economics as well as possess excellent verbal and written communication skills, outstanding project management skills, and exceptional attention to detail. Additional qualifications include but are not limited to:

  • Experience in investment banking with strong technical and corporate finance foundation.
  • Strong organizational and process-management skills, with the ability to utilize technology and data tools to build and maintain databases, investor and lender relationships, market comps, and transaction pipeline tracking.
  • Demonstrated proficiency in preparing client presentations and analyzing company and transaction comparables.
  • Highly collaborative and team-oriented professional with a strong work ethic, attention to detail, and the maturity to interface directly with clients, investors, and senior deal bankers.
  • Proactive self-starter with an entrepreneurial spirit that is comfortable operating within a lean organization and a desire to play an integral role in business development efforts / strategies across topical industries and products.
  • Completion of a profile on Suited to further assist in an evaluation of your candidacy: Creating a Suited Profile

Additional Information

Expected annualized base salary range: $200,000 – $250,000.

The annual base salary range reflects the low and high targets of the estimated base salary range for this position. The actual base salary an employee in this role receives may vary depending on a variety of factors, including but not limited to: work location, job-related knowledge, and prior experience. The range listed reflects base salary only, and the total compensation package may include other components.

Ducera Partners is an equal opportunity employer.  We do not discriminate on the basis of, and will consider all qualified applicants for employment without regard to race, color, religious creed, religion, sex, pregnancy, national origin, ancestry, citizenship status, age, martial or partnership status, sexual orientation, gender identity expression, disability, medical condition, genetic information or predisposition, veteran or military status, status as a victim of domestic violence, a sex offense or stalking, or any other category protected by law. jobs.title: Associate - Mergers & Acquisitions - New York
jobs.description:
Firm Profile

Ducera Partners is a leading, independent investment banking and strategic advisory firm. We take a collaborative, partnership-based approach to deliver superior results to our clients across a variety of advisory verticals, including mergers and acquisitions, strategic advisory, restructuring and liability management, capital solutions, and risk solutions. Ducera leverages its extensive experience with an execution-focused approach to advise clients through pivotal moments where judgment, creativity, and precision are critical. Founded in 2015, Ducera has advised on over $925 billion in transactions across a variety of industries and has offices in New York, Los Angeles, San Francisco, and Stamford.

Responsibilities

Ducera advises clients in a variety of complex and transformative transactions. Our professionals, with roots in complex corporate finance, offer independent advice to stakeholders in a broad range of situations. Our services include advice related to buy- and sell-side advisory; fairness opinions and special committee representation; merger advisory; cross-border advisory; takeover defense and shareholder advisory; and divestitures, asset sales, and spin-offs.

As an Associate, you will be involved in the development, structuring and financing of a wide range of transactions, and will regularly attend internal and client meetings, negotiations and due diligence sessions.

Associate work will include, but is not limited to:

  • Producing analysis and models on leveraged buyouts, discounted cash flows, mergers and acquisitions accretion and dilution, and multiple valuation.
  • Preparing memoranda for internal and external use.
  • Analyzing financial data, and conducting research of targeted industries.
  • Assisting in the execution and coordination of complex transactions and time-sensitive processes, including due diligence and valuation analysis.
  • Drafting client marketing and pitch materials.
  • Maintaining active involvement and participation in client facing situations and negotiations.
  • Providing mentorship and development to junior staff.

Qualifications

Qualified applicants will prove to be highly motivated and team-oriented with excellent leadership records. The successful candidate will demonstrate a strong background in financial modeling, finance, and accounting as well as possess excellent verbal and written communication skills, outstanding project management skills, and exceptional attention to detail.

In addition, qualified candidates will possess the following:

  • Bachelor’s degree (or foreign equivalent)
  • Knowledge of and experience in sell-side mergers and acquisitions
  • Completion of a profile on Suited to further assist in an evaluation of your candidacy: Creating a Suited Profile

Additional Information

Expected annualized base salary range: $200,000 – $250,000

The successful candidate will demonstrate a strong background in financial modeling, finance, and accounting as well as possess excellent verbal and written communication skills, outstanding project management skills, and exceptional attention to detail.

In addition, qualified candidates will possess the following:

  • Bachelor’s degree (or foreign equivalent)
  • Extensive knowledge of and background in investment banking
  • Completion of a profile on Suited to further assist in an evaluation of your candidacy: Creating a Suited Profile

Additional Information

Expected annualized base salary range of $250,000 - 275,000.

The annual base salary range reflects the low and high targets of the estimated base salary range for this position. The actual base salary an employee in this role receives may vary depending on a variety of factors, including but not limited to: work location, job-related knowledge, and prior experience. The range listed reflects base salary only, and the total compensation package may include other components.

jobs.title: Restructuring Director
jobs.description:
Firm Profile

Ducera Partners is a leading, independent investment banking and strategic advisory firm. We take a collaborative, partnership-based approach to deliver superior results to our clients across a variety of advisory verticals, including mergers and acquisitions, strategic advisory, restructuring and liability management, capital solutions, and risk solutions. Ducera leverages its extensive experience with an execution-focused approach to advise clients through pivotal moments where judgment, creativity, and precision are critical. Founded in 2015, Ducera has advised on over $925 billion in transactions across a variety of industries and has offices in New York, Los Angeles, San Francisco, and Stamford.

Responsibilities

As a Director, you will work on a wide range of assignments playing a central role executing complex financial restructuring and liability management transactions. The Director-level position requires 5+ years as an Investment Banking Analyst, Associate, or closely related role .

Director work will include, but is not limited to:

  • Lead day-to-day execution of restructuring mandates, including Chapter 11 filings, out-of-court restructurings, distressed M&A, liability management transactions, and financings;
  • Oversee preparation of financial models, business valuations, and other financial analyses;
  • Support Managing Directors and Partners in structuring transactions and presenting recommendations to boards, creditors, and stakeholders;
  • Review and refine pitch books, term sheets, marketing materials, and other client presentations;
  • Support business development efforts by participating in client meetings, preparing marketing materials, and maintaining industry relationships;
  • Lead coordination efforts amongst various stakeholders during live engagements;
  • Manage junior bankers (analysts and associates) across multiple assignments, ensuring high-quality deliverables and professional development

Qualifications

To be considered, candidates must hold a bachelor’s degree (or foreign equivalent) in Finance, Mathematics, Statistics, or closely related field.

In addition, qualified candidates must have 5 years’ experience with Bloomberg, FactSet, Microsoft Excel, Microsoft PowerPoint, Microsoft Word, Microsoft Outlook, Python, Adobe.

Expected annualized base salary range of $275,000.

Ducera Partners is an equal opportunity employer.  We do not discriminate on the basis of, and will consider all qualified applicants for employment without regard to race, color, religious creed, religion, sex, pregnancy, national origin, ancestry, citizenship status, age, martial or partnership status, sexual orientation, gender identity expression, disability, medical condition, genetic information or predisposition, veteran or military status, status as a victim of domestic violence, a sex offense or stalking, or any other category protected by law. jobs.title: Analyst or Associate, Wealth Management (Internal Sales)
jobs.description:
Adams Street Partners (“Adams Street”) is a global private markets investment manager that has $65 billion in assets under management. The firm is 100% employee-owned, which fosters a positive and unique culture of collaboration, intellectual rigor, integrity, and a commitment to professional development. For the eighth year in a row, Adams Street was named one of Pension & Investments’ Best Places to Work in Money Management in 2025. 

Employees operate as a single global team, integrating the expertise of the firm’s investment professionals across five strategies: primary investments, secondary investments, growth equity, private credit, and co-investments.  Adams Street is considered a private markets pioneer and was inducted into the Private Equity Hall of Fame in 2000.

The firm was founded more than 50 years ago, and clients include leading public and corporate pension plans, foundations, endowments, insurance companies, registered investment advisors, and high net worth individuals worldwide.

Adams Street has offices in Abu Dhabi, Austin, Beijing, Boston, Chicago, Hong Kong, London, Munich, Menlo Park, New York, Seoul, Singapore, Sydney, Tokyo and Toronto.

Overview:

Adams Street Partners is actively looking to expand its Wealth Management team to satisfy the needs of our growing client base. The position will be based out of our New York office. This “client focused” individual will not only be a central resource and relationship manager for their assigned territory but will actively engage with prospective clients through cold outreach. This position will provide a superior level of service to our prospective and existing investors across a diverse mix of private wealth channels including private banks, wirehouses, independent broker dealers, RIAs, and family offices. This person will assist in managing distribution relationships by facilitating business production, creative selling, and problem resolution. Given Adams Street’s collaborative culture, this role requires exceptional organizational and client servicing skills, with the ability to successfully manage multiple fundraising initiatives and other priorities to support a growing business.  

Responsibilities include, but are not limited to:

  • Generating new leads and prospecting accounts through cold/warm calling, emailing, LinkedIn, etc. daily
  • Working in sync alongside external partner(s) to help schedule both in-person and virtual meetings throughout their respective territory, in addition to providing continued support on prospect follow-up throughout the sales cycle
  • Servicing existing clients with impromptu needs and being readily available to handle time sensitive inquiries
  • Building and maintaining strong relationships with team members to establish and achieve sales targets.
  • Utilizing SalesForce CRM for all interactions that lead to reports on sales activity and production as required
  • Actively reviewing the team’s opportunity pipeline to accurately reflect the latest developments across our prospective and client accounts
  • Maintaining successful relationships with colleagues including but not limited to the broader investor relations team, investment teams, product strategy, client operations, marketing and investment strategy and risk management.
  • Developing and maintaining a strong knowledge base of Adams Street product offerings, industry trends, competitive landscape, and the universe of prospective clients across assigned regions
  • Consistently looking for “value adds” through service and selling ideas
  • Preparing and delivering customized client-facing materials in response to prospective and existing client requests

Requirements:

We seek an individual who is hungry, organized, self-motivated, detail-oriented, and demonstrates creative thinking. Candidates will have the following characteristics:

  • Bachelor’s degree, ideally with a business focus. CFA/CAIA preferred
  • 1-4 years’ experience as an internal salesperson
  • Knowledgeable about private market investment strategies and genuinely curious about public/private market news
  • Strong phone and interpersonal communication skills (verbal and written)
  • Self-starter who loves to go above and beyond expectations
  • Possesses the ability to plan, organize, develop, and deliver effective plans / presentations to a targeted audience
  • Knowledge of HNW/UHNW investors and/or family offices preferred

We offer a competitive U.S. benefits package designed to support health, financial security, and work-life balance. Highlights include medical, dental, and vision coverage; a 401(k) with immediate vesting and employer match; profit sharing; flexible spending accounts (FSA); and paid parental leave, including adoption. Employees also receive generous paid time off, commuter benefits, employer-paid short- and long-term disability and life insurance, and access to additional voluntary benefits.

NYC Pay Range
$85,000—$125,000 USD

jobs.title: Associate, Investor Relations
jobs.description:
Adams Street Partners (“Adams Street”) is a global private markets investment manager that has $65 billion in assets under management. The firm is 100% employee-owned, which fosters a positive and unique culture of collaboration, intellectual rigor, integrity, and a commitment to professional development. For the eighth year in a row, Adams Street was named one of Pension & Investments’ Best Places to Work in Money Management in 2025. 

Employees operate as a single global team, integrating the expertise of the firm’s investment professionals across five strategies: primary investments, secondary investments, growth equity, private credit, and co-investments.  Adams Street is considered a private markets pioneer and was inducted into the Private Equity Hall of Fame in 2000.

The firm was founded more than 50 years ago, and clients include leading public and corporate pension plans, foundations, endowments, insurance companies, registered investment advisors, and high net worth individuals worldwide.

Adams Street has offices in Abu Dhabi, Austin, Beijing, Boston, Chicago, Hong Kong, London, Munich, Menlo Park, New York, Seoul, Singapore, Sydney, Tokyo and Toronto.

Responsibilities:

Adams Street Partners is seeking an Investor Relations Associate to join our Investor Relations team, based in Singapore or Hong Kong. This individual will work closely with the Investor Relations, Marketing, Product Strategy, and Investment teams and will be responsible for conducting research, managing and analyzing data, and coordinating the execution of appropriate follow-up activities with prospective and existing clients across Asia, who have committed a total of $17 billion.

This position offers broad exposure to the firm’s strategy and investment capabilities. The ideal candidate is interested in building a long-term career in fundraising and client service, supporting the Singapore and Hong Kong Investor Relations teams, with a focus on Southeast Asia and Greater China. The successful candidate will demonstrate enthusiasm, intellectual curiosity, and a strong desire to learn the business.

Specifically, this individual will be involved and responsible for:

  • Manage client support activities, including due diligence coordination, onboarding processes, preparation of RFI/RFP responses, investor presentations, reporting materials, modeling support, and ad hoc client requests.
  • Collaborate cross-functionally with Investor Relations, Marketing, Product Strategy, and Investment teams to develop customized communications, marketing collateral, and targeted client materials.
  • Conduct market and prospect research across the region, maintain competitive intelligence systems, and manage tracking databases to identify prospective clients and monitor investment discussions.
  • Support the development of compelling and accurate written content that articulates Adams Street Partners’ capabilities, investment processes, and thought leadership.
  • Develop and maintain internal processes and procedures to ensure consistency, integrity, timeliness, and quality of communications to prospective and existing clients.
  • Lead and execute special projects related to client portfolios and firmwide strategic initiatives.
  • Identify and implement process improvements to enhance marketing efficiency and operational effectiveness.

Qualifications:

  • Undergraduate degree required; business or marketing focus preferred.
  • Relevant experience within a professional services environment, preferably in investment management, consulting, or investment banking, supporting research, analytics, or data management initiatives.
  • Strong project management capabilities with exceptional attention to detail, ability to prioritize effectively, manage multiple workstreams, and deliver under deadline pressure.
  • Excellent written and verbal communication skills, with demonstrated ability to synthesize technical and financial information into clear, accurate, and well-structured materials.
  • Working knowledge of private markets and the institutional investor and private wealth/banking landscape; demonstrated interest in staying current on industry and investor relations trends.
  • High proficiency in CRM and proprietary database systems (ideally Salesforce) and expert command of the MS Office Suite.
  • Strategic mindset with the ability to operate independently while exercising sound judgment.
  • Fluent in written and spoken English; proficiency in an Asian language preferred.

jobs.title: Vice President, Investor Relations (Consultant Relations)
jobs.description:
Adams Street Partners (“Adams Street”) is a global private markets investment manager that has $65 billion in assets under management. The firm is 100% employee-owned, which fosters a positive and unique culture of collaboration, intellectual rigor, integrity, and a commitment to professional development. For the eighth year in a row, Adams Street was named one of Pension & Investments’ Best Places to Work in Money Management in 2025. 

Employees operate as a single global team, integrating the expertise of the firm’s investment professionals across five strategies: primary investments, secondary investments, growth equity, private credit, and co-investments.  Adams Street is considered a private markets pioneer and was inducted into the Private Equity Hall of Fame in 2000.

The firm was founded more than 50 years ago, and clients include leading public and corporate pension plans, foundations, endowments, insurance companies, registered investment advisors, and high net worth individuals worldwide.

Adams Street has offices in Abu Dhabi, Austin, Beijing, Boston, Chicago, Hong Kong, London, Munich, Menlo Park, New York, Seoul, Singapore, Sydney, Tokyo and Toronto.

Reporting to the Global Head of Consultant Relations, the primary role of the Vice President, Investor Relations is to place Adams Street Partners in a position to significantly grow its business through the development and enhancement of existing investment consultant relationships with institutional consulting firms headquartered in the East Coast and parts of the Mid-Western US.

KEY RESPONSIBILITIES

  • Represent Adams Street Partners' full product line and develop and maintain world-class relationships with research, field consultants, and senior executives at investment consulting firms within assigned geography
  • Maintain a thorough understanding of assigned investment consulting firms, including their business strategy and competitors, client roster, key decision-makers/influencers and their biases, manager selection process, and communication preferences
  • Generate buy-ratings and effectively position the firm’s investment strategies and services
  • Proactively identify business development opportunities within a consultant’s client roster and coordinate with relevant institutional sales colleagues to target and close the opportunities
  • Leverage communication opportunities with clients and prospects to further Adams Street Partners' brand by representing the firm as a consultative thought leader, presenter/speaker, and contributor to content pieces.
  • Identify opportunities to enhance the consultant relationship through the introduction of relevant investment professionals and timely thought leadership
  • Consistently document interaction with consultants in the firm’s CRM system
  • Cultivate strong internal relationships and stay current on firm strategy, investments, and competitive landscape.

REQUIREMENTS

  • Bachelor’s degree. MBA and/or CFA will be viewed favorably
  • A demonstrable track record of raising assets with consultants, institutional investors, wealth channel
  • Solid private markets experience is strongly preferred
  • Strong understanding of assigned investment consulting firms with an ability to understand the perspective of different types of client organizations
  • Proven ability to establish and execute on a sales strategy
  • Strong relationship development and management skills
  • Bring a total portfolio perspective and ability to speak credibly about new investments and developments within clients’ portfolios
  • Highest degree of integrity and professionalism
  • A high level of discipline and focus. A driven, results-oriented individual
  • Unconditionally respectful and courteous towards others, values building relationships with colleagues to support business’s growth initiatives. Balances strength of conviction with collegiality and sensitivity to all stakeholders
  • Strong problem-solver who demonstrates effective organization skills and follow-through
  • Energetic, forward-looking, flexible and adaptable

We offer a competitive U.S. benefits package designed to support health, financial security, and work-life balance. Highlights include medical, dental, and vision coverage; a 401(k) with immediate vesting and employer match; profit sharing; flexible spending accounts (FSA); and paid parental leave, including adoption. Employees also receive generous paid time off, commuter benefits, employer-paid short- and long-term disability and life insurance, and access to additional voluntary benefits.

NYC Pay Range
$175,000—$250,000 USD

jobs.description:
Adams Street Partners (“Adams Street”) is a global private markets investment manager that has $65 billion in assets under management. The firm is 100% employee-owned, which fosters a positive and unique culture of collaboration, intellectual rigor, integrity, and a commitment to professional development. For the eighth year in a row, Adams Street was named one of Pension & Investments’ Best Places to Work in Money Management in 2025. 

Employees operate as a single global team, integrating the expertise of the firm’s investment professionals across five strategies: primary investments, secondary investments, growth equity, private credit, and co-investments.  Adams Street is considered a private markets pioneer and was inducted into the Private Equity Hall of Fame in 2000.

The firm was founded more than 50 years ago, and clients include leading public and corporate pension plans, foundations, endowments, insurance companies, registered investment advisors, and high net worth individuals worldwide.

Adams Street has offices in Abu Dhabi, Austin, Beijing, Boston, Chicago, Hong Kong, London, Munich, Menlo Park, New York, Seoul, Singapore, Sydney, Tokyo and Toronto.

Responsibilities:

Adams Street Partners seeks to recruit an accomplished business development professional to focus on the firm’s fundraising and client service activities in Asia, with a primary focus on Greater China. This role will join the Investor Relations team, based in Hong Kong.

This individual will be instrumental in expanding and diversifying the firm’s investor base in the region, while providing core account management support to prospective and existing clients across Asia, who have committed a total of $17 billion. This individual will work closely with the Investor Relations, Marketing, Product Strategy, and Investment teams and will be responsible for conducting research, managing and analyzing data, and coordinating the execution of appropriate follow-up activities.

A comprehensive understanding of the regional investor base, strong knowledge across alternative investment products, and excellent sales and relationship management skills are required.


Specifically, this individual will be involved and responsible for:

  • Strengthening the delivery of a systematic Asia business development strategy, generating new revenue initiatives through disciplined prospecting and pipeline management.
  •  Develop, broaden, and maintain long-term relationships with institutional, private wealth, and private bank investors, financial institutions, and other market participants.
  • Represent Adams Street’s full product platform, educating clients and prospects on portfolio construction opportunities and appropriate strategy inclusion.
  • Build and manage a qualified investor pipeline through proactive conversion of prospects into committed mandates, leveraging core and customized product solutions.
  • Manage the full client lifecycle, including due diligence processes, annual subscription and closing cycles, investor reporting coordination, and ongoing client servicing.
  • Serve as a consultative advisor to clients on portfolio construction, rebalancing, and private equity program development, leveraging the global IR/client service platform as appropriate.
  • Structure and deliver effective investor presentations and thought leadership initiatives, including conferences, white papers, and spokesperson engagements to strengthen the Adams Street brand.
  • Partner closely with Product Strategy, Investment Strategy, and investment teams to ensure coordinated product distribution and alignment with firm growth objectives.

Qualifications:

  • Strong academic credentials required; advanced degree or professional designation preferred. 
  • Demonstrated track record in sales and account management within a professional services environment, preferably in investment management.
  • High-energy, entrepreneurial business developer with a strategic mindset, strong results orientation, and the ability to operate independently.
  • Deep knowledge of private markets and the institutional, private wealth, and banking investor landscape, with a commitment to staying current on industry and investor relations trends.
  • Exceptional written and verbal communication skills, with the ability to articulate complex alternative investment strategies clearly and persuasively, synthesize technical information, and produce high-quality client-facing materials.
  • Strong project management discipline with exceptional attention to detail and the ability to prioritize, multitask, and execute under deadline pressure.
  • High proficiency in CRM and proprietary database systems (ideally Salesforce) and expert command of the MS Office Suite.
  • Fluent in written and spoken English; proficiency in Cantonese and/or Mandarin preferred.
