jobs.title: Associate, Investment Management
jobs.description:
About Invesco

As one of the world’s leading independent global investment firms, Invesco is dedicated to rethinking possibilities for our clients. By delivering the combined power of our distinctive investment management capabilities, we provide a wide range of investment strategies and vehicles to our clients around the world. If you're looking for challenging work, intelligent colleagues, and exposure across a global footprint, come explore your potential at Invesco.

What’s in it for you?

Our people are at the very core of our success. Invesco employees get more out of life through our comprehensive compensation and benefit offerings including:

  • Flexible paid time off

  • Hybrid work schedule 

  • 401(K) matching of 100% up to the first 6% with a discretionary supplemental contribution 

  • Health & wellbeing benefits 

  • Parental Leave benefits 

  • Employee stock purchase plan

Job Description

The Associate partners with Asset Managers in the management and oversight of various commercial and multifamily real estate investments, with the overall goal of optimizing asset performance and values. The Associate plays an integral role in developing and executing property specific strategies, analyzing major leasing decisions and how they impact value, performing major feasibility studies and evaluating property level performance against relevant industry benchmarks. In addition, the role is responsible for providing support and analysis for the preparation of the quarterly and annual property reports and budgets for a portfolio of assets.

SPECIFIC DUTIES AND RESPONSIBILITIES

  • Work with Asset Manager to create property level operational strategies and analyze major leases, capital projects or other investment factors impacting asset value.

  • Assist in the review of valuation models (Argus and Excel) and take part in the acquisition and disposition process, overseeing the flow of due diligence information for select investments as needed.

  • Prepare financial analysis to track historic portfolio and property level results in comparison to industry benchmarks.

  • Coordinate the development of Annual Business Plans and Quarterly reports by assisting in the preparation of property operating and capital budgets and reviewing and approving on-going financial variance reporting.   

  • Communicate with third party property management and leasing personnel as well as insurance and real estate tax consultants and other third party providers as needed.

  • Review lease submittal packages, evaluate prospective tenant businesses and credit worthiness, and perform other lease related financial analysis as needed.

  • Collect, analyze and synthesize relevant market reports and other lease or transaction related data.  

  • Help facilitate lender compliance with asset specific requirements such as obtaining approvals for lease agreements or significant capital expenditure projects and coordinating the release of escrow accounts.

  • Support enforcement of internal audit requirements with third party providers, and further ensure that such firms are conducting business in accordance with property management agreements.

POSITION QUALIFICATIONS

Education:

  • Four year or equivalent undergraduate degree in Business or related field. Post graduate education a plus.  A commitment to continued education in real estate will be encouraged.

Work Experience:

  • 2 – 5 years of experience in the commercial and/or multi-family real estate industry (or business related activity) is required.

  • Property level experience is a plus.

Skills:

  • Effective interpersonal, communication (verbal and written) and listening skills.

  • Strong computer literacy including fluency with Argus, VTS, RealPage, Microsoft Excel and Word, and PowerPoint.

  • Ability to understand and interpret commercial lease agreements and language as well as present value concepts and financial terminology.

  • Analytical problem solving skills capable to undertake detailed analyses and make well-reasoned, decisive recommendations.

  • Demonstrated prioritization, organization and time management skills necessary to balance competing deadlines.

  • Desire to multi-task in a complex, high-volume environment and competency to work with all levels of management and personnel on critical projects.

Travel:

  • Some travel will be required for periodic asset management related property visits and select acquisition due diligence

jobs.title: Senior Analyst, Asset-Backed Investments
jobs.description:
About Invesco

As one of the world’s leading independent global investment firms, Invesco is dedicated to rethinking possibilities for our clients. By delivering the combined power of our distinctive investment management capabilities, we provide a wide range of investment strategies and vehicles to our clients around the world. If you're looking for challenging work, intelligent colleagues, and exposure across a global footprint, come explore your potential at Invesco.

What’s in it for you?

Our people are at the very core of our success. Invesco employees get more out of life through our comprehensive compensation and benefit offerings including:

  • Flexible paid time off

  • Hybrid work schedule 

  • 401(K) matching of 100% up to the first 6% with a discretionary supplemental contribution 

  • Health & wellbeing benefits 

  • Parental Leave benefits 

  • Employee stock purchase plan

Job Description

The Invesco Structured Investments team manages a wide range of strategies to support the broader Invesco Fixed Income platform. Its mandate spans the residential and commercial agency and non-agency mortgage-backed sectors as well as the asset-backed securities sector with the goal of delivering best-in-class fund performance. The team manages approximately $40 bn USD across a variety of strategies including mutual funds, ETFs, institutional separately managed accounts, and an Agency-focused mortgage REIT. 

The Senior Analyst will serve as a key member of the team responsible for evaluating, selecting and positioning public and private asset-backed investments across Invesco-managed products. They will also participate in business development activities with both current and prospective clients. Additionally, the Senior Analyst will be actively involved in elevating the team profile across the broader Invesco Fixed Income platform.

Responsibilities:

  • Collaborate with portfolio managers and other analysts on a variety of public and private asset-backed finance investment strategies managed by the Structured Investments team, including mutual funds, exchange-traded funds and institutional accounts

  • Deep experience in asset-backed underwriting which includes, but is not limited to, assessing underlying collateral and conducting loan and bond cash flow modeling

  • Underwrite public ABS and private asset-backed finance investments across a wide range of ABS/ABF sectors and issuers. The team invests in flow, liquid credit, esoteric, and non-public sectors

  • Conduct market research and assist senior team members in formulating an investment outlook

  • Utilize excel, general-purpose programming languages and artificial intelligence to conduct monthly credit surveillance/monitoring

  • Employ a relative value-oriented mindset when assessing cross-sector asset allocation opportunities across the universe of public and private structured products (ABS, CMBS, MBS, asset-backed loans and mortgage-backed loans) as well as other fixed income sectors

  • Develop meaningful relationships with loan originators and sell-side contacts to help facilitate deal flow and best execution on investment acquisitions

  • Interact with broader Structured Investments analysts to better understand underwriting, internal rating processes and sector nuances

  • Formulate and communicate trade ideas for the broader Invesco Fixed Income platform to generate potential alpha for the wider set of investment teams

  • Periodically interact with current or prospective retail and institutional investors

  • Draft periodic investment commentary for both internal and external audiences

  • Commercialize asset-backed investment capabilities through product development and marketing efforts

Qualifications:

  • 7+ years of experience in underwriting asset-backed securities or private asset-backed finance investments across a variety of collateral types (e.g., credit cards, autos, student loans, whole business, aircraft, container, data center, fiber optic, etc.)

  • Expertise in the use of Intex and Bloomberg

  • Strong knowledge of Microsoft Office, including Excel and PowerPoint

  • Python and VBA programming skills are a plus

  • CFA Charterholder or pursuit thereof preferred   

Preferred Skills & Attributes:

  • Humble, client centric focus - we are fiduciaries of our clients’ assets and must understand their goals, desires, and investment objectives

  • Commercially oriented mindset, with desire to assist in growth of the ABS and private asset-backed finance platform

  • Open minded and willingness to learn new topics and investment/asset subsectors

  • Strong written and verbal presentation skills and ability to work constructively as a key member of a dynamic investment team

  • Highly methodical with a strong attention to detail

  • Proficiency in quantitative methods and analytical mindset

  • Ability to articulate a comprehensive investment framework and adapt it to meet specific client objectives

  • Ability to source investments and drive outcomes through decisive risk-taking

jobs.title: Associate Quantitative Analyst, Fixed Income
jobs.description:
About Invesco

As one of the world’s leading independent global investment firms, Invesco is dedicated to rethinking possibilities for our clients. By delivering the combined power of our distinctive investment management capabilities, we provide a wide range of investment strategies and vehicles to our clients around the world. If you're looking for challenging work, intelligent colleagues, and exposure across a global footprint, come explore your potential at Invesco.

What’s in it for you?

Our people are at the very core of our success. Invesco employees get more out of life through our comprehensive compensation and benefit offerings including:

  • Flexible paid time off

  • Hybrid work schedule 

  • 401(K) matching of 100% up to the first 6% with a discretionary supplemental contribution 

  • Health & wellbeing benefits 

  • Parental Leave benefits 

  • Employee stock purchase plan

Job Description

About the Role:

The Associate Quantitative Analyst, Fixed Income will be the primary developer and maintainer of internal systems critical to the daily management of our successful and growing Fixed Income ETF lineup. The Analyst will work under the direction of ETF Portfolio Management to operate and extend tools for automated trading, performance attribution, corporate action analysis, basket negotiation tools and data quality monitoring, among other duties. Successful candidates will be self-directed, curious and eager to identify and implement opportunities to improve the portfolio management process. Knowledge of Python and SQL is required. The ability to work in a group environment with daily interactions with portfolio managers, information technology, and operational staff is expected. General knowledge of ETFs, currency hedging, fixed income securities, trading strategies, broker relationships and portfolio management functions is a plus, but not required. The position, located in Downers Grove, Illinois, will serve as a single contributor with no immediate direct reports. 

Responsibilities of the Role:

  • Develop, support and maintain our proprietary risk, flow and order optimization/monitoring system.
  • Own our In-Kind Order negotiation and capture process, working directly with Brokers, Portfolio Management, Trading and Technology to enhance our capabilities.
  • Develop a full understanding of our investment process; become capable of handling orders and rebalances with minimal supervision.
  • Gain a thorough understanding of the global ETF infrastructure and workflow, which includes Portfolio Management requirements for ETF portfolios managed in Europe, Asia, and the United States.

Requirements of the Role:

  • Bachelor's degree in computer science, physics, mathematics, or electrical engineering, or other scientific discipline preferred.
  • Prior experience with Python and SQL is required. Experience with JavaScript (esp. React/Angular), Rust, C, C++, Java, C# or similar is also valuable.
  • Experience with Polars, Plotly Dash (or equivalent), Flask, and the standard python data science stack.
  • Basic knowledge of git is required. Experience with CI tools (GitHub/BitBucket pipelines, Jenkins) is a plus.
  • Working knowledge of Linear Programming at the undergraduate level is desirable. Experience with libraries such as Gurobi/CPLEX/SCIP is a huge plus.
  • Linux experience, or experience with cloud platforms (AWS, GCP, Azure, etc.) is a plus.
  • Fixed Income/Trading experience is desirable but not required.
  • Ability to make quick decisions, establish priorities and concentrate on detailed information under time sensitive conditions.
  • Knowledge of the global fixed income markets, prior trading experience, and the ability to provide market color are preferred skills but not required.
  • Proficiency in any of the following PM systems is considered a plus: Aladdin, Bloomberg, Charles River.

The salary range for this position in Downers Grove is $125,000 to $147,000 annually. The total compensation offered for this position, which includes salary and incentive pay, will vary based on skills, experience and location.

Full Time / Part Time

Full time

Worker Type

Employee

Job Exempt (Yes / No)

Yes

Workplace Model

Pursuant to Invesco’s Workplace Policy, employees are expected to comply with the firm’s most current workplace model, which as of October 1, 2025, includes spending at least four full days each week working in an Invesco office. This reflects our belief that spending time together in the office helps us build stronger relationships, collaborate more easily, and support each other’s growth and development.

It is not designed to contain or be interpreted as a comprehensive inventory of all duties, responsibilities and qualifications required of employees assigned to this job. The job holder may be required to perform other duties as deemed appropriate by their manager from time to time.

All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, creed, color, religion, sex, gender, gender identity, sexual orientation, marital status, national origin, citizenship status, disability, age, or veteran status. Our equal opportunity employment efforts comply with all applicable U.S. state and federal laws governing non-discrimination in employment.

jobs.description:
As one of the world’s leading independent global investment firms, Invesco is dedicated to rethinking possibilities for our clients. By delivering the combined power of our distinctive investment management capabilities, we provide a wide range of investment strategies and vehicles to our clients around the world. Your Role:

To ensure successful delivery of new private markets product launches and product change initiatives with an emphasis on technical, structural, regulatory, tax, customer channels, disclosures and operational items, whilst managing internal governance and key stakeholder relationships.

What you will be doing:

  • To independent project manage private market product initiatives from design and approval through to implementation in conjunction with all key business partners.

  • To contribute across all phases of the Product Development Lifecycle with an emphasis on technical, structural, regulatory, tax, customer channels, disclosures and operational business case inputs.

  • Manage internal governance as required (adhering to the requirements of various committees, boards, product development lifecycle and policies and procedures).

  • To successfully manage any issues and risks relating to each product initiative.

  • To manage expectations and escalate any potential delays, issues and/or risks.

  • Relationship building - Develop close working relationships with key stakeholders both within the business (e.g. Investments, Distribution, Control Functions & Operations) and within key counterparts to resolve barriers to the successful achievement of agreed projects.

  • Help drive the team to achieve its strategic goals and contribute to team strategy.  Contribute to the development Invesco EMEA’s role within our Global Private Markets Product function.

  • To take on significant ad hoc projects as required with respect to technical or product related regulatory change and develop end to end solutions to support business needs.

  • Ensure all activities are in line with Invesco’s Conduct principles

What you will bring:

  • Prior experience within the investment management sector and/or financial services industry within a product or related function
    Prior experience with Private Markets product structuring and/or product management

  • Understanding of fund structures, governance, and regulatory frameworks.

  • In depth knowledge of financial markets with investment experience a plus

  • Proficient in Microsoft Office applications, including Word, Excel, & PowerPoint

  • Familiar with third party data providers

  • Proven communication and interpersonal skills

  • PRINCE2, Agile or another project management accreditation desirable but not essential

  • Educated to Master’s degree level or equivalent from recognised university and/or CFA would be preferable, ideally with finance, mathematics, engineering or economic qualifications

  • Self-motivated and a desire to succeed with a results-based orientation

  • Process and organisational skills

  • Strategic thinking skills

  • Commercial/ business acumen

  • Written communication and oral presentation skills

  • Numeracy skills

jobs.title: Senior Fund Manager, Greater China (Hong Kong Based)
jobs.description:
About Invesco

As one of the world’s leading independent global investment firms, Invesco is dedicated to rethinking possibilities for ourclients. By delivering the combined power of our distinctive investment management capabilities, we provide a wide range of investment strategies and vehicles to our clients around the world. If you're looking for challenging work, intelligent colleagues, and exposure across a global footprint, come explore your potential at Invesco.

What's in it for you?

Our people are at the very core of our success. Invesco employees get more out of life through our comprehensive compensation and benefit offerings including: 

  • Generous annual leave plus flexible paid time off

  • Hybrid work schedule 

  • Learning and development programs

  • Health & wellbeing benefits 

  • Parental Leave benefits 

  • Employee stock purchase plan (if applicable in your region)

Job Description

About the role:

Reporting to Team Leader, Hong Kong & China, the successful candidate will become responsible for managing Greater China/Chinese equities funds investing in Greater China/Chinese equities. This role is expected to conduct own fundamental research to develop high conviction, while leveraging research, investment ideas and recommendations of sector analysts in the team.

Responsibilities of the role:

  • Fund management responsibilities including retail funds and institutional client portfolios, investing in Greater China/Chinese equities.

  • Conduct in-depth fundamental research and analysis on Chinese equities, share sectoral coverage responsibilities with investment teams, to determine investment merits and risks.

  • Participate in internal portfolio review meetings and meetings/livestreams/teach-ins/roadshows with clients and consultants to discuss investment process, review performance & risk of portfolios, review strategy, outlook, active bets and portfolio changes.

  • Work closely with sales to grow Greater China/Chinese equity AUM and pitch for new business. Be mindful of opportunities to develop new products.

  • Work closely with Asia ex Japan investment team and with our JV partner, Invesco Great Wall, to leverage research and discover new opportunities in China.

Requirement for the Role:

The ideal candidate should be able to clearly articulate a robust investment philosophy and process. Proven track record of at least 3-5 years investing in Greater China/Chinese equities (including China A-shares), and is expected to be an effective communicator who, with the support of our Client Portfolio Managers, can build/strengthen relationships of trust with key internal and external stakeholders, including local/overseas institutional investors, consultants and distributors.

  • Bachelor’s degree holder in Finance / Accounting / Economics or related discipline.

  • 10+ years of relevant work experience in being an analyst and portfolio manager of Greater China/Chinese equities (onshore and offshore).

  • Proven strong performance track record in stock picking, portfolio construction and generating strong risk-adjusted performance for Greater China/Chinese equity funds.

  • Strong writing and communications skills in English and Mandarin Chinese a must.

  • Strong interpersonal skills with proven ability to build trust and relationships.

  • Considers the ultimate impact of decisions and actions on internal and external customers.

  • Mission Driven: care deeply about the impact, goals, and overall values of the firm.

How to Apply:

  •  Apply for the role directly at Invesco Careers: Careers at Invesco | APAC.

As one of the world’s leading independent global investment firms, Invesco is dedicated to rethinking possibilities for our clients. Job Description

We are seeking a highly skilled and experienced Advanced AV Support Professional to provide exceptional on-site, white-glove support for audiovisual (AV) systems. The ideal candidate will have a strong background in AV technologies, including expertise with Crestron DSPs, Q-SYS Designer, and a proven track record in delivering outstanding service in a corporate environment. As part of this role, you will be responsible for ensuring a seamless AV experience for clients and stakeholders, troubleshooting and resolving technical issues, and maintaining high-quality AV setups for meetings, events, and presentations.

Key Responsibilities:

  • Provide on-site, dedicated AV support to ensure smooth operation of all AV systems and technologies.

  • Offer white-glove, proactive assistance to ensure an exceptional AV experience for executives, teams, and clients.

  • Manage and support the configuration, deployment, and troubleshooting of AV hardware, software, and systems.

  • Serve as the expert for Crestron equipment, ensuring its proper use and integration with other AV technologies.

  • Troubleshoot and resolve issues related to AV setups, including video conferencing, audio systems, and display equipment.

  • Coordinate with vendors and internal teams to ensure timely and efficient resolution of AV-related incidents.

  • Maintain a deep understanding of client-specific needs and provide tailored AV solutions for high-profile meetings and events.

  • Regularly perform system checks and preventive maintenance to ensure AV systems remain in optimal working condition.

  • Document issues and resolutions, ensuring detailed records for continuous improvement.

  • Provide training and guidance to users on best practices for AV system usage.

  • Collaborate with Technology, Facilities, and Event teams to ensure AV requirements are met for conferences, meetings, and corporate events.

Required Skills and Qualifications:

  • Proven experience as an AV Support Engineer or similar role, with at least 5 years of experience in the AV support field.

  • Deep understanding of Crestron Microsoft Teams rooms.

  • Strong knowledge and hands-on experience with Crestron Microsoft Teams Rooms, Crestron DSPs, Q-SYS Designer application, including configuration, troubleshooting, and system integration.

  • Expertise in AV technologies such as video conferencing, audio systems, displays, and AV control systems.

  • Strong problem-solving skills with the ability to troubleshoot and resolve technical issues in real-time.

  • Exceptional customer service and communication skills, with a focus on delivering a high-level of white-glove support.

  • Ability to work under pressure and handle urgent AV support requests efficiently.

  • Proficient in using AV-related software, tools, and systems.

  • Excellent attention to detail and ability to maintain high standards of service.

  • Ability to work independently and as part of a team to achieve successful outcomes.

Preferred Qualifications:

  • Certifications in AV technologies or related fields (e.g., CTS, Crestron, Extron).

  • Experience supporting high-profile or executive-level clients in a corporate environment.

  • Knowledge of additional AV software and systems is a plus.

Full time

Employee

No

jobs.title: Associate, Private Credit
jobs.description:
As one of the world’s leading independent global investment firms, Invesco is dedicated to rethinking possibilities for our clients. By delivering the combined power of our distinctive investment management capabilities, we provide a wide range of investment strategies and vehicles to our clients around the world. If you're looking for challenging work, intelligent colleagues, and exposure across a global footprint, come explore your potential at Invesco.

Job Description

We have an outstanding benefits package which includes: 

  • Company-provided healthcare 

  • A competitive annual leave allowance 

  • Flexible working options, including hybrid working arrangements 

  • Generous pension provisions 

  • Income protection 

  • Health and wellness benefits 

  • Volunteering days 

  • Enhanced parental leave 

  • Life insurance 


Your job:

We’re looking for an Associate – Private Credit to join our Bank Loans team in London!

This is an exciting opportunity to work across senior secured loans and high‑yield bonds while developing deep sector expertise, driving fundamental credit analysis, and contributing directly to portfolio construction. If you enjoy analytical work, want to deepen your understanding of leveraged finance, and thrive in a collaborative investment environment, we’d love to hear from you.

Among your responsibilities will be:

Investment Analysis & Credit Underwriting

  • Review and prepare credit theses for new senior secured loan and high‑yield bond investments (fixed and floating).

  • Produce detailed credit proposals, including company and industry analysis, capital structure assessment, cash‑flow projections, collateral adequacy, management quality, lender and sponsor strength, and restructuring considerations.

  • Present well‑argued investment recommendations to the Investment Committee.

  • Develop clear views on entry and exit yields for both existing and potential investments.

Sector Coverage & Monitoring

  • Build in‑depth knowledge across 2–3 industries and maintain full coverage of all senior loan and high‑yield opportunities within those sectors.

  • Work toward taking responsibility for 25–30 loan and bond positions.

  • Conduct ongoing monitoring, including financial analysis, cash‑flow modelling, rating reviews, collateral assessments, and performance tracking.

  • Identify relative value opportunities and make informed buy/sell/hold recommendations.

  • Actively generate new investment ideas within your coverage universe.

Market Interaction & Due Diligence

  • Participate in management meetings, site visits, plant tours, and inspections.

  • Engage with syndicate desks, sell‑side analysts, and private equity sponsors to evaluate both primary and secondary investment opportunities.

  • Review loan and bond documentation for new transactions and amendments, in collaboration with senior analysts and team leaders.

  • Build and maintain a strong network of peers to understand market sentiment on amendments and new deals.

Distressed & Special Situations

  • Assist in the workout and restructuring of distressed positions.

  • Support senior team members in evaluating complex secondary and stressed opportunities.

Risk Management & Administration

  • Maintain accurate electronic credit files, compliance reports, collateral records, and exposure reporting.

  • Ensure all activities align with Invesco’s Conduct Principles.

What you can bring:

  • Experience as a credit, leveraged finance, or corporate finance analyst (buy-side, sell-side, rating agency, PE, consulting, or similar).

  • Strong proficiency in Microsoft Office, especially Excel and Word.

  • Bachelor’s degree required; CFA qualification beneficial.

  • Additional European language(s) preferred.

  • Strong analytical skills with the ability to “join the dots” and see the bigger picture.

  • Excellent financial modelling, quantitative, and qualitative assessment capabilities.

  • Clear written and verbal communication skills; comfortable presenting to senior stakeholders.

  • Highly organised, detail-oriented, and able to manage multiple priorities.

  • A proactive, solution-oriented mindset with strong integrity and teamwork skills.

Disability Confident Scheme: 

Applicants who opt in to the Disability Confident Scheme and meet the criteria for the role will be offered an interview. We are committed to providing an inclusive recruitment process for all candidates who make an application. By opting-in to this scheme, applicants will be disclosing that they have a disability solely for the purpose of the Disability Confident Scheme.   

The Disability Confident Scheme only guarantees an interview – it does not automatically mean that applicants interviewed will gain employment with Invesco at that time.  

To apply through the Disability Confident Scheme, you’ll need to firstly ensure you have applied for the role via our external careers page. jobs.description:
About Invesco

As one of the world’s leading independent global investment firms, Invesco is dedicated to rethinking possibilities for ourclients. By delivering the combined power of our distinctive investment management capabilities, we provide a wide range of investment strategies and vehicles to our clients around the world. If you're looking for challenging work, intelligent colleagues, and exposure across a global footprint, come explore your potential at Invesco.

What's in it for you?

Our people are at the very core of our success. Invesco employees get more out of life through our comprehensive compensation and benefit offerings including: 

  • Generous annual leave plus flexible paid time off

  • Hybrid work schedule 

  • Learning and development programs

  • Health & wellbeing benefits 

  • Parental Leave benefits 

  • Employee stock purchase plan (if applicable in your region)

Job Description

Your Team
You will join a global team of 27+ experienced portfolio managers from diverse backgrounds managing a large number of Equity and Fixed Income ETFs. The newly established Taiwan ETF PM team will play an integral part in managing and growing the local ETF business.

About the Role:

  • Manage passive ETFs, index funds, and feeder ETFs in equity, fixed income, or balanced/multi-asset categories.
  • Ensure compliance with Taiwan regulatory requirements and internal investment processes.
  • Oversee index reconstitution, portfolio trading, cash management, and corporate action decisions.
  • Monitor tracking error and implement strategies to optimize portfolio performance.
  • Collaborate with equity and fixed income analysts and traders to implement portfolio decisions.
  • Lead or contribute to investment process improvements to enhance efficiency and scalability.
  • Engage with internal stakeholders (capital markets, product development) and external clients (institutional due diligence, conferences).
  • Utilize advanced portfolio analytics and modeling tools (e.g., Aladdin, Bloomberg, FactSet, Barra/Axioma) for portfolio optimization.
  • Maintain thorough documentation of policies, procedures, and investment decisions.

Requirement for the Role :

  • Master’s degree in Business, Finance, Economics, or related field preferred.
  • CFA designation or progress towards CFA is preferred but not mandatory.
  • 5 to 15 years of asset management experience, specifically managing passive ETFs or mutual funds in both equity and fixed income.
  • Minimum 5 years of direct experience managing or co-managing passive funds/ETFs.
  • Strong knowledge of global equity and fixed income markets, index construction, and passive investment strategies.
  • Experience with portfolio optimization, tracking error management, and corporate action analysis.
  • Excellent communication skills for client engagement and cross-team collaboration.
  • Experience with portfolio management systems such as Aladdin, Bloomberg, or Charles River is preferred.
  • Proficiency in programming languages such as Python, SQL/Oracle, and VBA is a significant advantage.

  • On-site presence in Taipei office is mandatory.

  • Ability to work under regulatory requirements, personally implementing investment processes.

    Strong analytical and problem-solving skills.

  • Ability to work effectively in a team environment with portfolio managers, analysts, traders, compliance, and operations.

    Speak fluent English to communicate with the global team

Taiwan Licensing and Certification Requirements

The candidate must meet one of the following certification and experience criteria:

  • Certified Securities Investment Analyst (CSIA) License
  • Passed a Senior Securities Specialist Examination
  • Passed examination for Securities Investment Trust and Consulting Professionals

How to Apply:

  • Apply for the role directly here or at Invesco Careers: Careers at Invesco | APAC.

jobs.title: Senior ETF Analyst
jobs.description:
About Invesco

As one of the world’s leading independent global investment firms, Invesco is dedicated to rethinking possibilities for our clients. By delivering the combined power of our distinctive investment management capabilities, we provide a wide range of investment strategies and vehicles to our clients around the world. If you're looking for challenging work, intelligent colleagues, and exposure across a global footprint, come explore your potential at Invesco.

What’s in it for you?

Our people are at the very core of our success. Invesco employees get more out of life through our comprehensive compensation and benefit offerings including:

  • Flexible paid time off

  • Hybrid work schedule 

  • 401(K) matching of 100% up to the first 6% with a discretionary supplemental contribution 

  • Health & wellbeing benefits 

  • Parental Leave benefits 

  • Employee stock purchase plan

Job Description

About the Team / Department:
The ETF Product Strategy Group within the Americas Product is committed to providing superior client support and actionable investment insights that drive our product line’s strategic agenda for growth.

About the Role:

This individual will work closely with others on the team and help develop ETF research and analysis that positions our US ETFs in the current macro environment. The Sr. Analyst will work cross-functionally with Product Development, Portfolio Management, Capital Markets, Sales and Marketing to position and grow the product line.

Responsibilities of the Role:

  • Work alongside analysts and strategists on the team to develop product segment research and analysis on Invesco ETFs, the ETF industry, and broader financial markets serving as a subject matter expert for the product line
  • Develop and update product materials that position Invesco’s ETFs
  • Project manage and lead research projects as assigned by senior leaders
  • Execute on actions needed to maintain and improve product line
  • Partner closely with ETF strategists and specialists to support our clients and ensure product integrity, occasionally participating in client meetings

Requirements of the Role:

  • Undergraduate degree in business, finance, mathematics or related discipline required
  • 3-5 years of investment management and/or research experience preferred
  • Series 7 & 63 (already licensed is a plus, can study for these exams after being hired)
  • CFA track or progress towards advanced strongly preferred
  • Skills and professional development in a senior analyst role with a desire to take on more responsibility and accountability
  • Previous experience or solid understanding of factor, thematic or fixed-income investing strongly preferred
  • ETF and index familiarity and industry experience required
  • Experience with Bloomberg, Morningstar Direct, FactSet, Python a plus
  • Strong research and analytical skills to analyze and interpret various market research reports and data and apply such information to daily activities
  • Strong subject matter acumen and written communication skills
  • Team player with excellent interpersonal skills; maintain effective, cooperative working relationships with all departments and organizational levels
  • Self-directed; must be comfortable with establishing objectives, then working independently to achieve them, seeking the team’s input as needed

The total compensation offered for this position, which includes salary and incentive pay, will vary based on skills, experience and location. The salary range for this position in Downers Grove is $110k to $135k annually.

Meets with clients, prospects, consultants and/or intermediaries to explain the investment philosophy, process and performance of specific products. Provides updates on product performance and market trends. Works closely with investment and product development staff on product enhancements.

Meets clients, prospects, consultants and/or intermediaries to explain the investment philosophy, process and performance on alternative products (e.g., private equity, venture capital, hedge funds, real estate). Reports to the Investment function.

The general role of the Junior CPM (senior associate) is to support CPM team covering Korea market, ensuring the timely and accurate execution of general business activities. jobs.description:
About Invesco

As one of the world’s leading independent global investment firms, Invesco is dedicated to rethinking possibilities for our clients. By delivering the combined power of our distinctive investment management capabilities, we provide a wide range of investment strategies and vehicles to our clients around the world. If you're looking for challenging work, intelligent colleagues, and exposure across a global footprint, come explore your potential at Invesco.

What’s in it for you?

Our people are at the very core of our success. Invesco employees get more out of life through our comprehensive compensation and benefit offerings including:

  • 401(K) matching of 100% up to the first 6% with a discretionary supplemental contribution

Job Description

The role:

The Director of Private Markets Investment Due Diligence is responsible for accelerating industry-leading platform relevance for Invesco with Alternative Investments Teams. This role will enable broad adoption of Invesco’s private markets capabilities, initially focusing on real estate and private credit. You will share investment thought leadership, collaborate with clients on product development, and work closely with Invesco’s private markets investment teams. Key to success is pursuing a tailored client service experience with professional buyers (research professionals) with the primary goal of increasing Invesco’s presence on approved / recommended lists. The Investment Due Diligence (IDD) team strives to be the industry’s leading home office engagement team that meaningfully exceeds client expectations.

Responsibilities of the role:

  • Establish and sustain deep relationships with Alternatives Investments decision-makers at U.S. wealth management firms

  • Understand each client’s investment priorities, infrastructure, and decision-making process

  • Develop a strategic pipeline for private markets strategies in conjunction with investment teams and WMP, inclusive of timelines and goals

  • Drive all aspects of research engagement through high-level and frequent interaction

  • Monitor platform-level trends and opportunities to strategically position Invesco’s product line

  • Acquire and share feedback with internal partners on client priorities, changes in investment views, and industry trends

  • Actively engage investment teams to cultivate new opportunities, support existing placements, and build awareness on the multi-layered process of distributing in the wealth management channel

  • Collaborate closely with other members of WMP client coverage team

  • Coordinate due diligence, facilitate negotiations and onboarding activity with Invesco functional enterprise support teams

  • Deliver outstanding client service by being highly adaptable and ensuring timely completion of requests

Requirements of the role:

  • 10+ years of due diligence experience or wealth management platform-related business development experience

  • 5+ years private market experience; operational due diligence; real estate and/or private credit experience a plus

  • High level of investment competence, confirmed by specific work experience and investment credentials (MBA, CFA, CAIA)

  • Experience with third party alternative markets platform vendors who offer services that enable access to alternative investments

  • Excellent interpersonal skills and a validated ability to build and manage relationships

  • A results driven mentality with a natural sense of timeliness in pursuing opportunities

  • Meticulous organization and business leadership skills

  • Strong communication (written and oral) and engaging presentation skills

  • Up to 40% domestic travel required

The salary range for this position is $190,000 to $200,000/ year. The total compensation offered for this position includes salary and incentive pay and will vary based on skills, experience and location.

jobs.title: Portfolio Manager, Agency MBS
jobs.description:
About Invesco

As one of the world’s leading independent global investment firms, Invesco is dedicated to rethinking possibilities for our clients. By delivering the combined power of our distinctive investment management capabilities, we provide a wide range of investment strategies and vehicles to our clients around the world. If you're looking for challenging work, intelligent colleagues, and exposure across a global footprint, come explore your potential at Invesco.

What’s in it for you?

Our people are at the very core of our success. Invesco employees get more out of life through our comprehensive compensation and benefit offerings including:

  • Flexible paid time off

  • Hybrid work schedule 

  • 401(K) matching of 100% up to the first 6% with a discretionary supplemental contribution 

  • Health & wellbeing benefits 

  • Parental Leave benefits 

  • Employee stock purchase plan

Job Description

The Invesco Structured Investments team manages a wide range of strategies to support the broader Invesco Fixed Income platform. Its mandate spans the residential and commercial agency and non-agency mortgage-backed sectors as well as the asset-backed securities sector with the goal of delivering best-in-class fund performance. The team manages approximately $44 bn USD across a variety of strategies including mutual funds, ETFs, institutional separately managed accounts, and an Agency-focused mortgage REIT.

The Portfolio Manager will serve as a key member of the team responsible for evaluating, selecting and positioning Agency MBS and related investments across Invesco-managed products.

Responsibilities:

  • Apply Agency MBS expertise to generate and implement investment ideas through comprehensive analysis of specified pools, CMOs, TBAs, and other agency mortgage derivatives

  • Manage portfolios focused primarily on Agency MBS across various investment vehicles, including mutual funds, ETFs and separately managed institutional accounts

  • Collaborate with portfolio management colleagues on investment strategies managed by the broader Structured Investments and Invesco Fixed Income team

  • Employ a relative value-oriented mindset when assessing cross-sector asset allocation opportunities across the universe of structured products and other investment grade credit sectors

  • Add value through design and execution of advanced hedging strategies

  • Develop and communicate investment outlook to internal and external stakeholders

  • Build trusted relationships with sell-side counterparties to facilitate best execution on Agency MBS sourcing and trading

  • Cultivate strong, lasting partnerships with current and prospective retail and institutional clients

  • Commercialize Agency MBS and broader structured products investment capabilities through product development and marketing efforts

Qualifications:

  • 10+ years of direct experience in analyzing, trading, and managing risk in Agency MBS including TBA, specified pools, CMOs, and derivatives

  • CFA or MBA preferred

  • Comprehensive understanding of all facets of Agency MBS and related macroeconomic, financial market, and regulatory dynamics

  • Significant knowledge of other structured products sectors

  • Expertise in design and implementation of hedging strategies via swaps, futures, swaptions, and other interest rate derivatives

  • Experience managing levered portfolios is beneficial

  • Command of industry tools including Bloomberg, Tradeweb, Yield Book, DataQuery, etc.

  • Familiarity with BlackRock Aladdin platform is beneficial

Preferred Skills & Attributes:

  • Demonstrated success in driving investment outcomes through decisive risk-taking and portfolio management

  • Proficiency in quantitative methods and analytical mindset

  • Ability to articulate a comprehensive investment framework and adapt it to meet specific client objectives

  • Humble, client centric focus - we are fiduciaries of our clients’ assets and must understand their goals, desires, and investment objectives

  • Commercially oriented mindset

  • Strong written and verbal presentation skills and ability to work constructively as a key member of a dynamic investment team

  • Proficiency leveraging AI tools to automate and optimize workflows

jobs.description:
About Invesco

As one of the world’s leading independent global investment firms, Invesco is dedicated to rethinking possibilities for our clients. By delivering the combined power of our distinctive investment management capabilities, we provide a wide range of investment strategies and vehicles to our clients around the world. If you're looking for challenging work, intelligent colleagues, and exposure across a global footprint, come explore your potential at Invesco.

What’s in it for you?

Our people are at the very core of our success. Invesco employees get more out of life through our comprehensive compensation and benefit offerings including:

  • Flexible paid time off

  • Hybrid work schedule 

  • 401(K) matching of 100% up to the first 6% with a discretionary supplemental contribution 

  • Health & wellbeing benefits 

  • Parental Leave benefits 

  • Employee stock purchase plan

Job Description

About the Role:

The Associate Portfolio Manager will work closely with the Senior Portfolio Manager, Global Equities, to help shape the strategy, direction, and effective management of Global Equities portfolios within Invesco Fundamental Equities’ investment and risk framework. This role requires strong fiduciary responsibility to clients, active engagement with the Global Equities team, and the ability to interact confidently with clients, consultants, brokers, and other external stakeholders. Covers equity securities of companies with market capitalizations of over USD $10B that are headquartered in many developed and emerging countries across the world. Performs due diligence on investments in the portfolio (e.g., conducts site visits, meets with management). Works with the trading desk for securities market trading and provides instructions to traders. May participate in client marketing activities. This position includes direct stock coverage responsibilities and is a part of a team that manages a total of $18.3B as of January 31st, 2026.

Responsibilities of the Role:

Portfolio Management & Investment Process

  • Partner with the Senior Portfolio Manager to develop, refine, and implement strategies for Global Equities portfolios
  • Analyze portfolio composition, performance, and risk attributes to support optimal portfolio outcomes
  • Maintain financial models and active coverage on approximately 20 companies across at least three sectors.
  • Contribute to portfolio construction and integrate quantitative signals in collaboration with internal partners (e.g., quantitative analyst)
  • Review and analyze investment results, attribution, and trends to enhance performance with strong fiduciary discipline
  • Work with the Senior Portfolio Manager to develop and maintain an effective investment process, including trading policies, procedures, and annual business goals

Research & Market Coverage

  • Monitor legislative, regulatory, and macroeconomic developments affecting global equities
  • Interface with the ECM desk regarding potential IPO opportunities
  • Collaborate with ESG and Global Investment Forum teams 

Risk, Compliance & Governance

  • Ensure full compliance with regulatory requirements and internal risk frameworks
  • Engage regularly with the Risk team to review portfolio exposures, discuss findings, and provide feedback
  • Prepare materials for investment committees and respond to ad hoc information requests from senior management

Client, Consultant & Internal Partner Engagement

  • Represent the Global Equities team to internal and external stakeholders, including clients, prospects, consultants, brokers, and gatekeepers
  • Market the team’s capabilities to existing and potential clients as well as internal distribution partners
  • Participate in meetings with major wire houses, key accounts, and investment platforms
  • Provide timely information, reporting, and insights to internal partners and external constituencies

Requirements of the role:

  • A Master’s degree in finance, economics, mathematics, business, or a related field is ideal
  • 10+ years of experience in equity research and/or portfolio management
  • Strong understanding of both retail and institutional clients
  • Coverage experience across at least three sectors
  • Strong written and verbal communication skills with effective presentation ability
  • Demonstrated success as a team player in collaborative, global, and cross-functional environments
  • Effective interpersonal skills with the ability to influence outcomes across teams and organizational levels
  • Confident engaging with management teams, brokers, consultants, and clients
  • Open-minded, flexible, and receptive to diverse viewpoints
  • Exhibits leadership qualities, intellectual curiosity, and a strong work ethic
  • Ability to travel internationally required
  • CFA designation strongly preferred

The salary range for this position in New York is $250,000 to $280,000 / year. The total compensation offered for this position includes salary and incentive pay and will vary based on skills, experience and location

jobs.description:
About Invesco

As one of the world’s leading independent global investment firms, Invesco is dedicated to rethinking possibilities for our clients. By delivering the combined power of our distinctive investment management capabilities, we provide a wide range of investment strategies and vehicles to our clients around the world. Job Description

Your Role
The Senior Principal will serve as a highly experienced subject matter expert in Bank Loan and Private Credit markets, providing strategic leadership and technical expertise across large-scale transformation initiatives. This role is a senior individual contributor position requiring deep knowledge of bank loan instruments, market infrastructure, and regulatory frameworks. The Senior Principal will drive operational excellence, process optimization, and best practice adoption while collaborating with internal and external stakeholders to ensure superior service delivery and risk mitigation.

You Will Be Responsible For:

  • Lead and influence large-scale transformation projects within Bank Loan Services, ensuring alignment with organizational goals and industry best practices.

  • Develop and implement Key Risk Indicators (KRIs) and Key Performance Indicators (KPIs) to monitor operational health and performance.

  • Champion continuous improvement initiatives and automation strategies to enhance efficiency and reduce operational risk.

  • Maintain deep understanding of Private Credit and Bank Loan markets, including CLO structures, trade settlement, and lifecycle management.

  • Act as a senior escalation point for complex issues such as restructurings, corporate actions, and new fund launches.

  • Oversee communications and coordination with trustees, agent banks, custodians, and other third-party providers.

  • Ensure robust internal controls and compliance with regulatory requirements.

  • Conduct process reviews and recommend enhancements to achieve best practice standards.

  • Design and implement solutions for new products in collaboration with FO, MO, technology teams and other stakeholders as necessary.

  • Lead audit processes across multiple fund structures with internal and external auditors.

  • Serve as a trusted advisor and mentor, providing training and guidance to team members and leadership as needed.

  • Build and maintain strong relationships with internal stakeholders (Front Office, MO, Global Operations, Risk, Legal, Fund Accounting) and external partners.

  • Represent Bank Loan Services in committees, service meetings, and industry forums.


The Experience You Bring:

  • Minimum 15 years of experience in investment industry with a focus on Private Credit and Bank Loan markets.

  • Proven track record of leading complex projects and transformation initiatives.

  • Extensive knowledge of trustee, agent bank, and custodian operations.

  • Familiarity with Wall Street Office Suite, Everest, and S&P Global industry tools/ apps, and rating agency platforms (S&P, Moody’s).

  • Strong understanding of automation tools and data analytics for process improvement.

  • Exceptional leadership and strategic thinking skills.

  • Excellent communication and interpersonal skills for engaging stakeholders at all levels.

  • BA/BS – Finance/Accounting preferred

jobs.description:
About Invesco

As one of the world’s leading independent global investment firms, Invesco is dedicated to rethinking possibilities for our clients. By delivering the combined power of our distinctive investment management capabilities, we provide a wide range of investment strategies and vehicles to our clients around the world. If you're looking for challenging work, intelligent colleagues, and exposure across a global footprint, come explore your potential at Invesco.

What’s in it for you?

Our people are at the very core of our success. Invesco employees get more out of life through our comprehensive compensation and benefit offerings including:

  • Flexible paid time off

  • Hybrid work schedule 

  • 401(K) matching of 100% up to the first 6% with a discretionary supplemental contribution 

  • Health & wellbeing benefits 

  • Parental Leave benefits 

  • Employee stock purchase plan

Job Description

Your Team

The ETF Product Management group is tasked with the development and execution of category and product specific strategies and tactics designed to better position the Invesco ETF product line in the competitive exchange traded product (ETP) market. 


Your Role

The Senior Fixed Income Product Strategist will leverage his/her capital markets, investment management and product knowledge to deliver market views and product positioning to institutions and financial advisors and will develop timely market outlook and sales ideas to address changing market conditions. This individual, in working with the Head of Fixed Income ETF Product Strategy, as part of the Product Strategy and Research Team, will be an expert in the assigned products and categories, and is responsible for supporting the research and strategy content development for the assigned product suite. The individual will utilize a client-focused approach to help communicate product strategy to effectively position and market assigned products.

You Will Be Responsible For:

  • Drive growth of the Fixed Income ETF product line in partnership with cross-functional teams (sales, marketing, capital markets, product management, portfolio management and product development) 

  • Help build and reinforce a brand for Invesco products by presenting at industry conferences and serving as the “voice” for the Fixed Income ETF product line in media interviews, sponsored events, webcasts, etc.

  • Support efforts to develop and manage insights on the product line through

  • Asset class and product-specific communication

  • Product training and positioning

  • Product-specific competitive analysis

  • Partner with Sales to execute product-specific distribution strategies and participate in client meetings

  • Content development in partnership with Marketing

  • Market and product performance commentaries

  • Monitor macro market conditions to formulate asset class views, and translate them into actionable investment and product positioning ideas for clients

  • Identify new product opportunities as well as assess existing product line to identify opportunities for enhancement

  • Help manage team responsible for Invesco Fixed Income ETF Strategy


The Experience You Bring:

  • 10 years of investment management and/or research experience required

  • Previous experience and solid understanding of fixed-income markets required

  • ETF and/or index familiarity and industry experience required

#LI-MICHELLETILLIS

The total compensation offered for this position, which includes salary and incentive pay, will vary based on skills, experience and location. The salary range for this position in New York City is $150K to $167K annually.

Full Time / Part Time

Full time

Worker Type

Employee

Job Exempt (Yes / No)

Yes

Workplace Model

Pursuant to Invesco’s Workplace Policy, employees are expected to comply with the firm’s most current workplace model, which as of October 1, 2025, includes spending at least four full days each week working in an Invesco office. This reflects our belief that spending time together in the office helps us build stronger relationships, collaborate more easily, and support each other’s growth and development.

The above information on this description has been designed to indicate the general nature and level of work performed by employees within this role. It is not designed to contain or be interpreted as a comprehensive inventory of all duties, responsibilities and qualifications required of employees assigned to this job. The job holder may be required to perform other duties as deemed appropriate by their manager from time to time.

Invesco's culture of inclusivity and its commitment to diversity in the workplace are demonstrated through our people practices. We are proud to be an equal opportunity employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, creed, color, religion, sex, gender, gender identity, sexual orientation, marital status, national origin, citizenship status, disability, age, or veteran status. Our equal opportunity employment efforts comply with all applicable U.S. state and federal laws governing non-discrimination in employment.

jobs.title: Manager, Product Research and Insights, APAC (Hong Kong Based)
jobs.description:
About Invesco

As one of the world’s leading independent global investment firms, Invesco is dedicated to rethinking possibilities for ourclients. By delivering the combined power of our distinctive investment management capabilities, we provide a wide range of investment strategies and vehicles to our clients around the world. If you're looking for challenging work, intelligent colleagues, and exposure across a global footprint, come explore your potential at Invesco.

What's in it for you?

Our people are at the very core of our success. Invesco employees get more out of life through our comprehensive compensation and benefit offerings including: 

  • Generous annual leave plus flexible paid time off

  • Hybrid work schedule 

  • Learning and development programs

  • Health & wellbeing benefits 

  • Parental Leave benefits 

  • Employee stock purchase plan (if applicable in your region)

Job Description

About the Role:

Invesco’s Product Research & Insights team in EMEA and APAC is a dynamic group that drives deep analysis of products, markets, and asset flows to uncover insights that sharpen product positioning, strengthen client engagement, inform product development, and unlock growth opportunities across EMEA and APAC.

Responsibilities of the role:

  • Develop deep expertise in Invesco’s product suite, competitor offerings, and industry trends within public and private markets

  • Conduct in-depth product research to uncover competitive advantages and develop differentiated value propositions that resonate with both internal and external audiences

  • Present at internal meetings to educate stakeholders on the competitive landscape and industry developments

  • Create and deliver compelling investment cases and insights that foster client engagement and influence decision-making

  • Collaborate with regional distribution teams to enhance client engagement through high-quality product and market intelligence

  • Partner with regional product development team to evaluate Invesco’s product shelf, identify critical gaps, and support the ideation of new products and structures

  • Contribute to our client-driven product prioritization process for APAC to align distribution efforts with areas of highest client demand and strongest product potential

  • Ensure all activities are in line with Invesco’s Conduct principles

Requirement for the Role:

  • University degree, preferably in a field related to financial markets; CFA designation or equivalent are highly desirable

  • 8+ years of experience in asset management or the broader financial industry, ideally in a role that combined research with effective delivery to key stakeholders and/or clients. Experience in product research & selection as well as product strategy is a plus

  • Deep knowledge and understanding of the asset management industry, its products, all asset classes spanning private and public, vehicle structures and financial markets in general

  • Proficiency with product and flow research tools such as Morningstar Direct, eVestment, Broadridge, Preqin, and Bloomberg is a plus

  • Good programming knowledge (e.g., Python, VBA) is a plus

  • Highly analytical and structured, with strong attention to detail

  • Excellent communication, presentation and writing skills, essential for distilling technical information into concise and compelling narratives

  • Proven ability to influence, lead projects, and manage cross-functional stakeholders

  • Excel at building relationships, as the role involves working across countries, cultures, teams, and business areas

  • Self-motivated and willing to take ownership, you demonstrate assertive follow-through and can work without close supervision

How to Apply:

  •  Apply for the role directly at Invesco Careers: Careers at Invesco | APAC.

To know more about us:

  • About Invesco: https://www.invesco.com/corporate/en/home.html

  • About our Culture: https://www.invesco.com/corporate/en/about-us/our-culture.html

  • About our CR program: https://www.invesco.com/corporate/en/our-commitments/corporate-responsibility.html

Full Time / Part Time

Full time

Worker Type

Employee

Job Exempt (Yes / No)

Yes

Workplace Model


At Invesco, our workplace model supports our culture and meets the needs of our clients while providing flexibility our employees value. As a full-time employee, compliance with the workplace policy means working with your direct manager to create a schedule where you will work in your designated office at least four days a week, with one day working outside an Invesco office.  

Invesco's culture of inclusivity and its commitment to diversity in the workplace are demonstrated through our people practices. We are proud to be an equal opportunity employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, creed, color, religion, sex, gender, gender identity, sexual orientation, marital status, national origin, citizenship status, disability, age, or veteran status. Our equal opportunity employment efforts comply with all applicable local laws governing non-discrimination in employment.

Personal data provided by job applicants will be used strictly in accordance with our personal data policy and for recruitment purposes only. Candidates not notified within twelve weeks may consider their application unsuccessful. All related information will be kept in our file for future recruitment purposes. When there are vacancies in our subsidiary or associate companies, we may transfer the application to them for consideration of employment. Under the Personal Data (Privacy) Ordinance, job applicants have a right to request access to, and to request correction of, Personal Data in relation to the application. If job applicants wish to exercise these rights, they should contact the Human Resources department.

jobs.title: Client Portfolio Manager - Custom Equity Platform
jobs.description:
About Invesco

As one of the world’s leading independent global investment firms, Invesco is dedicated to rethinking possibilities for our clients. By delivering the combined power of our distinctive investment management capabilities, we provide a wide range of investment strategies and vehicles to our clients around the world. If you're looking for challenging work, intelligent colleagues, and exposure across a global footprint, come explore your potential at Invesco.

What’s in it for you?

Our people are at the very core of our success. Invesco employees get more out of life through our comprehensive compensation and benefit offerings including:

  • Flexible paid time off

  • Hybrid work schedule 

  • 401(K) matching of 100% up to the first 6% with a discretionary supplemental contribution 

  • Health & wellbeing benefits 

  • Parental Leave benefits 

  • Employee stock purchase plan

Job Description

About the Department / Team:

Invesco is a well-established provider of separately managed account solutions through our Custom SMA platform.  These solutions seek to provide investors with customizable and tax-efficient exposure to equity and fixed income asset classes. The rapidly growing Custom Equity SMA platform is focused on offering highly customized long-only and long/short equity portfolios that allow, among other things, a range of tax management techniques and funding options.

About the Role:

The Client Portfolio Manager (CPM) acts as an integral part of the portfolio management team and possesses deep knowledge of capital markets and the team’s investment philosophy and quantitative investment process. The CPM is regarded as a credible investor who acts as the primary interface between our client-facing teams and the investment team. The CPM interacts with the portfolio management team daily to understand past and current portfolio positioning, performance attribution, the rationale for specific holdings and portfolio activity, risk attributes, views of the team/asset class, and has primary responsibility for conveying this information effectively to retail and institutional clients including gatekeepers, research analysts, consultants, and internal colleagues. The CPM will maintain detailed knowledge of the competitive landscape/peer group, stay current on market trends and developments in all relevant distribution channels, and be able to clearly articulate what differentiates our products from the competition. The CPM acts in partnership with rest of the CPM team, the SMA team, marketing, and other relevant partners on business and marketing related projects. The result is a multi-faceted, dynamic, and challenging role that demands highly developed skills in communications, stage presence, relationship building, business acumen, and analytics. We hire highly engaged, confident individuals who are persistently curious, will not settle for average, and continually strive to improve themselves, their work, and their environment.

Responsibilities of the Role:

  • Grow and retain business by representing the investment team and key investment strategies to clients.
  • Play a lead role in managing new business opportunities by working with the distribution teams to educate advisors, prospects, and investment platform gatekeepers on Custom Equity strategies – this includes participation in the RFP/RFI process and addressing ad hoc prospect questions.
  • Participate in advisor pitches, due diligence meetings, and stand and deliver presentations as needed.
  • Service existing clients via in-person meetings (on- and off-site), conference calls and investment committee meetings, as needed in place of Portfolio Managers.
  • Provide portfolio updates and insights to drive client engagement and loyalty.
  • Understand distribution opportunities in each channel and help shape product strategy with the Senior CPM and the product manager.
  • Participate in the development of advisor-facing content, presentations, and research.
  • This includes monitoring key investment and industry trends, working with research and product development and identifying issues and topics that leverage insights into the marketplace.
  • Manage the ongoing internal and external experience for our products and work with Marketing to support product specific content.
  • Present at Invesco roundtables and industry conferences.
  • Build strong relationships with the Distribution teams supporting their efforts to execute and effective sales strategy.
  • Support Distribution with training, large conferences, meeting support.
  • Assist Sales with custom proposals for significant business opportunities and participate in client calls/meetings as needed.
  • Liaise with the firm’s various marketing and RFP teams to ensure all data and responses regarding a given product are accurate and appropriate.
  • Assist with quarterly investment commentaries and regulatory commentary content.

Requirements of the Role:

  • Undergraduate degree required, preferably in business or related discipline.
  • 5-8 years of investment-related client-facing experience.
  • Proven success engaging and building relationships with wealth management advisors, gatekeepers, and investment due diligence teams.
  • Experience working with advisors across all channels including wirehouses, RIAs, and independent advisory firms strongly preferred.
  • Entrepreneurial spirit. The Custom Equity team is building upon a newer capability and growing quickly, so a successful candidate must have a desire for team success that allows her or him to identify, own, and solve problems.
  • Technical skills, including strong understanding of quantitative investment processes, understanding of financial markets and economics, proficiency with Bloomberg, Microsoft Office, and other software products.
  • Highly developed communication skills, both written and verbal, including the ability to simplify complex topics into understandable and compelling messages.
  • Strong and influence management skills
  • MBA/CFA a plus
  • Series 7 and 63 or 66 required
  • Regular travel required
The salary range for this position in New York City and Seattle is $150,000 - $180,000 / year. The total compensation offered for this position includes salary and incentive pay and will vary based on skills, experience and location.

Full Time / Part Time

Full time

Worker Type

Employee

Job Exempt (Yes / No)

Yes

Workplace Model

Pursuant to Invesco’s Workplace Policy, employees are expected to comply with the firm’s most current workplace model, which as of October 1, 2025, includes spending at least four full days each week working in an Invesco office. This reflects our belief that spending time together in the office helps us build stronger relationships, collaborate more easily, and support each other’s growth and development.

The above information on this description has been designed to indicate the general nature and level of work performed by employees within this role. It is not designed to contain or be interpreted as a comprehensive inventory of all duties, responsibilities and qualifications required of employees assigned to this job. The job holder may be required to perform other duties as deemed appropriate by their manager from time to time.

Invesco's culture of inclusivity and its commitment to diversity in the workplace are demonstrated through our people practices. We are proud to be an equal opportunity employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, creed, color, religion, sex, gender, gender identity, sexual orientation, marital status, national origin, citizenship status, disability, age, or veteran status. Our equal opportunity employment efforts comply with all applicable U.S. state and federal laws governing non-discrimination in employment.

jobs.title: Associate, Private Equity Advisory
jobs.description:
About Kingfish Group
Kingfish is a different kind of private equity firm. We have pioneered a new approach to private equity investing that places us at the intersection of people, ideas and capital. At the core of our efforts are deep, systematic relationships with proven operating executives. Our firm comprises two core business segments; our private equity investment business and an advisory business that supports a limited set of high-performing PE sponsors as they identify, invest in and grow businesses. Our philosophy is to find the best people and give them the freedom to learn, innovate and outperform. In that spirit, we are structured to ensure that each individual contributes substantially to firm strategy and goals, and there is extensive feedback on performance. We pride ourselves on assembling high-performing teams to pursue inspiring goals.
Position Summary
Our Associates work closely with our private equity firm partners, operating as an extension of their deal teams to evaluate investment opportunities and advance portfolio company initiatives. Specifically, Associates are responsible for (a) Building relationships with industry executives and aligning them to support the team throughout the investment process (b) Synthesizing information and translating it into key insights that support investment decision making, (c) Managing projects to deliver high impact insights and relationships that provide an advantage to the team (d) Leading company-wide initiatives that improve how we do our work and make us a stronger team.
Responsibilities
● Build relationships with industry executives and align them to support the team throughout the investment process
  ○ Guide our research team as they identify industry executives and other primary information relevant for a particular investment opportunity
  ○ Lead calls with industry executives, serving as the primary point of contact and ambassador for the firm
●  Synthesize information and translate into key insights that support investment decision making
  ○  Analyze industries, markets and competitive landscapes to better understand investment opportunities
  ○  Perform primary diligence as needed alongside executive advisors throughout the deal process
  ○  Identify key insights from information and recommendations gathered from conversations with industry executives and research
●   Manage projects to deliver high impact insights and relationships, providing an advantage to the team
  ○  Manage project plans and deal timelines, working closely with senior leaders at our private equity partners to ensure our work is focused on the highest priorities and maximum impact
  ○  Create and deliver high quality presentations and memoranda for our Partners as well as Kingfish leadership (e.g. primary diligence deliverables, executive spec documentation, and Kingfish Investment Committee discussions)
●   Lead company-wide initiatives that improve how we do our work and make us a stronger team.
Qualifications
● Bachelor’s degree with 2-3 years of experience within consulting or financial services
● Relationship Building: High EQ with advanced written/ verbal communication skills, executive presence and an ability to build relationships with stakeholders at all levels
● Intellectual Curiosity & Creativity: thoughtful in asking questions and pursuing answers, excited to learn
● Critical Thinking: breaks down complex problems and solves them in creative ways
● Communication & Information Synthesis: effectively identifies key insights and presents insights
● Project Management: ability to manage multiple concurrent projects with tight timelines
● Proactive & Collaborative: team player that takes initiative to improve the team and the firm
